title: Admin Panel Overview
summary: Complete overview of the Admin panel: users, track types, settings, integrations, compliance, training, EDI, events, and more.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Admin Panel Overview

The Admin panel is the central configuration hub for Forge. Only users with the Admin role (or Manager for some sub-sections) can access it. Navigate to **Admin** in the sidebar to see all available tabs.

### Users Tab

Manage all user accounts. Create new users (the system generates a setup token — admins never set passwords), assign roles, configure kiosk scan identifiers (RFID/NFC/barcode), set avatar colors, and deactivate departing employees. Each user row shows their avatar, name (Last, First format), email, role chip, compliance completion status, and active/inactive indicator.

### Track Types Tab

Track types define the different kanban boards in the system (Production, R&D/Tooling, Maintenance, etc.). Each track type has an ordered list of stages with names, codes, colors, WIP limits, and irreversibility flags. You can create custom track types for specialized workflows.

### Settings Tab

Configure company profile (name, phone, email, EIN, website), manage company locations (multiple physical sites with one default), upload your logo, set brand colors, configure pay period locking, and adjust system settings like planning cycle duration and auto-archive timing.

### Integrations Tab

Connect external services: QuickBooks Online (OAuth), shipping carriers (UPS, FedEx, USPS, DHL), SMTP email, and MinIO storage. Each integration card shows its status (Active, Not Configured, Standalone) with Test, Connect, Disconnect, and Configure buttons.

### Terminology Tab

Customize application labels. If your company calls 'Jobs' something else (like 'Work Orders'), change the label here. Changes apply immediately across the entire app for all users.

### Reference Data Tab

Manage all lookup values used in dropdowns throughout the app: expense categories, lead sources, asset types, priorities, and more. Each group is expandable with individual items that have a code (immutable) and label (editable).

### Compliance Templates Tab

Manage the compliance form templates that employees fill out (W-4, I-9, state withholding, direct deposit, handbook). Upload PDFs for AI-powered field extraction. Monitor employee completion status and approve submissions.

### Training Tab

Manage the learning management system. Three sub-tabs: Content (create/edit training modules with Article, Walkthrough, QuickRef, or Quiz types), Paths (organize modules into ordered learning sequences), and Progress (monitor per-user completion).

### Teams & Kiosks Tab

Organize users into teams with colors and descriptions. Manage kiosk terminals for shop floor displays. Assign team members via checkbox selection.

### Events Tab

Create and manage company events: meetings, training sessions, safety briefings. Set event type, date/time range, location, attendees, and required/optional status. Events appear on the shop floor display.

### Audit Log Tab

View the complete system audit trail. Every create, update, and delete across all entities is logged with timestamp, user, action, entity type, entity ID, field-level details, and IP address. Filter by entity type, action, user, or date range.

### EDI Tab

Manage Electronic Data Interchange: trading partners (X12/EDIFACT), transaction processing, and field mappings. Two sub-tabs: Trading Partners (connection config) and Transactions (processing history with retry).

### MFA Policy Tab

Configure which roles require multi-factor authentication. View MFA compliance status across all users showing who has MFA enabled and what device type they're using.

### Sales Tax Tab

Manage sales tax rates by state/jurisdiction with effective dates. Configure default rates and per-customer overrides.

### Time Corrections Tab

Review and correct employee time entries. Filter by user and date range. View original entry details and create corrections with required reasons that create an audit trail.", "sections": []}

This ensures consistency as codes are used internally as immutable identifiers."}, {"id": "ad14", "text": "What happens when you lock a pay period through a specific date?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Employees cannot submit expenses after that date"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Time entries on or before that date can no longer be modified by employees or managers", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The payroll report for that period is automatically generated"}, {"id": "d", "text": "All jobs due before that date are automatically archived"}], "explanation": "Pay period locking prevents modifications to time entries within the locked period. This ensures payroll data integrity once it has been processed."}, {"id": "ad15", "text": "You created a new training module but employees can't see it. What is the most likely issue?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The module hasn't been added to a training path yet"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The module's Published toggle is set to OFF — employees only see published modules", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The module's content type is not supported by the training viewer"}, {"id": "d", "text": "New modules require a system restart to appear"}], "explanation": "Training modules have a Published toggle. When OFF (Draft), the module is invisible to regular users. Toggle it ON in Admin → Training → Content tab to make it visible."}], "passingScore": 75, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 10, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Admin: Managing Users and Roles
summary: For admins and managers: how to create users, assign roles, configure kiosk access, and manage teams.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Admin: Managing Users and Roles

User management in Forge is handled through the Admin panel. Only users with the Admin or Manager role can access this area.

### Creating a New User

Navigate to **Admin → Users** and click **New User**. Fill in the user's name, email address, and role. Do NOT set a password — the system generates a secure setup token and emails it to the new user. The employee follows the link to set their own password and complete their account.

### Roles

Forge uses additive roles:

- **Engineer** — kanban board, assigned work, time tracking, expenses, files
- **Production Worker** — simplified task list, start/stop timer, move cards, notes
- **PM** — backlog, planning, leads, reporting (read-only board)
- **Manager** — everything PM + assign work, approve expenses, set priorities
- **Office Manager** — customer/vendor, invoice queue, employee documents
- **Admin** — everything + user management, roles, system settings, track types

### Kiosk Auth (Badge + PIN)

For shop floor workers who don't use a computer, enable Tier 2 kiosk authentication. In the user's profile, assign an employee barcode (printed on their badge). The worker scans their badge at a kiosk terminal and enters a 4–6 digit PIN to clock in, start timers, and move jobs.

### Teams

Organize users into teams under **Admin → Teams**. Teams are used for filtered board views (swimlanes by team), workload reporting, and shift scheduling.

### Deactivating Users

When an employee leaves, deactivate their account from Admin → Users. Deactivated users cannot log in but all their historical data (jobs, time entries, expenses) is preserved.", "sections": []}

title: System Settings and Branding
summary: How to configure application settings, company profile, brand colors, logo, and company locations.
contentJson:
{"body": "## System Settings and Branding

### Company Profile

Navigate to **Admin → Settings → Company Profile**. Enter your company's legal name, phone number, email, EIN, and website. This information appears on invoices, packing slips, and other customer-facing documents.

### Company Locations

Under **Admin → Settings → Locations**, add all your physical locations (main office, warehouse, remote sites). Each location has a name, address, and state. Mark one as the Default — this is the default work location for employees who haven't specified one, and it drives state withholding form requirements.

### Brand Colors and Logo

In **Admin → Settings**, scroll to the Brand section. Enter hex color codes for your primary and accent brand colors. These update the entire app's color theme in real time — all users see the change immediately.

To upload your company logo, click the Logo section and drag your image file (PNG or SVG recommended). The logo appears in the header bar and on printed documents.

### Application Settings

The Settings panel also has configuration for:
- **Application name** — shown in the browser tab and header
- **Planning cycle duration** — default length for new planning cycles
- **Auto-archive days** — how long after completion before jobs auto-archive
- **Max upload size** — maximum file attachment size
- **Email notifications** — enable/disable system emails", "sections": []}

title: Compliance Templates Administration
summary: How to manage compliance form templates: uploading PDFs, configuring fields, and monitoring employee completion.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Compliance Templates Administration

### What Are Compliance Templates?

Compliance templates are the master definitions of each form employees must complete (W-4, I-9, state withholding, employee handbook, etc.). Admins manage the templates; employees fill them out from their Account page.

### Creating a Template

Navigate to **Admin → Compliance** and click **New Template**. Give it a title (e.g., 'Federal W-4 2024'), a form type (Tax Withholding, Identity Verification, Acknowledgment), and an effective date. Then configure which form fields employees will fill out.

### Uploading a PDF Form

For government forms like the W-4, upload the official PDF. The system uses AI-powered PDF extraction to automatically identify form fields and generate a renderable form definition. Review the extracted fields and adjust any that weren't parsed correctly.

### Field Configuration

Each form field has a type (text, number, dropdown, signature, checkbox), validation rules, and optional help text. Some fields (name, SSN, address) are auto-populated from the employee's profile.

### Monitoring Completion

Navigate to **Admin → Compliance → User Status** to see completion status per employee. The table shows which forms each employee has completed, which are pending, and which are overdue. Click any employee row to see their individual form submissions.

### Approval Workflow

When an employee submits a form, it appears in the approval queue. Review the submission, and either Approve (locks the form) or Request Changes (sends it back with your notes). Approved forms generate a PDF record stored in the employee's documents.", "sections": []}

title: Integration Configuration
summary: How to connect QuickBooks Online, configure SMTP email, set up MinIO storage, and manage integration settings.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Integration Configuration

Navigate to **Admin → Integrations** to configure external service connections.

### QuickBooks Online

Forge syncs with QuickBooks Online for accounting. To connect:
1. Click **Connect QuickBooks** — you'll be redirected to the Intuit OAuth consent screen.
2. Sign in to your QBO account and authorize Forge.
3. You're returned to the app with the connection active.

Once connected, the following sync automatically:
- Customers (bidirectional)
- Items/Products (from QB)
- Invoices (Forge → QB)
- Payments (Forge → QB)
- Vendor Bills from POs (Forge → QB)
- Time Activities (Forge → QB for payroll)

Note: When QuickBooks is connected, some features (invoicing, payments, AR aging) are read-only in Forge — manage them in QBO directly.

### SMTP Email

For system emails (setup tokens, notifications, invoice delivery), configure your SMTP server under **Integrations → Email**. Enter the SMTP host, port, username, and password. Use the **Test Connection** button to send a test email before saving.

### Storage (MinIO)

File attachments are stored in MinIO (S3-compatible). In a Docker Compose deployment, this is already configured. If you're using an external storage provider (AWS S3, Cloudflare R2), enter the endpoint, access key, and secret key here.

### Mock Mode

During development or testing, enable **Mock Integrations** to bypass all external APIs. All services return simulated responses. This is controlled by the `MOCK_INTEGRATIONS` environment variable in docker-compose.yml.", "sections": []}

title: Reference Data and Terminology
summary: How to manage lookup values (expense categories, lead sources, priorities) and customize application labels.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Reference Data and Terminology

### Reference Data

Navigate to **Admin → Reference Data** to manage all lookup values used throughout the application. Reference data groups include:

- **Expense Categories** — the dropdown in the expense form
- **Lead Sources** — where leads came from (Trade Show, Referral, Website, etc.)
- **Asset Types** — categories for the asset register
- **Job Priorities** — the priority options on job cards

Each group is expandable. Click a group to see its current values. You can add new values, edit labels, and reorder entries. The `code` field is immutable once set — it's used internally. Only the `label` can be changed.

### Adding New Values

Click **Add Item** within a group. Enter the label (what users see) and the code (internal identifier, lowercase-snake-case). Click Save. The new option immediately appears in the relevant dropdowns.

### Terminology

Navigate to **Admin → Terminology** to customize application labels. If your company calls jobs 'Work Orders' instead of 'Jobs', enter 'Work Orders' next to the `entity_job` key. Click **Save Terminology**.

Terminology changes apply across the entire app — all users see the updated labels immediately. The change is cached in each browser for fast rendering.

### Resetting Labels

Delete the custom text for any terminology key to revert to the system default. The default is derived from the key name (e.g., `entity_job` → 'Job').", "sections": []}

title: Chat Overview
summary: An overview of the built-in chat system including 1:1 direct messages, group rooms, real-time delivery via SignalR, and file/entity sharing.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Chat Overview

Forge includes a built-in chat system for real-time internal communication. You can reach it from the sidebar or by clicking the chat icon in the header bar.

### Accessing Chat

Chat opens as a slide-out panel from the right side of the screen. You can toggle it open or closed at any time without losing your place on the current page. The header chat icon also shows an unread message count badge so you always know when new messages arrive.

### 1:1 Direct Messages

The primary chat mode is one-on-one direct messaging. Click the **New Message** button (+ icon) to open the user picker, search for a colleague by name, and start a conversation. Messages are delivered in real time via SignalR — no page refresh needed.

### Group Rooms

Beyond 1:1 conversations, chat supports group rooms. Group rooms allow multiple team members to collaborate in a shared thread. Room members see all messages posted to the room in real time.

### Real-Time Delivery (SignalR)

All chat messages are delivered instantly through the ChatHub SignalR connection. When you send a message, the recipient sees it appear immediately without any polling or refresh. The connection is established automatically when you log in and reconnects with exponential backoff if interrupted.

### File and Entity Sharing

You can share files and entity references (jobs, parts, purchase orders, etc.) directly within chat messages. Shared entities render as clickable links that open the relevant detail dialog.

### Conversations List

The default chat view shows all your conversations sorted by most recent activity. Each conversation row displays:
- **Avatar** with the other user's initials and color
- **User name** in Last, First MI format
- **Last message preview** (truncated to fit)
- **Timestamp** — relative format (just now, 5m, 2h, 3d) or full date for older messages
- **Unread badge** showing the count of unread messages

### Message Thread

Opening a conversation shows the full message thread with:
- **Sender avatar** with initials and background color
- **Message text** with preserved line breaks
- **Timestamp** in HH:MM format
- **Date separator lines** — Today, Yesterday, or the full date for older messages
- **Visual distinction** for your own messages vs. received messages

### Read Receipts

When you open a conversation, all messages in that thread are automatically marked as read via `POST /api/v1/chat/messages/{otherUserId}/read`. The unread badge on the conversations list updates accordingly.", "sections": []}

title: Training Module Administration
summary: How to create training modules, manage learning paths, generate AI walkthroughs, and monitor employee progress.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Training Module Administration

Navigate to **Admin → Training** to manage the entire learning management system.

### Content Tab

The Content tab lists all training modules. Each module has a type (Article, Video, Walkthrough, QuickRef, Quiz), published status, and estimated time. Use the search box to find modules.

### Creating a Module

Click **New Module** to open the editor. Fill in the title, summary, content type, and estimated time. Set the content:
- **Article/QuickRef** — write the content in the JSON content editor or use the rich text interface
- **Walkthrough** — define the driver.js tour steps, or use **AI Generate** to automatically create steps from the live page
- **Quiz** — add questions with 4 options each, mark the correct answer, and optionally add explanations

### Publishing

Modules are Draft until you toggle **Published**. Only published modules are visible to employees.

### AI Walkthrough Generation

For Walkthrough modules, click the sparkle icon to trigger AI generation. The system opens the target page in a headless browser, analyzes the DOM, and sends it to the local AI model to generate relevant tour steps. Review the suggested steps and edit as needed before saving.

### Paths Tab

Learning Paths group modules into ordered sequences. Create a path, add modules to it in order, and mark required vs. optional. Set allowed roles and whether the path is auto-assigned to new users.

### Progress Tab

The Progress tab shows completion percentages for all users. Click the detail icon on any user row to open their per-module breakdown in a side panel.", "sections": []}

title: AI Assistant Overview
summary: What the AI Assistant feature does, how conversations work, and how to get the most out of domain-specific assistants.
contentJson:
{"body": "## AI Assistant Overview

The AI Assistant feature gives you access to domain-specific AI assistants that can answer questions, draft content, and help with day-to-day tasks. Each assistant is trained for a specific area — HR, Procurement, Sales, or general engineering — so the answers you get are relevant to your work.

### How It Works

The AI page uses a two-panel layout:
- **Sidebar (left):** A list of assistant cards showing each assistant's icon, name, description, and an active indicator for the one you're currently chatting with.
- **Chat area (right):** Where the conversation happens. When you first select an assistant, you'll see a welcome screen with the assistant's icon, name, description, and a grid of starter questions to help you get going.

### Starting a Conversation

Click any assistant card in the sidebar to select it. If you haven't chatted with that assistant yet, you'll see the welcome screen with starter questions. Click a starter question or type your own in the message input at the bottom.

### Message Input

- Type your question in the text field (placeholder shows 'Ask {assistantName}')
- Press **Enter** to send, **Shift+Enter** to add a new line
- The **Send** button (send icon) is disabled when the input is empty or a response is being generated
- The **Clear Chat** button (delete_sweep icon) clears the current conversation and returns to the welcome screen — it's disabled when there are no messages

### How Context Works

The assistant sees the last 10 messages of your conversation as context, so it can follow up on previous questions. Conversations are maintained per-assistant in memory — switching to a different assistant and back will keep your chat history for the current session. However, conversations are not persisted between sessions (closing the browser clears them).

### Assistant Responses

Assistant messages appear with an avatar and icon. While the assistant is thinking, you'll see a typing indicator (three animated dots). Responses may include formatted text, lists, and code snippets depending on the question.

### When to Use the AI Assistant

- When you need a quick answer about a process or policy
- When drafting job descriptions, emails, or documentation
- When you want to search across uploaded documents (RAG-powered)
- When exploring what-if scenarios for procurement or sales
- When you need help interpreting QC data or production metrics", "sections": []}

Does not affect other assistants' conversations."}, {"label": "Starter Question (card click)", "value": "Sends the selected starter question as your first message, beginning the conversation."}, {"label": "Assistant Card (sidebar click)", "value": "Switches to the selected assistant. Your previous conversation with each assistant is preserved in memory for the session."}], "heading": "Buttons and Actions"}, {"items": [{"label": "Text Input", "value": "Multi-line text field. Placeholder: 'Ask {assistantName}' where {assistantName} is the current assistant's name."}, {"label": "Enter", "value": "Sends the message (equivalent to clicking Send)."}, {"label": "Shift+Enter", "value": "Inserts a new line without sending. Use for multi-line questions."}, {"label": "Empty Input", "value": "Send button is disabled. You cannot send an empty message."}, {"label": "While Sending", "value": "Send button is disabled and shows a loading state until the assistant responds."}], "heading": "Input Behavior"}, {"items": [{"label": "Assistant List", "value": "Loaded from GET /api/v1/ai/assistants. Returns AiAssistantListItem objects with id, name, description, icon, color, category, starterQuestions, and isActive flag."}, {"label": "Chat Request", "value": "POST /api/v1/ai/assistants/{id}/chat with body {text, history[]}. History includes the last 10 messages for context."}, {"label": "Conversation Storage", "value": "In-memory only (not persisted). Each assistant has its own conversation. Closing the browser or refreshing the page clears all conversations."}, {"label": "Route", "value": "/ai/{assistantId} — the selected assistant ID is in the URL, making it shareable/bookmarkable."}], "heading": "Data and API"}]}

title: AI Assistant Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the AI Assistant feature: navigation, conversations, context, and assistant management.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "ai1", "text": "You want to ask the HR Assistant about the company's PTO policy. How do you start?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Admin → AI Settings and type your question in the configuration panel"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Navigate to the AI page, click the HR Assistant card in the sidebar, then type your question or click a starter question", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Open the Chat feature and create a new room called 'HR Questions'"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Go to Training → search for PTO policy in the module list"}], "explanation": "Navigate to the AI page (/ai), select the HR Assistant from the sidebar, and either type your question in the input or click one of the starter questions on the welcome screen."}, {"id": "ai2", "text": "You're chatting with the Sales Advisor and want to start a new line in your message without sending it. What key combination do you use?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Ctrl+Enter"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Alt+Enter"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Shift+Enter", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Tab then Enter"}], "explanation": "Shift+Enter inserts a new line in the message input. Pressing Enter alone sends the message."}, {"id": "ai3", "text": "How many of your previous messages does the AI assistant use as context when generating a response?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "All messages from the entire conversation"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The last 10 messages", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Only your most recent message — no history"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The last 50 messages or 4000 tokens, whichever is smaller"}], "explanation": "The API sends the last 10 messages as the history array in the chat request. This gives the assistant enough context to follow up on recent topics without sending excessive data."}, {"id": "ai4", "text": "You switch from the Procurement Assistant to the HR Assistant and then back to Procurement. What happens to your Procurement conversation?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "It's gone — switching assistants clears all conversations"}, {"id": "b", "text": "It's preserved in memory for the current session", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "It's saved to the database and can be resumed anytime"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Only the last 3 messages are kept; older ones are discarded"}], "explanation": "Conversations are maintained per-assistant in memory. Switching assistants doesn't clear anything — each assistant keeps its own conversation. However, conversations are not persisted, so closing the browser clears them."}, {"id": "ai5", "text": "The Clear Chat button (delete_sweep icon) is grayed out. Why?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "You don't have permission to clear chats"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The assistant is currently generating a response"}, {"id": "c", "text": "There are no messages in the current conversation — nothing to clear", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Clear Chat is only available to Admin and Manager roles"}], "explanation": "The Clear Chat button is disabled when there are no messages. Once you send at least one message, the button becomes active."}, {"id": "ai6", "text": "What do the three animated dots in the chat area indicate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The assistant is offline and cannot respond"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Your message failed to send and is being retried"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The assistant is generating a response — a typing indicator", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "The system is loading the assistant's configuration"}], "explanation": "The typing indicator (three animated dots) appears while the assistant is processing your question and generating a response. The Send button is also disabled during this time."}, {"id": "ai7", "text": "You refresh the browser while chatting with an assistant. What happens to your conversation?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "It's fully restored from the server"}, {"id": "b", "text": "It's restored from localStorage"}, {"id": "c", "text": "It's cleared — conversations are in-memory only and not persisted", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Only the last message is preserved via the URL query string"}], "explanation": "Conversations are stored in memory only. Refreshing the page, closing the tab, or navigating away clears all conversations. This is by design — the AI feature is for quick interactions, not long-term record-keeping."}, {"id": "ai8", "text": "Where do starter questions come from for each assistant?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "They're hardcoded in the frontend based on the assistant category"}, {"id": "b", "text": "They're configured by an admin in the AI Assistants admin panel and loaded from the API", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The AI generates them dynamically based on your role and recent activity"}, {"id": "d", "text": "They're pulled from the Training module's quiz questions"}], "explanation": "Starter questions are part of each assistant's configuration (the starterQuestions field on AiAssistantListItem). Admins configure them in Admin → AI Assistants. They're loaded from GET /api/v1/ai/assistants."}, {"id": "ai9", "text": "You want to share a link to the Procurement Assistant with a coworker. Is this possible?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "No — the AI page doesn't support direct linking to specific assistants"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Yes — the assistant ID is in the URL (/ai/{assistantId}), so you can copy and share the link", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Yes, but only if you export the conversation first"}, {"id": "d", "text": "No — they need to navigate to the AI page and select the assistant manually"}], "explanation": "The route is /ai/{assistantId}, so the selected assistant is reflected in the URL. Your coworker can use the link to go directly to that assistant's page. However, they won't see your conversation — only the welcome screen."}, {"id": "ai10", "text": "The Send button is disabled even though you've typed a message. What's the most likely reason?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The assistant is currently generating a response to your previous message", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Your message exceeds the maximum character limit"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The assistant has been deactivated by an admin"}, {"id": "d", "text": "You need to select an assistant first before sending"}], "explanation": "The Send button is disabled in two cases: when the input is empty, or when the assistant is currently generating a response (sending state). Since you've typed a message, the most likely reason is that a response is still being generated."}, {"id": "ai11", "text": "What information does each assistant card in the sidebar display?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Name only — you have to click to see details"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Icon, name, description, and an active indicator for the selected assistant", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Name, last message preview, and timestamp of last conversation"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Name, category, number of conversations, and a star rating"}], "explanation": "Each sidebar card shows the assistant's icon (Material icon), name, description, and an active indicator highlighting which assistant is currently selected."}, {"id": "ai12", "text": "An admin wants to add a new AI assistant for the Quality department. Where do they configure it?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "In the AI page itself — click 'New Assistant' in the sidebar"}, {"id": "b", "text": "In Admin → AI Assistants — create a new assistant with name, description, icon, color, category, and starter questions", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "By editing the assistant configuration JSON file and restarting the server"}, {"id": "d", "text": "In Admin → System Settings under the AI section"}], "explanation": "AI assistants are managed in the Admin → AI Assistants panel. Admins can create, edit, and deactivate assistants there. The assistant list is loaded from the API (GET /api/v1/ai/assistants), so changes are reflected immediately."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Assets Overview
summary: What the Assets module does, when to use it, and how it connects to jobs, parts, and maintenance.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Assets Overview

The Assets module is your equipment and tooling register. It tracks every physical asset your shop owns or manages — machines, tooling, vehicles, and facilities — along with their maintenance history, current status, and operating hours.

### What Counts as an Asset?

Assets are categorized by type:
- **Machine** — CNC mills, lathes, saws, presses, and other production equipment
- **Tooling** — molds, dies, jigs, fixtures, and cutting tools (includes customer-owned tooling)
- **Facility** — buildings, HVAC systems, electrical panels, and infrastructure
- **Vehicle** — delivery trucks, forklifts, company vehicles
- **Other** — anything that doesn't fit the above categories

### How Assets Connect to Other Features

**Tooling and Parts:** A tooling asset can be linked to a specific part (the part it produces) and to the job that created it. This gives you full traceability: which mold makes which part, and which job built the mold.

**Maintenance:** Each asset tracks its maintenance history — scheduled maintenance, repairs, and inspections. When an asset is set to Maintenance status, it signals to the team that the machine is down.

**Barcode/QR Labels:** Every asset can have a barcode label printed for quick identification on the shop floor. Scan the label to pull up the asset record instantly.

### When to Use Assets

- When onboarding a new piece of equipment
- When tracking customer-owned tooling (molds they send you)
- When scheduling or recording maintenance
- When an asset goes out of service and you need to track why
- When you need to know the shot count or tool life remaining on a mold", "sections": []}

You'll enter the name, type, location, manufacturer, and model. For tooling assets, additional fields appear: cavity count, tool life expectancy, and customer ownership."}}, {"element": ".page-header app-input", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Search Assets", "description": "Search by asset name, manufacturer, model, or serial number. Results update in real time as you type."}}, {"element": ".page-header app-select:first-of-type", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Type Filter", "description": "Filter assets by type: Machine, Tooling, Facility, Vehicle, or Other. Each type has a distinct icon for quick identification."}}, {"element": ".page-header app-select:last-of-type", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Status Filter", "description": "Filter by status: Active (in service), Maintenance (currently down), Retired (no longer used), or Out of Service (broken/unavailable)."}}, {"element": "app-data-table", "popover": {"side": "top", "title": "Assets Table", "description": "Click any row to open the asset detail panel. Columns include name, type, location, manufacturer, status (color-coded chip), and current operating hours. Click column headers to sort."}}], "appRoute": "/assets", "startButtonLabel": "Tour Assets"}

contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "as1", "text": "You've received a new CNC mill from Haas. What is the first step to register it in Forge?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Admin → Equipment and register the serial number"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Navigate to Assets, click Add Asset, fill in the name, type (Machine), manufacturer (Haas), and location", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Create a Job on the Kanban Board and tag it as equipment setup"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Add it to Inventory as a new storage location"}], "explanation": "New equipment is registered in the Assets module. Click Add Asset, select type Machine, and fill in the details. The asset starts in Active status by default."}, {"id": "as2", "text": "A customer sends you a mold they own for production. How do you track this in Forge?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Create an Asset with type Tooling and toggle Customer Owned to ON", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Add it as a Part in the Parts Catalog with status Draft"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Create a Purchase Order to record its receipt"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Add it to Inventory as received stock"}], "explanation": "Customer-owned tooling is tracked as a Tooling asset with the Customer Owned toggle enabled. This distinguishes it in reports and ensures proper handling."}, {"id": "as3", "text": "An asset shows a status of 'Out of Service' (red chip). What does this mean?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The asset is scheduled for routine maintenance next week"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The asset has been permanently retired and will be sold"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The asset is broken or otherwise unavailable for use — an unplanned outage", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "The asset's operating hours have exceeded its service life"}], "explanation": "Out of Service (red) indicates an unplanned outage — the asset is broken or unavailable. This is different from Maintenance (yellow, which is planned downtime) and Retired (gray, permanently removed)."}, {"id": "as4", "text": "You want to see all tooling assets that are currently down for maintenance. How?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Assets, set the Type filter to Tooling and the Status filter to Maintenance", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Run a report in the Reports module filtered by asset status"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Check the Dashboard maintenance widget"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Go to Admin → Equipment → filter by status"}], "explanation": "The Assets page has two filters: Type and Status. Set Type to Tooling and Status to Maintenance to see exactly the assets you need."}, {"id": "as5", "text": "What additional fields appear when you set an asset's type to Tooling?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Warranty Date, Insurance Policy, and Depreciation Rate"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Customer Owned toggle, Cavity Count, and Tool Life Expectancy", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Operator Assigned, Shift Schedule, and Calibration Date"}, {"id": "d", "text": "No additional fields — all asset types have the same form"}], "explanation": "Tooling assets get three extra fields: Customer Owned (toggle), Cavity Count (number of cavities in a mold), and Tool Life Expectancy (expected total shots before replacement)."}, {"id": "as6", "text": "A tooling asset's Current Shot Count is approaching its Tool Life Expectancy. What does this indicate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The tool needs to be recalibrated before the next production run"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The tool is nearing end of life and may need replacement or refurbishment soon", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The tool has exceeded its warranty period"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Nothing — shot count is informational only and has no significance"}], "explanation": "When Current Shot Count approaches Tool Life Expectancy, it's a signal that the tool may need replacement or refurbishment. This helps you plan ahead rather than having a tool fail unexpectedly during production."}, {"id": "as7", "text": "You want to quickly change a machine's status from Active to Maintenance without opening the edit dialog. How?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Click the asset row, then click the Maintenance status button in the detail panel", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Right-click the row and select Set Status → Maintenance"}, {"id": "c", "text": "You must open the edit dialog to change status"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Drag the asset row to the Maintenance section of the table"}], "explanation": "The asset detail panel has quick-action status buttons. Click the row to open the detail, then click the Maintenance button to change status immediately."}, {"id": "as8", "text": "What happens to an asset's data when you set its status to Retired?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The record is permanently deleted to free up space"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The record is archived and hidden from all views"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The record is preserved with full history — it just won't appear in Active/Maintenance filtered views", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Only the name is kept — all maintenance history and details are purged"}], "explanation": "Retired assets are never deleted. title: Backlog Overview
summary: What the backlog is, how it relates to the kanban board, filtering jobs, view modes, and creating new jobs.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Backlog Overview

The Backlog is your master list of all jobs across every track type. While the Kanban Board gives you a visual, stage-by-stage view of a single track, the Backlog lets you see every job in the system at once — search, filter, sort, and triage work before it hits the board.

### Backlog vs. Kanban Board

The Kanban Board shows jobs organized into columns by stage within a single track type (e.g., Production, R&D/Tooling, Maintenance). You interact with jobs visually — dragging cards between stages, seeing WIP limits, and tracking flow.

The Backlog is a flat list of all jobs regardless of track type. It is the place to:
- **Find any job** by number, title, customer, or assignee
- **Triage incoming work** — review unassigned or low-priority jobs
- **Bulk review** — scan across tracks to spot overdue items, unbalanced workloads, or stalled jobs
- **Create new jobs** that land on the board in their track's first stage

Every job in the Backlog also appears on its corresponding Kanban Board. Changes made in either place are reflected in both.

### Two View Modes

The Backlog offers two ways to look at your jobs:

**Table View (default)** — A data table with sortable, filterable columns: Job Number, Title, Stage, Priority, Assignee, Customer, and Due Date. Click any column header to sort. Use the column filter icons for per-column filtering (text search, enum selection, or date range). Click any row to open the full job detail dialog.

**Card View** — A grid of job cards showing the job number, title, stage chip (color-coded), priority dot, assignee avatar, customer name, and due date. Cards with active holds show an 'On Hold' badge. Overdue cards are visually highlighted. Click any card to open the job detail dialog.

Toggle between views using the view icons in the page header (view_list for table, view_module for cards). Your preference is saved and restored on your next visit.

### Filtering

Four filters in the page header narrow down the list:

- **Search** — free-text search across job number and title. Results update as you type.
- **Track Type** — filter to a specific track (Production, R&D/Tooling, etc.). Shows only jobs whose current stage belongs to that track.
- **Priority** — filter by Low, Normal, High, or Urgent.
- **Assignee** — filter to jobs assigned to a specific team member.

All filters work together (AND logic). The filtered count is reflected in both table and card views.

### Creating Jobs

Click the 'Create Job' button (with the + icon) in the page header to open the job creation dialog. You will enter:

- **Title** (required, max 200 characters)
- **Description** (optional, multi-line)
- **Track Type** (required) — determines which board and initial stage the job enters
- **Customer** (optional) — links the job to a customer record
- **Assignee** (optional) — assigns a team member. If the selected user has an incomplete profile, a warning is shown.
- **Priority** (defaults to Normal) — Low, Normal, High, or Urgent
- **Due Date** (optional) — displayed in red when overdue

The new job is placed in the first stage of the selected track type and appears on both the Backlog and the Kanban Board immediately.

### Job Detail

Clicking any row (table view) or card (card view) opens the full job detail dialog via the DetailDialogService. The URL updates to include `?detail=job:{id}`, making the link shareable and bookmarkable. From the detail dialog you can edit the job, view subtasks, files, activity log, and more.

### Row Styling (Table View)

- **Overdue rows** get the `row--overdue` class — a subtle red tint indicating the due date has passed.
- **Selected row** gets the `row--selected` class — highlighted to show which job's detail is currently open.
- **Stage color tint** — each row has a faint background tint matching its stage color via the `--row-tint` CSS custom property.

### Priority Colors

Priority is shown as a colored dot next to the priority name:
- **Low** — slate gray (#94a3b8)
- **Normal** — teal (#0d9488)
- **High** — amber (#f59e0b)
- **Urgent** — red (#dc2626)", "sections": []}

The Table View toggle uses view_list. Both are in the page header's view-toggle group. The active button gets the icon-btn--active class."}, {"id": "bl11", "text": "In card view, what triggers the 'On Hold' badge to appear on a job card?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The job's priority is set to Urgent"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The job has been in the same stage for more than 7 days"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The job has one or more active holds (job.activeHolds.length > 0)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "The job's assignee has not logged in recently"}], "explanation": "The 'On Hold' badge (a warning-colored chip) appears in the card header when job.activeHolds.length > 0. Holds are managed through the job detail dialog's status lifecycle features and indicate the job is blocked or paused."}, {"id": "bl12", "text": "What color represents a 'High' priority job's dot in the backlog?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Red (#dc2626)"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Teal (#0d9488)"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Amber (#f59e0b)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Slate gray (#94a3b8)"}], "explanation": "Priority colors are: Low = #94a3b8 (slate gray), Normal = #0d9488 (teal), High = #f59e0b (amber), Urgent = #dc2626 (red). These colors are defined in the PRIORITY_COLORS constant and used for both the table dot and the card view dot."}, {"id": "bl13", "text": "What happens when you hover over a disabled Save button in the job creation dialog?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Nothing — the button is grayed out with no additional feedback"}, {"id": "b", "text": "A tooltip says 'Please fill in all required fields'"}, {"id": "c", "text": "A validation popover appears listing which fields have errors (e.g., 'Title is required')", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "The invalid fields flash red to draw attention"}], "explanation": "The Save button uses the ValidationPopoverDirective ([appValidationPopover]). When the form is invalid and you hover over the disabled button, a popover appears with a bulleted list of validation violations, such as 'Title is required' or 'Track Type is required'. This is the standard Forge form validation pattern — no inline mat-error messages."}, {"id": "bl14", "text": "How does the Track Type filter work in the Backlog?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "It hides all jobs except those whose job.trackTypeId matches the selected track"}, {"id": "b", "text": "It filters jobs by matching the job's current stage name against the selected track type's stage list", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "It groups the table rows by track type, collapsing non-selected tracks"}, {"id": "d", "text": "It navigates you to the Kanban Board for the selected track"}], "explanation": "The Track Type filter looks up the selected track's stages, builds a Set of stage names, and filters jobs to only those whose current stageName is in that set. This means if a job is in a stage that belongs to the selected track, it appears — the filter works through stage membership, not a direct trackTypeId on the job."}, {"id": "bl15", "text": "What data-testid would you use to locate the due date picker in the job creation dialog?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "data-testid=\"job-dueDate\""}, {"id": "b", "text": "data-testid=\"job-due-date\"", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "data-testid=\"backlog-due-date\""}, {"id": "d", "text": "data-testid=\"create-due-date\""}], "explanation": "The due date picker has data-testid=\"job-due-date\". The job dialog uses the naming convention data-testid=\"job-{field-name}\" with kebab-case field names: job-title, job-description, job-track-type, job-customer, job-assignee, job-priority, job-due-date, and job-save-btn."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Calendar Overview
summary: What the Calendar module shows, how the three views work, how PO deliveries integrate, and how it connects to jobs.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Calendar Overview

The Calendar module gives you a visual timeline of all jobs by their due dates, plus optional purchase order delivery dates. It is the go-to view when you need to see what is coming up, what is overdue, and how work is distributed across days, weeks, or months.

### Three Views

The calendar offers three views — **Month**, **Week**, and **Day** — toggled with buttons in the header. Each view shows the same underlying data at a different zoom level.

- **Month View** displays a traditional grid. Each cell shows the date number, up to three job chips, and an overflow count if there are more. Job chips are color-coded by their kanban stage and include priority icons for Critical and High priority work.
- **Week View** shows seven columns with expanded job chips that include the customer name and additional detail. It is useful for planning the current or upcoming week.
- **Day View** is a vertical list for a single date. Each job shows its full metadata: job number, title, priority, customer, stage, and assignee. An empty day shows an event_available icon and a message.

### PO Deliveries Toggle

The header includes a PO Deliveries toggle button (local_shipping icon). When active, purchase order expected delivery dates appear on the calendar alongside jobs. Each PO event shows the PO number, vendor name, and line count. This helps you anticipate incoming materials and coordinate production around deliveries.

### How Jobs Appear

Each job chip shows:
- The **job number** and **title**
- A **left border** colored by the job's kanban stage color
- **Priority icons**: a priority_high icon for Critical jobs and an arrow_upward icon for High priority jobs
- An **overdue warning** icon when the due date has passed

### Track Type Filtering

A track type dropdown in the header lets you filter which kanban track's jobs are shown. This is useful when you have multiple tracks (Production, R&D, Maintenance) and want to focus on one.

### Navigation

Previous and Next buttons (chevron_left / chevron_right) move backward and forward by one month, week, or day depending on the active view. The Today button snaps back to the current date. Clicking a date in Month or Week view switches to Day view for that date.

### When to Use the Calendar

- To see the week ahead and identify scheduling conflicts
- To check which jobs are overdue at a glance
- To correlate PO delivery dates with production schedules
- To quickly jump to a specific day and review all work due
- To filter by track type and focus on one area of the business", "sections": []}

title: Compliance Forms Overview
summary: What compliance forms are, which forms Forge supports (W-4, I-9, state withholding, direct deposit, workers' comp, handbook), when forms need updating, and how they connect to payroll and onboarding.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Compliance Forms Overview

The Employee Compliance Forms module manages the tax withholding, employment eligibility, and policy acknowledgment forms that every employee must complete. These are government-mandated and company-required documents that feed directly into payroll processing, employment verification, and regulatory compliance.

### What Forms Does Forge Support?

Forge handles six categories of compliance forms, each tracked as a ComplianceFormTemplate:

- **W-4 (Employee's Withholding Certificate)** — The IRS form that determines how much federal income tax is withheld from your paycheck. You choose your filing status (Single, Married Filing Jointly, Head of Household), indicate whether you hold multiple jobs, claim dependents, and specify any additional withholding or exemptions.

- **I-9 (Employment Eligibility Verification)** — The USCIS form that verifies your identity and authorization to work in the United States. It has two sections: Section 1 (completed by the employee — citizenship status, personal info, signature) and Section 2 (completed by the employer — document verification within 3 business days of the employee's start date). The I-9 requires supporting identity documents from List A (identity + work authorization) OR List B (identity) + List C (work authorization).

- **State Withholding** — Your state-specific income tax withholding form. Forge automatically determines which form you need based on your work location. Some states (like Texas, Florida, Nevada) have no income tax and require no form. Others use the federal W-4 as their withholding basis. States with their own forms (like California, New York, Idaho) have custom form definitions extracted and rendered electronically.

- **Direct Deposit** — Banking information for electronic payroll deposits. Records your bank name, account type (checking/savings), routing number, and account number. Sensitive fields are masked after submission (only last 4 digits shown).

- **Workers' Compensation Acknowledgment** — Company policy acknowledgment for workplace injury procedures and reporting requirements.

- **Employee Handbook Acknowledgment** — Confirmation that you have received, read, and agree to abide by the company's employee handbook.

### The Onboarding Wizard

All six form types are integrated into the unified onboarding wizard at /onboarding. New employees complete all required forms in a single guided workflow rather than navigating to each form individually. The Tax Forms page (Account > Tax Forms) shows a banner linking to the onboarding wizard when forms are incomplete.

After onboarding is complete, employees can return to Account > Tax Forms to review their submissions, download signed PDFs, or update forms when their tax situation changes.

### When Do Forms Need Updating?

- **W-4**: Any time your tax situation changes — marriage, divorce, new dependents, second job, or significant income changes. There is no annual requirement, but reviewing annually is recommended.
- **I-9**: Cannot be changed after submission per federal regulations. Contact HR for corrections. Reverification is required when work authorization documents expire.
- **State Withholding**: When your tax situation changes or when you move to a different state (your work location determines which state form applies).
- **Direct Deposit**: Contact payroll to update banking information.
- **Workers' Comp / Handbook**: Typically acknowledged once during onboarding. May require re-acknowledgment if policies are updated.

### How Forms Connect to Payroll

Compliance form data drives payroll calculations:
- W-4 filing status and withholding elections determine federal tax withholding amounts
- State withholding elections determine state tax withholding
- Direct deposit information routes payroll payments to the correct bank account
- Completed forms are tracked via profile completion keys that gate job assignment — employees with BlocksJobAssignment forms incomplete cannot be assigned to production jobs

### Form Lifecycle

Each form follows a consistent lifecycle:
1. **Template** — Admin configures the form template (source URL, form definition, DocuSeal integration)
2. **Submission Created** — Employee starts the form (status: Pending)
3. **Draft Saved** — Employee can save progress and return later
4. **Submitted** — Form data validated against required fields and submitted
5. **Signed** — For forms with DocuSeal integration, a fill-and-sign ceremony produces a legally signed PDF
6. **Completed** — Profile completion key updated, form marked complete

### Electronic Form Rendering

Forge renders government forms electronically using a metadata-driven compliance form renderer. The system extracts form definitions from official government PDFs (using pdf.js + AI verification), producing JSON layouts that match the original paper form's structure — including step sections, filing status radio groups, amount lines, signature blocks, and worksheets. This means employees fill out the actual W-4 or I-9 layout digitally, not a simplified approximation.

### DocuSeal Integration

Forms requiring legally binding signatures use DocuSeal for digital signing. The flow is: collect form data electronically → fill the official government PDF with the collected data (AcroForm field mapping) → send the pre-filled PDF to DocuSeal for e-signature → store the signed PDF in MinIO. This produces a legally compliant signed document that matches what would have been filed on paper.", "sections": []}

Saves the current form data as a draft without submitting. The employee can return later to continue filling out the form. Disabled while save is in progress (shows spinning sync icon)."}, {"label": "Submit Form", "value": "Primary action button with check icon in the form renderer footer. Validates all required fields against the form definition, then submits the form data. For fill-and-sign forms, this triggers the PDF fill + DocuSeal signing ceremony. Disabled when the form is invalid or submission is in progress."}, {"label": "Start E-Sign", "value": "Primary button with draw icon. Shown for forms with DocuSeal integration that have not yet been started. Creates a new submission and initiates the signing ceremony."}, {"label": "Download PDF", "value": "Download icon button shown on completed forms. Downloads the signed PDF copy of the submitted form. Disabled while download is in progress (shows spinning sync icon)."}, {"label": "Update", "value": "Primary edit icon button shown on completed forms that allow resubmission. Opens the form in edit mode for the employee to update their information. Not shown for I-9 forms (which cannot be changed after submission per federal law)."}, {"label": "Acknowledge & Complete", "value": "Primary check icon button for forms without DocuSeal integration or electronic form definitions (simple acknowledgment forms like workers' comp and handbook). Marks the form as completed."}, {"label": "Supporting Identity Documents", "value": "Button with folder_open icon, shown only for I-9 forms (requiresIdentityDocs = true). Opens a dialog for uploading List A, List B, and List C identity documents. Badge shows count of uploaded documents."}, {"label": "Prev / Next", "value": "Navigation buttons in the form renderer footer for multi-page forms. Shows current page number (e.g., '1 / 2'). Prev is disabled on the first page, Next on the last page."}], "heading": "Form Actions and Buttons"}, {"items": [{"label": "Status Banner (Complete)", "value": "Green banner with check_circle icon showing 'Completed MM/dd/yyyy'. Displayed when a submission exists but is not yet completed."}, {"label": "W-4 Summary", "value": "After completion, shows: Filing Status, Multiple Jobs (Yes/No), Exempt status (Yes/No). Does not show sensitive data like SSN or dollar amounts."}, {"label": "I-9 Summary", "value": "After completion, shows: Citizenship Status (U.S. Citizen, Noncitizen National, Lawful Permanent Resident, or Alien Authorized to Work) and document count. Includes a lock icon warning that I-9 forms cannot be changed after submission."}, {"label": "State Withholding Summary", "value": "After completion, shows: Filing Status, Allowances count, Additional Withholding dollar amount, and Exempt status (Yes/No)."}, {"label": "Direct Deposit Summary", "value": "After completion, shows: Bank name, Account Type, Routing number (last 4 digits, masked), Account number (last 4 digits, masked). Displayed in monospace font for masked values."}, {"label": "Workers' Comp / Handbook Summary", "value": "After completion, shows: 'Acknowledged on MM/dd/yyyy'. Sandboxed with allow-scripts, allow-same-origin, allow-forms, allow-popups."}, {"label": "Pending Setup Banner", "value": "Shown when a form template should have an electronic form definition (W-4, I-9, state withholding with sourceUrl) but the admin has not yet extracted it. Displays hourglass_empty icon with a message to contact the administrator."}], "heading": "Submission History"}, {"items": [{"label": "List A Documents", "value": "Documents that establish both identity AND employment authorization. Examples: U.S. Passport, Permanent Resident Card (Green Card), Employment Authorization Document (EAD), Foreign Passport with I-551 stamp. ONE List A document is sufficient."}, {"label": "List B Documents", "value": "Documents that establish identity only. Examples: Driver's License, State ID Card, School ID with photograph, Voter Registration Card, U.S. Military Card. Must be combined with a List C document."}, {"label": "List C Documents", "value": "Documents that establish employment authorization only. Examples: Social Security Card (unrestricted), Birth Certificate (U.S.), Certificate of Citizenship. Must be combined with a List B document."}, {"label": "Document Upload", "value": "Each list section has a file upload zone (app-file-upload-zone). Accepts PDF, JPG, JPEG, and PNG files up to 10 MB. Files are uploaded to the identity-docs entity type."}, {"label": "Document Verification Status", "value": "Each uploaded document shows either a verified badge with date (green verified icon + 'Verified MM/dd/yyyy') or a 'Pending verification' label. Verification is performed by an admin/manager reviewing the physical documents."}, {"label": "Document Type", "value": "The document type category (e.g., 'ListA', 'Passport', 'DriversLicense', 'SsnCard'). Displayed below the file name in the document list."}, {"label": "Remove Document", "value": "Red delete icon button on each document. Removes the identity document from the submission. Requires confirmation."}, {"label": "Done Button", "value": "Primary close button in the identity documents dialog footer. Closes the dialog and returns to the form detail view."}, {"label": "Validation Rule", "value": "I-9 requires either one List A document OR one List B + one List C document. The Submit Form button's validation popover will show the specific missing document requirement (e.g., 'List C document required' when only List B is uploaded)."}], "heading": "Identity Documents (I-9)"}, {"items": [{"label": "Required Fields", "value": "The server validates all fields marked required=true in the form definition JSON. Missing or empty required fields return a 400 error with the field label (e.g., 'First Name is required'). The client-side validation popover shows the same messages on hover over the disabled Submit button."}, {"label": "SSN Format", "value": "Social Security Number fields use a dedicated 'ssn' input type with automatic XXX-XX-XXXX formatting. Maximum length is 11 characters (9 digits + 2 dashes). The formatter inserts dashes as you type."}, {"label": "Filing Status", "value": "W-4 and state withholding forms require a filing status selection from radio button options. The field is required and must have a value selected before submission."}, {"label": "Signature Fields", "value": "Signature fields require typing your full legal name. The input has a distinctive signature-style appearance. A corresponding signature date field is rendered alongside and auto-populated."}, {"label": "Identity Document Requirement (I-9)", "value": "Before submitting an I-9, the system checks that identity documents are sufficient: either one List A document, or one List B + one List C document. If only List B is uploaded, the popover shows 'You need to upload a List C document'. If only List C, it shows 'You need to upload a List B document'. If neither, it shows 'Identity documents are required'."}, {"label": "I-9 Section 2 Deadline", "value": "Section 2 must be completed by the employer within 3 business days of the employee's first day of work. The deadline is stored as i9Section2OverdueAt and tracked for compliance."}, {"label": "I-9 Reverification", "value": "When work authorization documents have an expiration date, the system tracks i9ReverificationDueAt. HR is notified when reverification is approaching."}, {"label": "Form Definition Version Pinning", "value": "Each submission is pinned to the exact form definition version it was filled against (formDefinitionVersionId). This ensures the form can be regenerated with the original layout at any time, even if the form definition is later updated."}, {"label": "BlocksJobAssignment", "value": "Templates with blocksJobAssignment=true prevent the employee from being assigned to production jobs until the form is completed. This is enforced server-side — the job assignment API checks compliance status."}, {"label": "Resubmission Restrictions", "value": "I-9 forms cannot be resubmitted after completion (canResubmit returns false for formType 'i9'). All other form types allow the employee to update their submission by clicking the Update button."}], "heading": "Validation Rules"}]}
