title: Inventory Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the Inventory module: stock tracking, locations, movements, receiving, cycle counts, reservations, and replenishment.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "inv1", "text": "A shipment of raw materials just arrived from your vendor with a purchase order. How do you record it in Forge?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Inventory → Receiving tab, click Receive Goods, select the PO line, enter quantity, assign a location, and optionally record a lot number", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Go to Parts Catalog and manually increase the on-hand quantity for each part"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Create a new bin movement with reason 'Receive' in the Movements tab"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Go to Purchase Orders and mark the PO as 'Received'"}], "explanation": "Receiving is done from the Inventory → Receiving tab. Click Receive Goods, select the open PO line (only lines with remaining quantity appear), enter the quantity received, optionally assign a storage location and lot number. This automatically creates a bin content record, updates the PO line, and logs a movement with reason 'Receive'."}, {"id": "inv2", "text": "What is the correct hierarchy for storage locations in the Inventory module?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Warehouse → Zone → Aisle → Position"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Building → Floor → Room → Cabinet"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Area → Rack → Shelf → Bin", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Section → Row → Column → Slot"}], "explanation": "Storage locations follow the hierarchy Area → Rack → Shelf → Bin. Area is the top level (e.g., 'Warehouse A'), Rack is a shelving unit within an area, Shelf is a level on the rack, and Bin is the lowest level where stock is actually stored."}, {"id": "inv3", "text": "You notice the on-hand quantity for a part doesn't match what's physically on the shelf. What's the correct way to fix this?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Edit the bin content record directly in the database"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Go to Stock Ops tab, click Adjust, select the bin content, enter the correct quantity, and provide a mandatory reason", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Delete the bin content and re-create it with the correct quantity"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Create a Transfer moving stock to the same bin with the corrected amount"}], "explanation": "Stock adjustments are done via the Adjust dialog on the Stock Ops tab. You must provide the new quantity and a mandatory reason (e.g., 'recount corrected', 'damaged goods'). This creates a movement record with reason 'Adjustment' for full audit traceability. The reason field cannot be left blank."}, {"id": "inv4", "text": "A row in the Stock table is highlighted in red. What does this indicate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The part has been discontinued and should not be reordered"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The available quantity is zero or negative — there is no pickable stock", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The part is on QC Hold and cannot be used"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The part's lot has expired and must be disposed of"}], "explanation": "A red row in the Stock table means the available quantity (on hand minus reserved) is zero or negative. This means there is no stock available to pick or ship. A yellow row indicates low stock (available is less than 20% of on-hand)."}, {"id": "inv5", "text": "What is the difference between the 'Reserved' bin content status and a 'Reservation' record?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "They are the same thing — both refer to stock held for a job"}, {"id": "b", "text": "'Reserved' is the bin content status flag, while a Reservation is a separate record linking stock to a specific job or sales order with quantity and notes", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "'Reserved' applies to entire bins, while Reservations apply to individual items"}, {"id": "d", "text": "'Reserved' is for customer-owned stock, while Reservations are for internal use"}], "explanation": "'Reserved' is a status on a bin content record indicating the stock is held. A Reservation is a separate record that links a specific quantity from a bin content to a job or sales order, with optional notes. Reservations reduce the 'available' quantity shown in the Stock tab. Releasing a reservation makes the quantity available again."}, {"id": "inv6", "text": "You need to move 50 units of a part from Warehouse A, Rack 1, Bin 3 to Warehouse B, Rack 2, Bin 1. Which feature do you use?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Create a new Receive record with the destination bin"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Go to Stock Ops tab, click Transfer, select the source bin content, choose the destination bin, and enter quantity 50", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Delete the stock from the first bin and place new stock in the second bin"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Use the Movements tab to manually create a movement record"}], "explanation": "Stock transfers are done via the Transfer dialog on the Stock Ops tab. Select the source bin content record, choose the destination location, and enter the quantity. This creates a movement record with reason 'Transfer' and updates both bin contents automatically."}, {"id": "inv7", "text": "What movement reasons are automatically generated by the system (not manually created)?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Receive (from PO receiving) and Cycle Count (from approved cycle counts)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "All reasons are manually selected by the user"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Only Ship and Pick are automatic"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Move and Transfer are the only automatic reasons"}], "explanation": "'Receive' movements are automatically created when goods are received against a PO line via the Receiving tab. 'Cycle Count' movements are auto-generated when a cycle count with variance is approved, creating adjustment records. Other reasons like Transfer, Adjustment, Pick, and Ship are created through their respective operations."}, {"id": "inv8", "text": "A cycle count shows a variance of -5 for a part in Bin 12. What does this mean?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Five extra units were found that the system didn't know about"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The system expected 5 more units than were physically counted — there is a shortage of 5", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Five units were moved to a different bin during the count"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The count was rejected because the variance exceeds the threshold"}], "explanation": "A negative variance means the actual count was lower than the expected (system) quantity. A variance of -5 means 5 fewer units were found than the system recorded. When the cycle count is approved, an adjustment movement is created to correct the system quantity to match the physical count."}, {"id": "inv9", "text": "Which PO statuses allow receiving goods in the Inventory module?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Only 'Submitted' purchase orders can be received against"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Submitted, Acknowledged, and Partially Received", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Any PO status except Cancelled and Closed"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Only 'Acknowledged' — the vendor must confirm before receiving is allowed"}], "explanation": "The Receive Goods dialog only shows PO lines from purchase orders with status Submitted, Acknowledged, or Partially Received. Additionally, only lines with remaining quantity (ordered minus already received) greater than zero are shown."}, {"id": "inv10", "text": "What does the 'Days of Stock Remaining' metric on the Replenishment tab represent?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The number of days until the next scheduled delivery arrives"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Available stock divided by the average daily consumption (burn rate) — how long current stock will last at the current usage rate", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The number of business days since the last stock receipt"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The lead time in days for the preferred vendor to deliver"}], "explanation": "Days of Stock Remaining is calculated by dividing available stock by the burn rate (average daily consumption). For example, if you have 100 units available and consume 10 per day, you have 10 days of stock remaining. This drives the Projected Stockout Date and reorder suggestions."}, {"id": "inv11", "text": "You scan a barcode while on the Inventory page. What happens?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "A dialog opens asking what action to take with the scanned code"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The scanned value is placed in the search field and the view switches to the Stock tab", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The system looks up the barcode in the location database and navigates to that bin"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Nothing — barcode scanning only works on the Shop Floor display"}], "explanation": "The Inventory module is scanner-aware with context 'inventory'. When a barcode is scanned, the value is automatically placed into the search field and the view switches to the Stock tab. This lets you quickly look up a part by scanning its label."}, {"id": "inv12", "text": "What is the 'QC Hold' bin content status used for?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Stock that has failed quality inspection and must be scrapped"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Stock awaiting quality inspection — it cannot be picked or shipped until QC releases it", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Stock that is reserved for quality control testing samples"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Stock returned by a customer pending quality review"}], "explanation": "QC Hold prevents stock from being picked or shipped until it passes quality inspection. Once inspection is complete, a 'QC Release' movement changes the status back to Stored, making the stock available. This is commonly used for incoming materials that require inspection before use in production."}, {"id": "inv13", "text": "How are reorder suggestions created in the Replenishment tab?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Users manually create suggestions by clicking 'Add Suggestion'"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The system automatically generates suggestions based on burn rate analysis, reorder points, lead times, and safety stock levels", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Suggestions are imported from the vendor's catalog system"}, {"id": "d", "text": "They are copied from the previous month's purchase orders"}], "explanation": "Reorder suggestions are system-generated based on burn rate analysis (30/60/90-day consumption), configured reorder points, lead times, and safety stock days. When available stock drops to or below the reorder point, a suggestion is created with a calculated order quantity. Suggestions can be approved (creating a PO) or dismissed (with a required reason)."}, {"id": "inv14", "text": "You want to create a new storage location called 'Bin 7' inside 'Rack B2' in 'Warehouse A'. What do you set in the Create Location dialog?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Name: 'Bin 7', Type: Bin, Parent: select 'Rack B2' from the dropdown", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Name: 'Warehouse A > Rack B2 > Bin 7', Type: Area, no parent"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Name: 'Bin 7', Type: Shelf, Parent: 'Warehouse A'"}, {"id": "d", "text": "You must first create 'Bin 7' as an Area, then move it under Rack B2"}], "explanation": "To create a bin inside an existing rack, set the Name to 'Bin 7', select Type as 'Bin' (the lowest level), and set the Parent to 'Rack B2' from the dropdown. The system will nest it in the hierarchy: Warehouse A → Rack B2 → Bin 7. The full path is displayed in stock tables and movement logs."}, {"id": "inv15", "text": "What happens when you approve a cycle count that has variances?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The variances are logged but no quantities are changed — you must manually adjust each one"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Adjustment movements are automatically generated to correct system quantities to match the physical counts", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "An email is sent to the warehouse manager listing all discrepancies"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The count is flagged for a second verification before any changes are made"}], "explanation": "Approving a cycle count with variances automatically generates adjustment movements with reason 'Cycle Count' to correct the system quantities. Each line with a non-zero variance gets an adjustment to make the system quantity match the actual counted quantity. This maintains the audit trail while keeping inventory accurate."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Invoices Overview
summary: What the Invoices module does, standalone vs integrated mode, statuses, line items, and how invoices connect to sales orders, shipments, and payments.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Invoices Overview

The Invoices module handles billing your customers for completed work. Each invoice is a formal request for payment that lists the goods or services provided, their quantities, prices, tax, and the total amount due.

### Accounting Boundary — Standalone vs Integrated Mode

Invoices are an **accounting-bounded feature**. This means the module operates differently depending on whether an accounting provider (e.g., QuickBooks Online) is connected:

- **Standalone mode** (no provider connected): Full CRUD — create, edit, send, void, and delete invoices directly in Forge. This is the default for shops that don't use external accounting software.
- **Integrated mode** (provider connected): Invoices become **read-only**. They are synced from the accounting system and displayed for reference, but all invoice management happens in the connected provider. A banner at the top of the page indicates which provider is managing invoices.

The rest of this article describes standalone mode, where you have full control.

### Invoice Statuses

Every invoice moves through a lifecycle tracked by its status:

- **Draft** (gray chip) — The invoice has been created but not yet sent to the customer. You can still edit, void, or delete it.
- **Sent** (blue chip) — The invoice has been sent to the customer. It can still be voided, but not deleted.
- **Partially Paid** (yellow chip) — The customer has made a partial payment. The balance due reflects the remaining amount.
- **Paid** (green chip) — The invoice is fully paid. The balance due is zero.
- **Overdue** (red chip) — The due date has passed and the invoice has not been fully paid. The balance due column highlights in red.
- **Voided** (gray chip) — The invoice has been cancelled. Voided invoices remain in the system for audit purposes but have no financial impact.

### Line Items

Each invoice contains one or more line items. A line item represents a billable entry with:
- An optional **Part ID** and **Part Number** linking the line to a part in the Parts Catalog
- A **Description** of the goods or services
- A **Quantity** and **Unit Price**
- A computed **Line Total** (Quantity x Unit Price)

The invoice's **Subtotal** is the sum of all line totals. **Tax** is calculated as Subtotal x Tax Rate. The **Total** is Subtotal + Tax. The **Balance Due** is Total minus any payments applied.

### How Invoices Connect to Other Features

**Sales Orders:** An invoice can be linked to a Sales Order. This connects the billing to the original customer order and lets you trace the full quote-to-cash cycle: Quote → Sales Order → Production → Shipment → Invoice → Payment.

**Shipments:** An invoice can also reference a specific Shipment. This is useful for per-shipment invoicing when a sales order is fulfilled in multiple deliveries.

**Payments:** Payments are applied to invoices through Payment Applications. As payments are recorded, the invoice's Amount Paid increases and Balance Due decreases. When fully paid, the status changes to Paid.

**Jobs:** Completed jobs that haven't been invoiced appear in the Uninvoiced Jobs panel. You can create an invoice directly from a job, which pre-populates the customer and generates line items from the job details.

**Customers:** Every invoice belongs to a customer. The customer link in the invoice detail is clickable and navigates to the customer's full profile.

### PDF Generation

Invoices can be exported as PDF documents for emailing or printing. The server generates the PDF using QuestPDF with your company branding, line items, totals, and payment terms.

### When to Use Invoices

- After completing a job and shipping goods to the customer
- When billing for services rendered
- When you need a formal payment request with line items and tax
- To track payment status and outstanding balances
- To maintain an audit trail of all billing activity", "sections": []}

title: MFA Field Reference
summary: Complete reference for MFA setup fields, QR code, manual key, recovery codes, admin policy controls, login challenge, and validation rules.
contentJson: {"title": "MFA Field Reference", "groups": [{"items": [{"label": "Status Banner", "value": "Shows 'Two-factor authentication is enabled' (green verified_user icon) or 'Two-factor authentication is not enabled' (gray shield icon). Reflects the current state of MFA on your account."}, {"label": "Policy Enforcement Badge", "value": "Displayed when an admin has required MFA for your role. Shows 'Required by organization policy' with a policy icon. When enforced and MFA is disabled, shows a warning icon with 'Required by organization policy — you must enable MFA'."}, {"label": "Recovery Codes Remaining", "value": "Displays the count of unused recovery codes (e.g., '8 recovery codes remaining'). Shows a vpn_key icon. Zero remaining means you have no backup if you lose your authenticator."}], "heading": "MFA Status (Account > Security)"}, {"items": [{"label": "QR Code", "value": "A 200x200 pixel QR code encoding the TOTP secret URI. Scan with any TOTP-compatible authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, Microsoft Authenticator, 1Password, Bitwarden). Error correction level: M."}, {"label": "Manual Entry Key", "value": "Hidden by default behind a 'Can't scan? Enter key manually' toggle. Displays the TOTP secret as a text string in monospace font. Includes a copy-to-clipboard button. Use when QR scanning is not available."}, {"label": "Verification Code (required)", "value": "Text input, 6 characters max. Enter the current 6-digit code displayed in your authenticator app. Must match the expected TOTP value. data-testid: mfa-verify-code"}, {"label": "Verify & Enable Button", "value": "Submits the verification code. Disabled when the code field is empty or invalid, or while verification is in progress. On success, MFA is immediately enabled. On failure, an error message appears below the input. data-testid: mfa-verify-submit"}], "heading": "MFA Setup Dialog Fields"}, {"items": [{"label": "Step 1: Loading", "value": "Shows a spinner with 'Preparing authenticator setup...' while the server generates the TOTP secret and QR code URI."}, {"label": "Step 2: Scan & Verify", "value": "Displays the QR code, manual key toggle, and verification code input. User scans QR, then enters the code from their authenticator app."}, {"label": "Step 3: Complete", "value": "Success screen with verified_user icon and confirmation message. Reminds user to generate recovery codes from the Security page."}], "heading": "MFA Setup Dialog Steps"}, {"items": [{"label": "Device Name", "value": "The name of the registered device (e.g., 'My Phone', 'Work Tablet'). Falls back to the device type (e.g., 'Totp') if no name was provided."}, {"label": "Device Type Icon", "value": "phone_android for TOTP devices, key for WebAuthn/hardware keys."}, {"label": "Last Used Date", "value": "Date the device was last used for authentication, formatted as MM/dd/yyyy. Blank if never used."}, {"label": "Default Badge", "value": "Blue 'Default' chip shown on the primary MFA device. The default device is used first during login challenges."}, {"label": "Remove Device Button", "value": "Red trash icon button. Opens a confirmation dialog warning that removing your only device may lock you out. Requires confirmation before deletion."}], "heading": "Registered Devices Section"}, {"items": [{"label": "Warning Message", "value": "Server-provided warning text explaining that generating new codes invalidates all previous codes. Displayed with a yellow warning icon."}, {"label": "Code Grid", "value": "Displays all generated recovery codes in a grid of monospace code blocks. Each code is a one-time-use backup for signing in without your authenticator."}, {"label": "Copy All Button", "value": "Copies all recovery codes to the clipboard as a newline-separated list."}, {"label": "Download Button", "value": "Downloads all recovery codes as a text file for offline storage."}, {"label": "I've Saved These Codes Button", "value": "Primary action button that closes the dialog. Confirms the user has saved their codes somewhere safe."}], "heading": "Recovery Codes Dialog"}, {"items": [{"label": "Required for Roles (multi-select)", "value": "Multi-select dropdown listing all system roles (e.g., Admin, Manager, Engineer, PM, Production Worker, Office Manager). Selected roles have MFA enforced — users in those roles cannot disable MFA and must set it up if not already enabled."}, {"label": "Save Button", "value": "Saves the selected role enforcement policy. data-testid: mfa-save-policy"}, {"label": "User Compliance Table", "value": "DataTable showing all users with columns: Name (sortable), Email (sortable), Role (sortable, filterable), MFA Enabled (sortable — green 'Enabled' or gray 'Disabled' chip), Device Type (sortable — shows device type or dash), Enforced (sortable — yellow 'Enforced' chip or dash). tableId: mfa-compliance"}], "heading": "Admin MFA Policy Panel (Admin > MFA)"}, {"items": [{"label": "Challenge Screen", "value": "Appears after successful password authentication when MFA is enabled. Shows a prompt to enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app."}, {"label": "Challenge Token", "value": "Server-issued temporary token that identifies the authentication session. Passed back with the verification code. Not visible to the user."}, {"label": "Verification Code Input", "value": "6-digit numeric input. Must match the current TOTP code for the user's registered device."}, {"label": "Remember This Device", "value": "Checkbox option to skip MFA on this device for future logins. Sets a trusted device cookie."}, {"label": "Use Recovery Code", "value": "Link/button to switch to recovery code entry mode instead of TOTP code. Enter one of your one-time backup codes."}, {"label": "Masked Target", "value": "For SMS/Email MFA types, shows a masked phone number or email (e.g., '***-***-1234' or 'j***@example.com'). Not applicable for TOTP."}], "heading": "Login MFA Challenge"}, {"items": [{"label": "Verification Code", "value": "Required. Must be exactly 6 digits. Must match the current TOTP value for the registered secret. Invalid codes show an inline error below the input field."}, {"label": "Recovery Code", "value": "Required. Must match one of the user's unused recovery codes. Used codes are permanently consumed and cannot be reused."}, {"label": "MFA Device Removal", "value": "Requires confirmation dialog (severity: danger). Warning message notes that removing the only device may cause lockout."}, {"label": "MFA Disable", "value": "Requires confirmation dialog (severity: danger). Removes all devices and recovery codes. Cannot be performed when MFA is enforced by admin policy."}, {"label": "Admin Policy Save", "value": "At least zero roles can be selected (empty selection means no enforcement). Changes take effect immediately on save."}], "heading": "Validation Rules"}, {"items": [{"label": "Totp", "value": "Time-based One-Time Password. Primary and recommended method. Uses authenticator apps (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.) to generate 6-digit codes that rotate every 30 seconds."}, {"label": "WebAuthn", "value": "Hardware security keys (YubiKey, etc.) or platform authenticators (Windows Hello, Touch ID). Uses public-key cryptography — stores CredentialId, PublicKey, and SignCount."}, {"label": "Sms", "value": "Verification code sent via SMS to a registered phone number. Less secure than TOTP due to SIM swapping risks."}, {"label": "Email", "value": "Verification code sent to a registered email address. Fallback option when other methods are unavailable."}], "heading": "MFA Device Types"}, {"items": [{"label": "DeviceType", "value": "Enum: Totp, Sms, Email, WebAuthn. Determines the authentication method and which fields are relevant."}, {"label": "EncryptedSecret", "value": "The TOTP secret key, encrypted at rest via ASP.NET Data Protection API. Used to generate/validate TOTP codes. Never exposed to the client after setup."}, {"label": "DeviceName", "value": "Optional user-provided name for the device (e.g., 'My iPhone'). Displayed in the device list. Falls back to DeviceType if null."}, {"label": "IsVerified", "value": "Boolean. True after the user successfully verifies a code during setup. Unverified devices are not usable for login."}, {"label": "IsDefault", "value": "Boolean. The primary device used first during login challenges. Only one device per user should be default."}, {"label": "LastUsedAt", "value": "Timestamp of the last successful authentication with this device. Null if never used. Displayed as MM/dd/yyyy in the UI."}, {"label": "FailedAttempts", "value": "Counter for consecutive failed verification attempts. Used for lockout logic."}, {"label": "LockedUntil", "value": "Timestamp until which the device is locked due to too many failed attempts. Null when not locked."}], "heading": "Entity Fields (UserMfaDevice)"}, {"items": [{"label": "CodeHash", "value": "PBKDF2 hash of the recovery code. The plaintext code is only shown once during generation and never stored."}, {"label": "IsUsed", "value": "Boolean. Set to true when the code is consumed during a recovery login. Used codes cannot be reused."}, {"label": "UsedAt", "value": "Timestamp when the code was consumed. Null if not yet used."}, {"label": "UsedFromIp", "value": "IP address from which the recovery code was used. Recorded for security audit purposes."}], "heading": "Entity Fields (MfaRecoveryCode)"}]}

title: MFA Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of multi-factor authentication: TOTP setup, recovery codes, admin policies, and login flow.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "mfa1", "text": "You want to enable two-factor authentication on your Forge account. Where do you go?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Admin > Users > edit your profile and toggle MFA on"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Account > Security, then click 'Enable Two-Factor Authentication' in the MFA card", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Settings > Privacy > Two-Factor tab"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Login screen > click 'Set up MFA' link"}], "explanation": "MFA is set up from Account > Security. The Two-Factor Authentication card shows your current status and provides the Enable button, which opens the setup dialog with QR code and verification."}, {"id": "mfa2", "text": "During MFA setup, the QR code cannot be scanned by your authenticator app. What should you do?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Contact your admin to manually enable MFA on your account"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Click 'Can't scan? Enter key manually' to reveal the secret key, then type it into your authenticator app", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Use a different browser and try again"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Skip MFA setup — it will work without scanning"}], "explanation": "The setup dialog has a toggle labeled 'Can't scan? Enter key manually' that reveals the TOTP secret as a copyable text string. You can paste this key into your authenticator app's manual entry option."}, {"id": "mfa3", "text": "You've entered a 6-digit code from your authenticator app but the verification failed. Why might this happen?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Your authenticator app is not compatible with Forge"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The code has already rotated — TOTP codes change every 30 seconds, so the code you entered may have expired", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "MFA only works with Google Authenticator, not other apps"}, {"id": "d", "text": "You need to scan the QR code again before entering a code"}], "explanation": "TOTP codes rotate every 30 seconds. If you entered a code just as it was expiring, it may fail validation. Wait for the next code to appear in your authenticator app and try again."}, {"id": "mfa4", "text": "You've lost your phone and can't access your authenticator app. How do you sign in to Forge?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "You cannot — contact your admin to reset your account"}, {"id": "b", "text": "On the MFA challenge screen, click 'Use recovery code' and enter one of your saved backup codes", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Use your kiosk PIN instead of the MFA code"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Wait 24 hours and MFA automatically disables itself"}], "explanation": "Recovery codes are one-time-use backup codes generated when you set up MFA. On the login MFA challenge screen, switch to recovery code entry mode and use one of your saved codes. Each code can only be used once."}, {"id": "mfa5", "text": "You just generated new recovery codes. What happens to your old recovery codes?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "They remain valid alongside the new ones for 30 days"}, {"id": "b", "text": "All previous recovery codes are immediately invalidated — only the new set works", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Old codes continue working but are marked as deprecated"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Nothing — you can have multiple sets of active recovery codes"}], "explanation": "Generating new recovery codes invalidates all previous codes immediately. This is a security measure to ensure only one set of valid codes exists at any time. The dialog warns you about this before generating."}, {"id": "mfa6", "text": "Your admin has enforced MFA for your role. What changes on your Security page?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Nothing changes — enforcement only affects new users"}, {"id": "b", "text": "A policy badge appears saying 'Required by organization policy' and the Disable MFA button is hidden", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Your MFA is automatically enabled without any setup needed"}, {"id": "d", "text": "You receive a one-time email with instructions but can still disable MFA"}], "explanation": "When MFA is enforced by admin policy, your Security page shows a 'Required by organization policy' badge and the Disable MFA button is hidden. You cannot turn off MFA while the policy applies to your role. If MFA is not yet enabled, a warning urges you to set it up."}, {"id": "mfa7", "text": "As an admin, you want to require MFA for all Manager and Admin users. How do you do this?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Edit each user individually and check 'Require MFA' on their profile"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Go to Admin > MFA, select Manager and Admin in the 'Required for Roles' multi-select, and click Save", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Enable a global setting that forces MFA for all users regardless of role"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Send an email notification asking those users to enable MFA voluntarily"}], "explanation": "The Admin > MFA panel has a 'Required for Roles' multi-select dropdown. Select the roles you want to enforce (e.g., Manager, Admin) and click Save. The compliance table below shows which users have MFA enabled and which roles are enforced."}, {"id": "mfa8", "text": "In the Admin MFA compliance table, a user shows 'Disabled' under MFA Enabled and 'Enforced' under the Enforced column. What does this mean?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The user has disabled MFA against policy — no action needed"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The user's role requires MFA but they haven't set it up yet — they'll be prompted on next login", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "There's a system error — enforcement and disabled status can't coexist"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The user was recently removed from an enforced role"}], "explanation": "When a user's role has MFA enforced but they haven't set it up yet, the compliance table shows Disabled + Enforced. The user will see a warning on their Security page and must complete MFA setup. They cannot bypass this requirement."}, {"id": "mfa9", "text": "You have two authenticator devices registered. One shows a blue 'Default' chip. What does the Default designation mean?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "It's the only device that can be used — the other is a backup that must be activated first"}, {"id": "b", "text": "It's the device used first during login challenges — the system prompts for a code from this device before trying others", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "It's the device that was registered first chronologically"}, {"id": "d", "text": "It receives push notifications while the non-default device requires manual code entry"}], "explanation": "The Default device is the one used first during the login MFA challenge. When you sign in, the system prompts for a code from your default device. You can still use any registered device, but the default is the primary one presented."}, {"id": "mfa10", "text": "You want to remove an MFA device from your account, but it's your only registered device. What happens?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The system prevents removal — you must have at least one device"}, {"id": "b", "text": "A confirmation dialog warns that removing your only device may lock you out, and you must confirm the deletion", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The device is removed and MFA is automatically disabled"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The device is removed but a temporary bypass code is emailed to you"}], "explanation": "Clicking the delete icon on your only device opens a danger-severity confirmation dialog warning that you may lose access to your account. If you confirm, the device is removed. You should have recovery codes saved as a backup, or disable MFA entirely if you no longer want it."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Invoices — Guided Tour
summary: A guided tour of the invoices list page: filters, data table, creating invoices, uninvoiced jobs panel, and viewing invoice details.
contentJson: {"steps": [{"element": "[data-testid='new-invoice-btn']", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Create Invoice Button", "description": "Click here to create a new invoice. A split-layout dialog opens with line items on the left and invoice details (customer, dates, tax rate, credit terms, notes) on the right. You must add at least one line item before saving."}}, {"element": ".page-header .action-btn:first-of-type", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Uninvoiced Jobs", "description": "Opens the Uninvoiced Jobs panel showing completed jobs that haven't been invoiced yet. Each job has a one-click 'Invoice' button to generate an invoice directly. The badge shows how many uninvoiced jobs exist."}}, {"element": ".filters-bar app-input", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Search Invoices", "description": "Search by invoice number, customer name, or other text fields. Press Enter to apply the search filter. Results update in the table below."}}, {"element": ".filters-bar app-select", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Status Filter", "description": "Filter invoices by status: Draft, Sent, Partially Paid, Paid, Overdue, or Voided. Select 'All Statuses' to clear the filter and show everything."}}, {"element": "app-data-table", "popover": {"side": "top", "title": "Invoices Table", "description": "Displays all invoices with columns for Invoice #, Customer, Status (color-coded chip), Invoice Date, Due Date, Total, Paid, and Balance Due. Click any row to open the invoice detail dialog. Click column headers to sort. Overdue balances are highlighted in red."}}], "appRoute": "/invoices", "startButtonLabel": "Tour Invoices"}

title: Invoices Field Reference
summary: Complete reference for every field, button, status, validation rule, and action in the Invoices module.
contentJson: {"title": "Invoices Field Reference", "groups": [{"items": [{"label": "Customer (required)", "value": "Dropdown of active customers. Determines who the invoice is billed to. Must select a customer before saving. data-testid: invoice-customer"}, {"label": "Sales Order ID", "value": "Number input, optional. Links the invoice to an existing Sales Order for traceability through the order-to-cash cycle. Shown as a clickable entity link in the detail view."}, {"label": "Invoice Date (required)", "value": "Date picker. The date the invoice is issued. Displayed in MM/dd/yyyy format. data-testid: invoice-date"}, {"label": "Due Date (required)", "value": "Date picker. The date payment is expected. Used to determine Overdue status. Displayed in MM/dd/yyyy format. data-testid: invoice-due-date"}, {"label": "Credit Terms", "value": "Dropdown of standard credit terms (Net 15, Net 30, Net 45, Net 60, Net 90, Due on Receipt, etc.). Optional. Sets payment expectations for the customer. data-testid: invoice-credit-terms"}, {"label": "Tax Rate (required)", "value": "Number input with % suffix. The tax percentage applied to the subtotal. Defaults to 0. Must be >= 0. The computed tax amount and grand total update live as you change this value. data-testid: invoice-tax-rate"}, {"label": "Shipment ID", "value": "Number input, optional. Links the invoice to a specific shipment for per-shipment invoicing."}, {"label": "Notes", "value": "Textarea, optional. Free-form notes that appear on the invoice. 3 rows visible. data-testid: invoice-notes"}], "heading": "Invoice Form Fields (Create Dialog — Sidebar)"}, {"items": [{"label": "Part ID", "value": "Number input, optional. Links the line item to a part record in the system for traceability."}, {"label": "Part Number", "value": "Text input, optional. The part number displayed on the invoice. Shown in monospace font in both the dialog table and detail view. data-testid: invoice-line-desc (on description)"}, {"label": "Description (required)", "value": "Text input. Describes the goods or services being billed. Must not be empty."}, {"label": "Qty (required)", "value": "Number input. Quantity of items. Must be at least 1. data-testid: invoice-line-qty"}, {"label": "Unit Price (required)", "value": "Number input. Price per unit. Must be >= 0. Displayed as currency. data-testid: invoice-line-price"}, {"label": "Total (computed)", "value": "Automatically calculated as Qty x Unit Price. Displayed as currency. Not editable."}, {"label": "Add button", "value": "Adds the line item to the invoice. Disabled when line form is invalid (missing description or invalid qty/price). data-testid: invoice-add-line-btn"}, {"label": "Remove button (X icon)", "value": "Red icon button on each line row. Removes the line item from the invoice."}], "heading": "Line Item Fields (Create Dialog — Main Area)"}, {"items": [{"label": "Subtotal", "value": "Sum of all line item totals. Updates live as lines are added or removed."}, {"label": "Tax", "value": "Computed as Subtotal x Tax Rate %. Shows the tax rate percentage in parentheses."}, {"label": "Total", "value": "Subtotal + Tax. Bold, highlighted row. This is the total amount the customer owes."}], "heading": "Invoice Summary (Create Dialog — Sidebar)"}, {"items": [{"label": "Draft (gray chip, chip--muted)", "value": "Invoice created but not sent. Can be edited, voided, or deleted. Default status for new invoices."}, {"label": "Sent (blue chip, chip--info)", "value": "Invoice has been sent to the customer. Can be voided but not deleted. Awaiting payment."}, {"label": "Partially Paid (yellow chip, chip--warning)", "value": "Customer has made a partial payment. Balance Due shows remaining amount. Cannot be voided or deleted."}, {"label": "Paid (green chip, chip--success)", "value": "Invoice fully paid. Balance Due is $0.00. No further actions available."}, {"label": "Overdue (red chip, chip--error)", "value": "Due date has passed with outstanding balance. Balance Due column highlighted in red in the table."}, {"label": "Voided (gray chip, chip--muted)", "value": "Invoice cancelled. Remains in system for audit trail. No financial impact. Cannot be un-voided."}], "heading": "Invoice Statuses"}, {"items": [{"label": "Invoice Number", "value": "Auto-generated identifier displayed in the header with a receipt_long icon."}, {"label": "Customer", "value": "Clickable entity link that navigates to the customer detail page."}, {"label": "Sales Order", "value": "Clickable entity link to the linked Sales Order. Only shown if a Sales Order is linked."}, {"label": "Shipment", "value": "Clickable entity link to the linked Shipment. Only shown if a Shipment is linked."}, {"label": "Invoice Date", "value": "Date the invoice was issued. Displayed as MM/dd/yyyy."}, {"label": "Due Date", "value": "Payment due date. Displayed as MM/dd/yyyy."}, {"label": "Credit Terms", "value": "The payment terms (e.g., Net 30). Only shown if set."}, {"label": "Notes", "value": "Free-form notes. Only shown if present."}, {"label": "Line Items Table", "value": "Columns: Part #, Description, Qty, Unit Price, Total. Part numbers in monospace. Dash shown for lines without a part number."}, {"label": "Subtotal", "value": "Sum of all line totals."}, {"label": "Tax (rate%)", "value": "Tax amount with the rate shown in parentheses."}, {"label": "Total", "value": "Grand total (subtotal + tax). Bold styling."}, {"label": "Amount Paid", "value": "Sum of all payment applications against this invoice."}, {"label": "Balance Due", "value": "Total minus Amount Paid. Bold styling. Highlighted when non-zero."}, {"label": "Created / Updated", "value": "Timestamps in MM/dd/yyyy format."}, {"label": "Activity Log", "value": "Chronological timeline of all changes to the invoice (status changes, edits, etc.)."}], "heading": "Detail View Fields"}, {"items": [{"label": "New Invoice (primary button)", "value": "Opens the create invoice dialog with split layout. data-testid: new-invoice-btn"}, {"label": "Uninvoiced Jobs (header button)", "value": "Opens the Uninvoiced Jobs panel showing completed jobs without invoices. Badge shows count."}, {"label": "Create Invoice (dialog save)", "value": "Saves the new invoice. Disabled when form is invalid, no lines exist, or save is in progress. Shows validation popover on hover when disabled. data-testid: invoice-save-btn"}, {"label": "Cancel (dialog)", "value": "Closes the create dialog without saving. Prompts if form is dirty (unsaved changes)."}, {"label": "Send (detail, primary)", "value": "Marks the invoice as Sent. Only available when status is Draft. Icon: send."}, {"label": "Void (detail)", "value": "Voids the invoice after confirmation dialog. Available when status is Draft or Sent. Icon: block. Confirmation uses warn severity."}, {"label": "Delete (detail, danger)", "value": "Soft-deletes the invoice after confirmation dialog. Only available when status is Draft. Icon: delete. Confirmation uses danger severity."}, {"label": "Close (detail, icon)", "value": "Closes the detail dialog. Icon: close. Tooltip: 'Close'."}, {"label": "Invoice from Job (uninvoiced panel)", "value": "Creates an invoice directly from a completed job. Disabled if the job has no customer assigned. Pre-populates customer and line items."}, {"label": "Row click (table)", "value": "Opens the invoice detail dialog via DetailDialogService. URL updates to ?detail=invoice:{id} for bookmarkability."}], "heading": "Buttons and Actions"}, {"items": [{"label": "Customer", "value": "Required. Must select a customer from the dropdown."}, {"label": "Invoice Date", "value": "Required. Must select a date."}, {"label": "Due Date", "value": "Required. Must select a date."}, {"label": "Tax Rate", "value": "Required. Must be a number >= 0."}, {"label": "At least one line item", "value": "The save button is disabled until at least one line item has been added."}, {"label": "Line Description", "value": "Required for each line item. Cannot be empty."}, {"label": "Line Quantity", "value": "Required. Must be >= 1."}, {"label": "Line Unit Price", "value": "Required. Must be >= 0."}, {"label": "Validation popover", "value": "Hovering over the disabled Save button shows a bulleted list of which fields need attention."}], "heading": "Validation Rules"}, {"items": [{"label": "Invoice # (120px)", "value": "Sortable. The auto-generated invoice number. Styled with monospace-like formatting."}, {"label": "Customer", "value": "Sortable. The customer name."}, {"label": "Status (130px)", "value": "Sortable, filterable (enum). Color-coded chip: Draft (gray), Sent (blue), Partially Paid (yellow), Paid (green), Overdue (red), Voided (gray)."}, {"label": "Invoice Date (110px)", "value": "Sortable, date type. The issue date in MM/dd/yyyy format."}, {"label": "Due Date (110px)", "value": "Sortable, date type. The payment due date in MM/dd/yyyy format."}, {"label": "Total (100px, right-aligned)", "value": "Sortable. The invoice total displayed as currency."}, {"label": "Paid (100px, right-aligned)", "value": "Sortable. The amount paid to date displayed as currency."}, {"label": "Balance (100px, right-aligned)", "value": "Sortable. The remaining balance due. Warning color when balance > 0 and not overdue. Error color when overdue."}, {"label": "Created (110px)", "value": "Sortable, date type. The creation date in MM/dd/yyyy format."}], "heading": "Table Columns (List Page)"}]}

title: Invoices Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the Invoices module: statuses, line items, accounting boundary, and common workflows.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "inv1", "text": "Your shop does not use QuickBooks or any other accounting provider. What mode does the Invoices module operate in?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Integrated mode — invoices are synced from the accounting system"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Standalone mode — you have full CRUD control over invoices directly in Forge", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Read-only mode — invoices can only be viewed, not created"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Disabled mode — the Invoices page is hidden entirely"}], "explanation": "When no accounting provider is connected, invoices operate in standalone mode with full create, edit, send, void, and delete capabilities. This is the default for shops without external accounting software."}, {"id": "inv2", "text": "You just finished a job for a customer and need to bill them. What is the fastest way to create an invoice?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Invoices, click New Invoice, manually enter all the line items"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Go to Invoices, click the Uninvoiced Jobs button, find the job, and click the Invoice button to auto-generate it", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Go to the Kanban board, right-click the job card, and select Create Invoice"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Go to Sales Orders and convert the order to an invoice"}], "explanation": "The Uninvoiced Jobs panel shows all completed jobs that haven't been invoiced yet. Clicking the Invoice button on a job auto-generates an invoice pre-populated with the customer and job details."}, {"id": "inv3", "text": "An invoice has a status of 'Sent' (blue chip). Which actions are available?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Edit, Void, Delete, and Send"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Void only — you can cancel the invoice but cannot delete or edit it", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Delete only — you can remove the invoice from the system"}, {"id": "d", "text": "No actions — Sent invoices are locked and cannot be changed"}], "explanation": "Sent invoices can be voided (cancelled) but cannot be deleted. The Delete action is only available for Draft invoices. This preserves the audit trail for invoices that have already been sent to customers."}, {"id": "inv4", "text": "What does the yellow 'Partially Paid' status chip indicate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The customer has paid exactly half of the invoice total"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The customer has made at least one payment, but the Balance Due is still greater than zero", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The invoice is pending approval before it can be sent"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The invoice tax calculation is incomplete"}], "explanation": "Partially Paid means one or more payments have been applied to the invoice, but the total amount paid is less than the invoice total. The Balance Due field shows exactly how much remains."}, {"id": "inv5", "text": "You need to create an invoice with 3 line items. When is the Save button enabled?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "As soon as you fill in the customer and dates"}, {"id": "b", "text": "After selecting a customer, filling in dates, setting tax rate, AND adding at least one line item", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "After adding all 3 line items — the form knows you intended 3"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The Save button is always enabled and validates on click"}], "explanation": "The Save button requires: a customer selected, invoice date set, due date set, tax rate >= 0, and at least one line item added. The button is disabled and shows a validation popover on hover until all requirements are met."}, {"id": "inv6", "text": "How is the invoice Total calculated?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Sum of all line item unit prices"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Subtotal (sum of line totals) plus Tax Amount (Subtotal x Tax Rate)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Sum of all line quantities multiplied by a flat rate"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Manually entered by the user in the Total field"}], "explanation": "The Total is computed automatically: Subtotal (sum of Qty x Unit Price for each line) + Tax Amount (Subtotal x Tax Rate %). All three values update live as you add/remove lines or change the tax rate."}, {"id": "inv7", "text": "You see a red Balance Due amount in the invoices table. What does this mean?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The invoice has a negative balance (overpayment)"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The invoice is Overdue — the due date has passed and it hasn't been fully paid", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The invoice was voided and the balance is being refunded"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The tax rate exceeds the maximum allowed percentage"}], "explanation": "The Balance Due column uses red (error) styling when the invoice status is Overdue, meaning the due date has passed with an outstanding balance. Non-overdue invoices with a balance show a warning (yellow) color instead."}, {"id": "inv8", "text": "A customer sends you a mold for production. The completed job appears in Uninvoiced Jobs but the Invoice button is disabled. Why?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The job hasn't been marked as complete yet"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The job has no customer assigned — you must assign a customer to the job before invoicing", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The invoice module is in integrated mode"}, {"id": "d", "text": "There are too many uninvoiced jobs in the queue"}], "explanation": "The Invoice button in the Uninvoiced Jobs panel is disabled when a job has no customer assigned. The tooltip reads 'No customer assigned'. You need to assign a customer to the job first, then the button becomes active."}, {"id": "inv9", "text": "What happens when you Void an invoice?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The invoice is permanently deleted from the database"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The invoice status changes to Voided (gray chip) and it remains in the system for audit purposes with no financial impact", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The invoice is moved to a separate Voided Invoices archive page"}, {"id": "d", "text": "All payments applied to the invoice are automatically refunded"}], "explanation": "Voiding an invoice sets its status to Voided and preserves the record for audit purposes. Forge uses soft deletes — nothing is permanently removed. A confirmation dialog with warn severity appears before voiding."}, {"id": "inv10", "text": "You connected QuickBooks Online to Forge. What happens to the Invoices page?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Nothing changes — invoices work exactly the same"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The page shows a banner indicating invoices are managed by QuickBooks, and invoice creation/editing is disabled (read-only)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The Invoices page is completely hidden from navigation"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Only the New Invoice button is hidden — you can still edit existing invoices"}], "explanation": "When an accounting provider is connected, the Invoices module switches to integrated mode. A cloud_sync banner appears at the top, and all CRUD operations are disabled. Invoices are synced from QuickBooks and displayed read-only."}, {"id": "inv11", "text": "In the invoice detail view, you see clickable blue links for 'Customer', 'Sales Order', and 'Shipment'. What do these links do?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "They copy the entity ID to your clipboard"}, {"id": "b", "text": "They open the respective entity's detail dialog or page, allowing you to navigate the full order-to-cash chain", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "They send an email notification to the related entity's owner"}, {"id": "d", "text": "They export a PDF report for the linked entity"}], "explanation": "These are EntityLink components that provide clickable cross-references. Customer links navigate to the customer detail page. Sales Order and Shipment links open their respective detail dialogs via the ?detail=type:id URL pattern."}, {"id": "inv12", "text": "You want to find all overdue invoices for a specific customer. What is the most efficient approach?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Export all invoices to CSV and filter in a spreadsheet"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Set the Status filter to Overdue, then use the Search box to type the customer name", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Go to the customer's profile page and check the Invoices tab"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Run a report from the Reports module filtered by invoice status"}], "explanation": "The Invoices page has both a Status filter dropdown and a Search box. Both filters work together."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Kanban Board Overview
summary: Comprehensive overview of the Kanban board — track types, stages, job cards, drag-and-drop, real-time sync, and how jobs flow through production.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Kanban Board Overview

The Kanban Board is the operational heart of Forge. Every job in your shop — whether it's a customer production order, an R&D prototype, a maintenance task, or an internal project — lives on the board and moves through stages from left to right as work progresses. The board gives you a real-time, at-a-glance view of everything happening in your shop.

### Track Types

The board is organized by **track types**. Each track type represents a distinct workflow with its own set of stages. Forge ships with four built-in track types:

- **Production** — The primary workflow for customer jobs. Stages follow the quote-to-cash flow: Quote Requested → Quoted (Estimate) → Order Confirmed (Sales Order) → Materials Ordered (PO) → Materials Received → In Production → QC/Review → Shipped (Invoice) → Invoiced/Sent → Payment Received (Payment).
- **R&D/Tooling** — For research, development, and tooling projects. Includes iteration tracking (version count and notes) for prototype cycles.
- **Maintenance** — For equipment maintenance, facility repairs, and upkeep tasks.
- **Other** — A catch-all for anything that doesn't fit the above categories.

Admins can create custom track types with custom stages from the Admin settings. Each track type can have its own stage colors, WIP limits, and irreversible stage flags.

You switch between track types using the **track type buttons** in the page header. The board reloads instantly when you switch, and SignalR automatically joins the correct board group for real-time updates.

### Stages and Columns

Each stage in a track type becomes a **column** on the board. Columns display from left to right in sort order. Each column header shows:

- **Stage name** — The name of the stage.
- **Job count** — How many jobs are currently in that column.
- **WIP limit** — If configured, the maximum recommended jobs for the column. When exceeded, the count turns red as a visual warning.
- **Lock icon** — Appears on irreversible stages. Jobs cannot be moved backward out of an irreversible stage (e.g., once an invoice is created at the 'Shipped' stage, you can't move the job back to 'In Production').
- **Color stripe** — A colored top border matching the stage color for quick visual identification.

Empty columns show an empty state message encouraging you to drag jobs in.

### Job Cards

Each job on the board is represented by a **job card**. Cards are compact but information-dense:

- **Left border color** — Indicates priority: green (Low), blue (Normal), orange (High), red (Urgent).
- **Job number** — Clickable link that opens the full job detail dialog. Auto-generated sequential number (e.g., JOB-0001).
- **Title** — The job's title text.
- **Cover photo** — Optional image displayed above the card content. Uploaded via the detail panel.
- **Hold indicator** — A pause icon appears when the job has active holds, with a tooltip listing hold reasons.
- **Overdue warning** — A yellow warning icon appears when the job's due date has passed.
- **Footer indicators:**
  - Assignee avatar (initials + color)
  - Customer name
  - Billing status icon (invoiced/uninvoiced)
  - Accounting document reference (e.g., QuickBooks invoice number)
  - Disposition icon (if the job has been disposed)
  - Sub-jobs count with tree icon
  - Due date (turns red when overdue)

### Drag-and-Drop

Cards can be **dragged between columns** to move jobs through stages. The move is optimistic — the card moves immediately in the UI, and if the server rejects the move (e.g., attempting to move backward from an irreversible stage), the card snaps back to its original position.

Within a column, you can **reorder cards** by dragging them up or down. Position changes are saved automatically.

When a job moves to the **final stage** of its track type, the system automatically sets the job's completed date. Moving it backward clears the completed date.

If the target stage has an **accounting document type** configured (e.g., Invoice at the 'Shipped' stage), moving a job there automatically enqueues the document for creation in your connected accounting provider.

### Multi-Select and Bulk Actions

Hold **Ctrl** (or **Cmd** on Mac) and click cards to select multiple jobs. A selection count badge and a **bulk action bar** appear at the bottom of the screen. Bulk actions include:

- **Move** — Move all selected jobs to a chosen stage.
- **Assign** — Assign all selected jobs to a team member (or unassign).
- **Priority** — Set priority on all selected jobs.
- **Archive** — Archive all selected jobs (with confirmation dialog).

Click any card without Ctrl to deselect all. Click the X button in the bulk bar to clear the selection.

### Board View vs. Team (Swimlane) View

The board has two view modes, toggled via icons in the header:

- **Board View** (default) — Traditional kanban columns. Best for seeing the flow of work through stages.
- **Team View** — A swimlane grid where each row is a team member and each column is a stage. Cards appear at the intersection of their assignee and current stage. Includes an 'Unassigned' row. You can drag cards between cells to simultaneously change stage and assignee.

### Filtering

- **Team Members** multi-select — Filter the board to show only jobs assigned to specific people.
- **My Work** toggle — Quick filter to show only jobs assigned to you. Persists as a user preference.

### Real-Time Sync (SignalR)

The board uses **SignalR WebSocket** connections for real-time updates. When another user creates a job, moves a card, updates a job, or reorders cards, your board refreshes automatically. No manual refresh needed.

The connection state is monitored — if the WebSocket disconnects, a yellow 'Reconnecting...' banner appears at the top of the page. If it fails to reconnect, the banner turns red.

### Barcode Scanner Integration

If you have a USB barcode scanner connected, scanning a job number barcode while on the kanban page will automatically find and open that job's detail dialog. The scanner context is set to 'kanban' when the page loads.

### Job Detail Dialog

Clicking a job number opens the **full job detail dialog** as a Material Dialog (not a side panel). The dialog is split into two columns:

**Left column (main content):**
- Title and description
- R&D iteration badge (for R&D/Tooling track)
- Disposition info (if disposed)
- Subtasks with checkbox completion
- Sub-jobs list with entity links
- BOM explosion button (if the job has a linked part)
- Linked cards (related jobs, blocking jobs, parent/child relationships)
- Parts list with search and add
- Cost analysis summary
- Operation time analysis
- Activity log, comments, notes, and history

**Right column (sidebar):**
- Status timeline with holds
- Disposition button
- Barcode/QR code info
- Details metadata (priority, assignee with quick-change menu, customer, due date, start date, track type, linked part, parent job)
- File attachments with drag-and-drop upload
- Time entries logged against the job

### Create / Edit Job Dialog

The create dialog collects: Title (required, max 200 chars), Description, Track Type (create only), Customer, Assignee, Priority, and Due Date. The edit dialog is identical but without Track Type (you can't change a job's track type after creation).

Draft auto-save is enabled — if you close the dialog accidentally, your form data is recovered on next visit.

### URL and Sharing

The board supports deep linking via the `?detail=job:{id}` query parameter. When you open a job detail dialog, the URL updates. You can copy-paste this URL to share a direct link to a specific job. Bookmarks and browser back/forward work correctly.", "sections": []}
