Violation message: 'Track Type is required.'"}, {"label": "Backward Move Restriction", "value": "Cannot move a job backward (to a lower sort-order stage) from an irreversible stage. The API returns an error and the UI snaps the card back. Error message: 'Cannot move backward from irreversible stage.'"}, {"label": "Cross-Track Move", "value": "Cannot move a job to a stage that belongs to a different track type. The API rejects such moves with an error."}, {"label": "Assignee Compliance", "value": "When assigning a job, the system checks if the assignee has completed their compliance profile. Users with incomplete profiles show a warning icon (⚠) in the assignee dropdown but can still be assigned."}, {"label": "Disposition (required)", "value": "When disposing a job, the disposition type must be selected. Notes are optional (max 2000 chars)."}, {"label": "Subtask Text", "value": "The add-subtask button is disabled when the input is empty or whitespace-only."}, {"label": "Link Target", "value": "The add-link button is disabled when no target job has been selected from the search results."}], "heading": "Validation Rules"}, {"items": [{"label": "Track Type Switch", "value": "Click a track type button to load that track's board. The board fully reloads and SignalR joins the new board group."}, {"label": "View Mode Toggle", "value": "Board View (view_column icon) or Team View (people icon). Board View is the default column layout. Team View shows a swimlane grid by user."}, {"label": "Drag Card Between Columns", "value": "Moves the job to the target stage. Optimistic UI — card moves immediately, server confirms or rolls back."}, {"label": "Drag Card Within Column", "value": "Reorders the card's position within the stage. Position is saved automatically."}, {"label": "Drag Card in Swimlane", "value": "In Team View, dragging between cells can change both the stage (column) and assignee (row) simultaneously."}, {"label": "Click Job Number", "value": "Opens the full job detail dialog. Updates the URL to ?detail=job:{id} for sharing."}, {"label": "Click Card (no modifier)", "value": "If cards are selected, clears selection. Otherwise opens the job detail dialog."}, {"label": "Edit Job (detail dialog)", "value": "Opens the edit dialog pre-filled with current values. Saves changes and refreshes the board."}, {"label": "Quick-Assign (detail sidebar)", "value": "Click the assignee row in the sidebar to open a dropdown menu. Select a user to reassign immediately without opening the edit dialog."}, {"label": "Add Subtask", "value": "Type in the subtask input and press Enter or click the add button. Subtask appears immediately in the list."}, {"label": "Toggle Subtask", "value": "Click the checkbox on a subtask to toggle its completion status. The text gets strikethrough when completed."}, {"label": "Add Link", "value": "Search for a job in the link search input, select it from results, choose a link type (Related to, Blocks, Parent of), and click add."}, {"label": "Remove Link", "value": "Click the X button on a linked card to remove the relationship."}, {"label": "Add Part", "value": "Search for a part in the part search input, select it from results, and click add. Part appears in the parts list with its quantity."}, {"label": "Remove Part", "value": "Click the X button on a part item to detach it from the job."}, {"label": "Explode BOM", "value": "Click the 'Explode BOM' button on a job with a linked part. Creates child jobs from the bill of materials. Only shown when the job has a part and no existing child jobs."}, {"label": "Start/Stop Timer", "value": "Click the timer button in the detail header to start tracking time for this job. Click again to stop. Time entries appear in the sidebar Time section."}, {"label": "Upload File", "value": "Drag files onto the upload zone in the sidebar, or click to browse. Files upload with progress bars and appear in the file list."}, {"label": "Download File", "value": "Click the download icon on a file item to download it."}, {"label": "Delete File", "value": "Click the red delete icon on a file item to remove it (soft delete — file preserved in storage)."}, {"label": "Dispose Job", "value": "Click the Dispose button in the sidebar. Opens a dialog to select disposition type and add notes. Options: Ship to Customer, Add to Inventory, Capitalize as Asset, Scrap, Hold for Review."}], "heading": "Board and Table Actions"}, {"items": [{"label": "Related to", "value": "General relationship between two jobs. Bidirectional — both jobs show the link. Icon: link."}, {"label": "Blocks", "value": "This job blocks the linked job from progressing. Used for dependency tracking. Icon: block."}, {"label": "Parent of", "value": "This job is the parent of the linked job. Used for hierarchical job structures. Icon: account_tree."}], "heading": "Link Types"}]}

title: Kanban Board Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the Kanban board: track types, stages, job cards, drag-and-drop, bulk actions, WIP limits, detail dialog, and real-time sync.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "kb1", "text": "You need to create a new production job for a customer order. What is the correct sequence?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to the Kanban board, click 'New Job', fill in Title, select Production track type, choose the customer, set priority, and click Save", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Go to Admin → Jobs → Add New and enter all the job details there"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Create a Sales Order first, then the system auto-generates the job"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Go to the Backlog page and drag a card to the board"}], "explanation": "Jobs are created directly from the Kanban board using the 'New Job' button. Fill in the required Title, select the track type (Production for customer orders), and optionally set customer, assignee, priority, and due date. The job number is auto-generated and the job starts in the first stage."}, {"id": "kb2", "text": "A job card on the board has a red left border and a yellow warning triangle. What does this tell you?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The job is Urgent priority and is overdue", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "The job has errors and needs immediate attention from an admin"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The job is on hold and cannot be moved"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The job has been flagged for quality review"}], "explanation": "The left border color indicates priority: red = Urgent. The yellow warning triangle indicates the job's due date has passed (overdue). Together they tell you this is an urgent job that is already late."}, {"id": "kb3", "text": "You try to drag a job card backward from the 'Shipped' column, but it snaps back to its original position. Why?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The 'Shipped' stage is marked as irreversible because an accounting document (invoice) was created at that stage", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "You don't have permission to move cards on this board"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The job is locked because it has a customer assigned"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Backward moves are never allowed on the kanban board"}], "explanation": "Stages can be marked as irreversible (shown with a lock icon). The 'Shipped' stage typically creates an invoice, making it irreversible — you can't undo the accounting document. The card snaps back to its position with an error. Backward moves are allowed from non-irreversible stages."}, {"id": "kb4", "text": "You want to reassign 8 jobs from one team member to another as quickly as possible. What is the most efficient method?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Ctrl+Click all 8 job cards, then use the Assign button in the bulk action bar to select the new assignee", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Open each job's detail dialog one at a time and change the assignee"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Use the Team View, create a filter for the old assignee, then drag each card to the new assignee's row one by one"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Go to Admin → Users → Reassign Jobs and enter the new assignee"}], "explanation": "Multi-select with Ctrl+Click lets you select multiple cards, then the bulk action bar's Assign button reassigns all of them in one operation. This is the fastest way to bulk-reassign jobs."}, {"id": "kb5", "text": "A column header shows '7 / 5' in red. What does this mean and what should you do?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The column has 7 jobs but the WIP limit is 5 — the stage is overloaded and you should move some jobs forward or redistribute work", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "7 jobs out of 5 have been completed in this stage — the stage is performing well"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The column has 5 jobs but the maximum allowed is 7 — you have room for 2 more"}, {"id": "d", "text": "7 jobs failed validation and 5 are blocked — contact an admin"}], "explanation": "WIP (Work-In-Progress) limits are advisory thresholds set per-stage. When exceeded (7 jobs vs. 5 limit), the count turns red as a visual warning that the stage is overloaded. This indicates a bottleneck — consider moving jobs forward, rebalancing work, or addressing blockers."}, {"id": "kb6", "text": "What happens when you move a job to the final stage of its track type?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The system automatically sets the job's completed date to the current timestamp", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "The job is permanently archived and removed from the board"}, {"id": "c", "text": "An invoice is automatically generated and sent to the customer"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Nothing special — it's treated the same as any other stage move"}], "explanation": "When a job reaches the final stage, the system sets its CompletedDate to the current UTC timestamp. The job remains on the board (it's not archived automatically). If the job is later moved backward from the final stage, the completed date is cleared."}, {"id": "kb7", "text": "You're viewing the Kanban board and another team member creates a new job. How does your board update?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The board updates automatically in real-time via SignalR WebSocket — the new card appears without you needing to refresh", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "You need to manually click a Refresh button to see the new job"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The board polls the server every 30 seconds and updates on the next cycle"}, {"id": "d", "text": "You need to navigate away and back to the kanban page to see the update"}], "explanation": "The Kanban board uses SignalR WebSocket connections for real-time sync. When any user creates a job, moves a card, updates a job, or reorders cards, a boardHub event fires and all connected clients automatically reload the board data."}, {"id": "kb8", "text": "What is the difference between Board View and Team View?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Board View shows columns by stage with cards stacked vertically; Team View shows a swimlane grid where rows are team members and columns are stages", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Board View is for managers and Team View is for individual contributors — they show different data based on your role"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Board View shows all jobs and Team View only shows your assigned jobs"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Board View is the live board and Team View is a planning/forecast view"}], "explanation": "Board View is the traditional kanban column layout. Team View adds a user dimension — each row represents a team member (plus an Unassigned row), and cards appear at the intersection of their assignee and stage. You can drag cards in both views, and in Team View, dragging between cells can change both stage and assignee simultaneously."}, {"id": "kb9", "text": "You open a job detail dialog and want to quickly reassign the job without opening the edit dialog. How?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Click the assignee row in the sidebar Details section to open a dropdown menu, then select the new assignee", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "You must click the Edit button and change the assignee in the edit dialog"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Drag the job card to a different row in Team View"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Right-click the job card and select 'Reassign' from the context menu"}], "explanation": "The assignee row in the detail sidebar is clickable and opens a mat-menu dropdown with all team members and an 'Unassign' option. Selecting a user reassigns the job immediately without needing to open the full edit dialog."}, {"id": "kb10", "text": "A job has a linked part and no child jobs. What does the 'Explode BOM' button do?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Creates child jobs from the part's bill of materials — each BOM line becomes a separate child job on the same track type", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Breaks the job into smaller subtasks based on the part's operations"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Generates a purchase order for all the materials needed for the part"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Opens a 3D view of the part's assembly structure"}], "explanation": "BOM Explosion takes the bill of materials from the job's linked part and creates a child job for each BOM line. This is useful for complex assemblies where each component needs its own production tracking. The button only appears when the job has a linked part and no existing child jobs."}, {"id": "kb11", "text": "You accidentally closed the Create Job dialog after filling in several fields. What happens to your form data?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The dialog has draft auto-save — your form data is saved to IndexedDB and recovered with a banner the next time you open the dialog", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "All data is lost and you need to re-enter everything"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The data is temporarily held in memory and available if you click 'New Job' again within 60 seconds"}, {"id": "d", "text": "A confirmation dialog prevents you from closing if the form has unsaved data"}], "explanation": "The create/edit job dialogs use the draft auto-save system. Form state is automatically saved to IndexedDB every 2.5 seconds. If you close accidentally (or the browser crashes), the next time you open the dialog a 'Recovered unsaved changes' banner appears, and your form data is restored."}, {"id": "kb12", "text": "How do you share a direct link to a specific job with a coworker?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Open the job detail dialog — the URL updates to include ?detail=job:{id}. Copy the URL from the browser address bar and share it.", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Click the Share button in the job detail dialog to generate a shareable link"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Right-click the job card and select 'Copy Link'"}, {"id": "d", "text": "You can only share job numbers — there are no direct links to specific jobs"}], "explanation": "When you open a job detail dialog, the URL automatically updates to include ?detail=job:{id} (e.g., /kanban?detail=job:1055). Anyone who opens this URL will land on the kanban board with that job's detail dialog already open. This works with bookmarks and browser history too."}, {"id": "kb13", "text": "You scan a barcode with a USB scanner while on the Kanban board. What happens?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The system searches for a job matching the scanned job number and, if found, automatically opens its detail dialog with a success snackbar", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "The scanned value is entered into the team members filter field"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Nothing — barcode scanning only works on the Inventory and Parts pages"}, {"id": "d", "text": "A new job is created with the scanned value as the title"}], "explanation": "The Kanban page sets the scanner context to 'kanban'. When a barcode is scanned, the system searches all jobs on the current board for a matching job number. If found, it opens the detail dialog and shows a success snackbar. If not found, it shows an error snackbar."}, {"id": "kb14", "text": "What are the three types of job links you can create between cards?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Related to (general relationship), Blocks (dependency), and Parent of (hierarchy)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Duplicate, Predecessor, and Successor"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Depends on, Required by, and Same as"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Before, After, and Parallel"}], "explanation": "Forge supports three link types: 'Related to' for general relationships between jobs, 'Blocks' for dependency tracking (this job blocks the linked job), and 'Parent of' for hierarchical structures (this job is the parent of the linked job)."}, {"id": "kb15", "text": "When you move a job card to a stage that has an accounting document type configured (e.g., Invoice), what happens automatically?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "If an accounting provider is connected and the job has a customer with an external ID, the system enqueues the document (e.g., invoice) for creation in the accounting system", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "An invoice is immediately created and sent to the customer via email"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The job card turns green to indicate it has been invoiced"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Nothing automatic — you must manually create the invoice from the Sales Orders page"}], "explanation": "When a stage has an AccountingDocumentType configured, moving a job there triggers an enqueue operation. The system checks: (1) is an accounting provider connected, (2) does the job have a customer, and (3) does the customer have an external ID (linked to the accounting system). If all conditions are met, the document creation is queued for processing."}, {"id": "kb16", "text": "You see a pause icon (⏸) in a job card's header. What does this indicate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The job has one or more active holds — hover over the icon to see the hold reasons in a tooltip", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "The job's timer is currently paused"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The job is waiting for customer approval"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The job is in a paused/maintenance stage and cannot be moved"}], "explanation": "The pause circle icon appears on job cards that have active holds. Holds are set via the Status Timeline in the detail dialog (e.g., 'Waiting for Materials', 'Customer Review'). Hovering over the icon shows a tooltip listing all active hold reasons."}, {"id": "kb17", "text": "In Team View, you drag a card from Alice's row in the 'In Production' column to Bob's row in the 'QC/Review' column. What happens?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The job is both moved to the QC/Review stage and reassigned from Alice to Bob simultaneously", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Only the stage changes to QC/Review — the assignee stays as Alice"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Only the assignee changes to Bob — the stage stays at In Production"}, {"id": "d", "text": "An error occurs — you can only change one attribute at a time"}], "explanation": "In Team View, each cell represents the intersection of a user (row) and stage (column). Dragging between cells can change both the stage and the assignee in a single drag operation. The system sends two separate API calls — one for the stage move and one for the assignee change."}, {"id": "kb18", "text": "You want to see only your assigned jobs on the board and have this preference persist across sessions. Which approach is correct?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Click the 'My Work' toggle button in the header — it filters to your jobs and saves the preference so it persists when you return", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Use the Team Members filter and select only yourself — but you'll need to reselect each time you visit"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Go to Account → Settings → Default Board Filter and set it to 'My Jobs Only'"}, {"id": "d", "text": "There is no way to persist board filters — you must set them each time"}], "explanation": "The 'My Work' toggle button filters the board to show only jobs assigned to you and saves this preference to your user preferences store. The setting persists across sessions and is also reflected in the URL as ?myWork=true. The Team Members filter works too but does not persist."}, {"id": "kb19", "text": "What are the five disposition types when disposing a completed job?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Ship to Customer, Add to Inventory, Capitalize as Asset, Scrap, and Hold for Review", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Complete, Cancel, Return, Rework, and Archive"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Deliver, Store, Transfer, Dispose, and Recycle"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Accept, Reject, Rework, Quarantine, and Ship"}], "explanation": "Job disposition determines what happens to the job's output: Ship to Customer (deliver the product), Add to Inventory (stock the finished goods), Capitalize as Asset (register as a tooling/equipment asset), Scrap (discard as waste), or Hold for Review (keep for further evaluation). Each has different downstream effects."}, {"id": "kb20", "text": "The SignalR connection banner at the top of the page turns yellow and says 'Reconnecting...'. What is happening and what should you do?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The WebSocket connection to the server was interrupted and is automatically attempting to reconnect — wait a few seconds, and the board will resume real-time updates once reconnected", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "The server is shutting down for maintenance — save your work and close the application"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Your session has expired — you need to log in again"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Another user has taken control of the board — you are now in read-only mode"}], "explanation": "The ConnectionBannerComponent monitors the SignalR connection state. Yellow 'Reconnecting...' means the WebSocket dropped (network blip, server restart, etc.) and the client is automatically attempting to reconnect with exponential backoff. Usually it reconnects within seconds. If it fails completely, the banner turns red ('Disconnected'). The board continues to work but won't receive real-time updates until reconnected."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 10, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Leads Overview
summary: What leads are, how the sales pipeline works, lead statuses, converting leads to customers, and how leads connect to quotes and jobs.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Leads Overview

The Leads module is your sales pipeline. It tracks potential customers from first contact through qualification and quoting to conversion or loss. Every lead represents a company that might become a customer — and every customer relationship starts here.

### What Is a Lead?

A lead is a prospective customer record. It captures the company name, contact person, email, phone, how they found you (source), and any notes about the opportunity. Leads move through a defined pipeline of statuses as you work them.

### The Sales Pipeline

Leads progress through five statuses:

1. **New** — A fresh lead that hasn't been contacted yet. This is the default status when you create a lead.
2. **Contacted** — You've reached out to the prospect. They know who you are and what you offer.
3. **Quoting** — The prospect has expressed interest and you're preparing or have sent a quote/estimate.
4. **Converted** — The lead became a customer. A Customer record is created automatically when you convert.
5. **Lost** — The opportunity didn't work out. You can record a reason (competitor won, budget, timing, etc.) for future analysis.

Leads can move forward or backward through New, Contacted, and Quoting freely. Converted and Lost are terminal states, though a Lost lead can be reopened back to New.

### Two Ways to View Your Pipeline

The Leads page offers two view modes:

- **Table View** — A sortable, filterable data table showing all leads with columns for company, contact, source, status, follow-up date, and created date. Best for searching and bulk review.
- **Pipeline View** — A kanban-style board with columns for each status. Drag and drop cards between columns to update status. Best for visual pipeline management.

Switch between views using the toggle buttons in the page header. Your preference is saved.

### Follow-Up Dates

Each lead can have a follow-up date. When the follow-up date passes without the lead being updated, the date displays in red (overdue) as a visual reminder. Use follow-up dates to ensure no lead falls through the cracks.

### Lead Sources

The Source field tracks how leads find you — referral, website, trade show, cold call, etc. Sources are configured in Admin → Reference Data (group: lead_source), so your team can add new sources without code changes. Tracking sources helps you understand which marketing channels produce results.

### Converting Leads to Customers

When a lead is ready to become a customer, you have two conversion options:

- **Convert to Customer** — Creates a Customer record from the lead's company name, contact, email, and phone. The lead status changes to Converted.
- **Convert to Customer + Create Job** — Same as above, plus creates a new Job on the default kanban board linked to the new customer. Use this when you're ready to start work immediately.

Conversion is a one-way action. Once converted, the lead shows a green confirmation badge and links to the customer record. You cannot convert a Lost lead — reopen it first.

### How Leads Connect to Quotes and Jobs

The typical flow is: Lead → Customer → Quote/Estimate → Sales Order → Job. Converting a lead creates the Customer record. From there, you can create Estimates (ballpark figures) or Quotes (binding, line-itemized) for that customer. When a quote is accepted, it converts to a Sales Order, which can generate Jobs on the kanban board.

### Marking a Lead as Lost

When you mark a lead as Lost (either by clicking the Lost status button or dragging the card to the Lost column in pipeline view), a dialog appears asking for the reason. The lost reason is optional but valuable — it helps you identify patterns (price too high, competitor won, bad timing) and improve your win rate over time.

### Reopening Lost Leads

Circumstances change. A Lost lead can be reopened by clicking the 'Reopen Lead' button in the detail view. This resets the status to New so you can work the opportunity again.", "sections": []}

title: Leads Field Reference
summary: Complete reference for every field, button, status, validation rule, and action in the Leads module.
contentJson: {"title": "Leads Field Reference", "groups": [{"items": [{"label": "Company Name (required)", "value": "Text input, identifies the prospect company. Must not be empty. This becomes the Customer name if the lead is converted. data-testid: lead-company-name"}, {"label": "Contact Name", "value": "Text input, optional. The person at the company you're working with. If provided, a Contact record is created when the lead is converted (first/last name split on space). data-testid: lead-contact-name"}, {"label": "Email", "value": "Text input, optional. Free-form notes about the lead, opportunity details, conversation history, or requirements. data-testid: lead-notes"}], "heading": "Lead Form Fields"}, {"items": [{"label": "New (primary/blue chip)", "value": "Default status for new leads. title: Multi-Factor Authentication Overview
summary: What MFA is, how TOTP authenticator apps work, recovery codes, and admin-enforced policies.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

Multi-factor authentication adds an extra layer of security to your Forge account. Instead of relying on your password alone, MFA requires a second verification step — a time-based code from an authenticator app — every time you sign in.

### What Is TOTP?

TOTP stands for Time-based One-Time Password. When you enable MFA, Forge generates a secret key that you register with an authenticator app on your phone. The app uses this key plus the current time to produce a 6-digit code that changes every 30 seconds. When you sign in, you enter your password first, then the current code from your app. Because the code changes constantly and requires your physical device, a stolen password alone is not enough to access your account.

### Supported Authenticator Apps

Any TOTP-compatible authenticator app works with Forge:
- **Google Authenticator** — simple, widely used
- **Authy** — supports cloud backup and multi-device sync
- **Microsoft Authenticator** — integrates with Microsoft accounts
- **1Password / Bitwarden** — password managers with built-in TOTP support

During setup, you scan a QR code with your chosen app, or manually enter the secret key if scanning is not possible.

### Recovery Codes

Recovery codes are one-time-use backup codes that let you sign in if you lose access to your authenticator app (lost phone, factory reset, app deleted). Forge generates a set of codes when you enable MFA, and you can regenerate them at any time from Account > Security.

**Important rules about recovery codes:**
- Each code can only be used once — after use it is permanently consumed
- Generating new codes invalidates all previous codes
- Store them somewhere safe and separate from your phone (printed, in a password manager, or in a secure document)
- The Security page shows how many unused codes you have remaining

### The Login Flow with MFA

1. Enter your email and password as usual
2. If MFA is enabled, you see a challenge screen asking for your 6-digit code
3. Open your authenticator app and enter the current code
4. Optionally check "Remember this device" to skip MFA on trusted devices
5. If you cannot access your authenticator, click "Use recovery code" and enter one of your backup codes

### Admin-Enforced MFA Policies

Administrators can require MFA for specific roles (e.g., Admin, Manager). When a policy is enforced:
- Users in the affected roles see a warning on their Security page: "Required by organization policy"
- The Disable MFA button is hidden — you cannot turn off MFA while the policy applies to your role
- New users assigned to an enforced role must set up MFA on their next login
- The Admin > MFA panel shows a compliance table listing all users, their MFA status, device type, and whether their role is enforced

### When to Use MFA

- **Always recommended** for all users to protect against password breaches
- **Required** when your admin has enforced an MFA policy for your role
- **Especially important** for Admin and Manager roles that have access to sensitive data, user management, and system settings
- **Critical** if you access Forge from shared or public computers", "sections": []}

title: Notifications Overview
summary: What notifications are, how real-time push works, notification types, severity levels, and email delivery.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Notifications Overview

The Notifications system keeps you informed of important events across Forge in real time. Notifications are delivered instantly via SignalR push and optionally by email, ensuring you never miss a critical update — whether you're at your desk or away from the application.

### How Real-Time Push Works

When an event occurs (a job is assigned to you, an expense is overdue, a teammate mentions you), the server creates a notification and pushes it to your browser instantly via SignalR WebSocket. You see the bell icon badge update in the header without refreshing. If your connection drops temporarily, the system catches up when the panel reopens by reloading from the API.

### Notification Types

Notifications are categorized by what triggered them:
- **Assignment** — a job, task, or responsibility was assigned to you
- **Overdue** — a job, task, or deadline has passed its due date
- **Expense** — an expense requires your approval or was approved/rejected
- **Maintenance** — an asset status changed or maintenance is due
- **System** — application-level events (sync completed, backup finished, scheduled task ran)
- **Message** — a direct message or chat room message was sent to you
- **Mention** — someone @mentioned you in a comment or activity log

### Severity Levels

Each notification carries a severity that controls its visual presentation and email behavior:
- **Info** (blue) — informational updates that don't require immediate action. Examples: job moved to next stage, expense approved, sync completed.
- **Warning** (yellow) — something needs attention soon. Examples: job approaching due date, asset nearing tool life expectancy, expense pending approval for 3+ days.
- **Critical** (red) — requires immediate attention. Examples: job overdue, system error, asset out of service, failed sync.

### Email Delivery

Notifications can be delivered by email in addition to the in-app push. Email delivery is controlled per category in your notification preferences:
- **Critical notifications** — email enabled by default (recommended to leave on)
- **Assignment notifications** — email enabled by default
- **Mention notifications** — email enabled by default

Emails are sent via SMTP and include a direct link back to the relevant entity in Forge. You can enable or disable each email category independently on the Notifications page under the Preferences tab.

### The Bell Icon

The notification bell in the application header shows a badge with your unread count. Clicking it opens the notification panel — a slide-out overlay with tabs for All, Messages, and Alerts. Dismissed notifications are hidden from the panel but preserved in the system for audit purposes.

### Sound Notifications

A subtle audio alert can play when a new notification arrives. This is enabled by default and can be toggled off in Preferences. Sound is especially useful on shop floor displays where you might not be watching the screen constantly.", "sections": []}

contentJson: {"steps": [{"element": ".app-header__actions", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Header Actions", "description": "The notification bell, chat, and other controls are in the header actions area. The bell icon shows a red badge with your unread count. Click it to open the notification panel, which slides out with tabs for All, Messages, and Alerts."}}, {"element": ".user-trigger", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "User Menu", "description": "Access your profile, account settings, and notification preferences from the user menu. Notification preferences let you control email delivery per category and toggle sound alerts."}}], "appRoute": "/dashboard", "startButtonLabel": "Tour Notifications"}

title: Notifications Field Reference
summary: Complete reference for notification fields, severity levels, types, panel controls, preferences, actions, and filtering.
contentJson: {"title": "Notifications Field Reference", "groups": [{"items": [{"label": "Title", "value": "Short summary of the notification event. Displayed in bold when unread. Examples: 'Job #1042 assigned to you', 'Expense approved'."}, {"label": "Message", "value": "Detailed description of the notification event. Provides context beyond the title. Examples: 'Hartman, Daniel J assigned Job #1042 — CNC Bracket Assembly to you', 'Your $245.00 travel expense was approved by Smith, Jane A'."}, {"label": "Type", "value": "Categorizes what triggered the notification. Values: assignment, overdue, expense, maintenance, system, message, mention. Used for filtering and routing."}, {"label": "Severity", "value": "Visual priority level. Values: info (blue — informational), warning (yellow — needs attention), critical (red — immediate action required). Controls left border color and email delivery defaults."}, {"label": "Source", "value": "Where the notification originated. Values: user (from another person — messages, mentions, assignments) or system (automated — overdue alerts, sync events, maintenance warnings)."}, {"label": "Is Read", "value": "Boolean flag. Unread notifications show bold title text and contribute to the bell badge count. Marking as read removes the bold styling and decrements the badge."}, {"label": "Is Pinned", "value": "Boolean flag. Pinned notifications always sort to the top of the list regardless of date. Useful for keeping important items visible. Toggle on/off per notification."}, {"label": "Is Dismissed", "value": "Boolean flag. Dismissed notifications are hidden from the panel but preserved in the database. They do not appear in any tab or filter view. Cannot be undone from the panel."}, {"label": "Entity Type", "value": "Optional. The type of entity this notification relates to (e.g., job, expense, asset, part). Enables click-to-navigate from the notification to the source entity."}, {"label": "Entity ID", "value": "Optional. The ID of the related entity. Combined with Entity Type, creates a navigable link in the notification panel."}, {"label": "Sender Initials", "value": "Optional. Two-letter initials of the user who triggered the notification (for user-sourced notifications). Displayed in the avatar circle."}, {"label": "Sender Color", "value": "Optional. Background color of the sender's avatar circle. Matches the user's assigned color in the system."}, {"label": "Created At", "value": "Timestamp when the notification was created. Displayed as relative time in the panel (e.g., '5 min ago', '2 hours ago') and as MM/dd/yyyy hh:mm in the full notifications table."}], "heading": "Notification Fields"}, {"items": [{"label": "Info (blue border)", "value": "Informational updates. No immediate action needed. Examples: job moved to next stage, expense approved, file uploaded, sync completed. Default email: OFF."}, {"label": "Warning (yellow border)", "value": "Attention needed soon. Examples: job approaching due date, asset nearing tool life, pending expense approval for 3+ days. Default email: OFF."}, {"label": "Critical (red border)", "value": "Immediate attention required. Examples: job overdue, system error, asset out of service, failed integration sync, security alert. Default email: ON."}], "heading": "Severity Levels"}, {"items": [{"label": "Assignment", "value": "Triggered when a job, task, or responsibility is assigned to the user. Source: user. Default email: ON."}, {"label": "Overdue", "value": "Triggered when a job, task, or deadline passes its due date. Source: system. Severity is typically warning or critical."}, {"label": "Expense", "value": "Triggered for expense approval requests, approvals, and rejections. Source: system or user depending on the action."}, {"label": "Maintenance", "value": "Triggered when an asset's status changes to Maintenance or Out of Service, or when scheduled maintenance is due. Source: system."}, {"label": "System", "value": "Application-level events: backup completion, sync status, scheduled task results, configuration changes. Source: system."}, {"label": "Message", "value": "Triggered when a direct message or chat room message is received. Source: user. Shows sender avatar."}, {"label": "Mention", "value": "Triggered when another user @mentions you in a comment, activity log, or chat message. Source: user. Default email: ON."}], "heading": "Notification Types"}, {"items": [{"label": "All", "value": "Shows every non-dismissed notification regardless of source. Default tab when the panel opens."}, {"label": "Messages", "value": "Filters to user-sourced notifications only. Shows DMs, chat messages, mentions, and assignment notifications from other users."}, {"label": "Alerts", "value": "Filters to system-sourced notifications only. Shows overdue warnings, maintenance alerts, sync events, and automated system notifications."}], "heading": "Panel Tabs"}, {"items": [{"label": "Mark as Read (per item)", "value": "Click a notification to mark it as read. Bold title becomes normal weight. Unread badge decrements by one. Optimistic UI — updates locally, syncs to server."}, {"label": "Pin / Unpin (per item)", "value": "Toggle pin on a notification to keep it at the top of the list. Pinned items sort before unpinned regardless of date. Useful for tracking ongoing issues."}, {"label": "Dismiss (per item)", "value": "Hide a single notification from the panel. The notification is flagged as dismissed and excluded from all views. Preserved in the database for audit."}, {"label": "Mark All Read (bulk)", "value": "Marks every notification as read in one action. Badge count drops to zero. Affects all notifications, not just the current tab filter."}, {"label": "Dismiss All (bulk)", "value": "Dismisses every visible notification. Clears the panel. Dismissed items are hidden but preserved."}], "heading": "Panel Actions"}, {"items": [{"label": "Email on Critical", "value": "Toggle. When ON, critical-severity notifications are also sent via SMTP email. Default: ON. Found on Notifications page → Preferences tab."}, {"label": "Email on Assignment", "value": "Toggle. When ON, assignment notifications are sent via email. Default: ON. Useful for staying informed when away from the app."}, {"label": "Email on Mention", "value": "Toggle. When ON, @mention notifications are sent via email. Default: ON. Ensures you see mentions even when not actively using Forge."}, {"label": "Sound Enabled", "value": "Toggle. When ON, plays an audio alert when a new notification arrives via SignalR. Default: ON. Can be turned off for quiet environments."}], "heading": "Notification Preferences"}, {"items": [{"label": "Search", "value": "Text filter across notification title and message fields. Filters in real time as you type."}, {"label": "Severity Filter", "value": "Dropdown: All, Info, Warning, Critical. Filters the table to show only notifications of the selected severity."}, {"label": "Source Filter", "value": "Dropdown: All, System, Board, Timer. Filters by the notification source."}], "heading": "Filtering (Notifications Page)"}, {"items": [{"label": "Title", "value": "Sortable. The notification's short summary text."}, {"label": "Message", "value": "Sortable. The detailed notification message."}, {"label": "Severity", "value": "Sortable, filterable (enum). Color-coded: info, warning, critical."}, {"label": "Source", "value": "Sortable. Whether the notification came from a user or the system."}, {"label": "Date", "value": "Sortable, date type. When the notification was created. Width: 150px."}], "heading": "Table Columns (Notifications Page)"}, {"items": [{"label": "Hub endpoint", "value": "/hubs/notifications — WebSocket connection authenticated via JWT query string parameter."}, {"label": "Event: notificationReceived", "value": "Pushed from server when a new notification is created. The client's NotificationHubService receives it and calls NotificationService.push() to add it to the in-memory list."}, {"label": "Connection recovery", "value": "When the panel opens, it reloads notifications from the API to catch any missed during disconnection. The connection banner shows yellow for reconnecting, red for disconnected."}, {"label": "Auto-connect", "value": "NotificationHub connects automatically on app init (after authentication) in AppComponent.ngOnInit(). No manual setup needed per page."}], "heading": "SignalR Real-Time"}]}

title: Notifications Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the notification system: types, severity, panel actions, preferences, and real-time delivery.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "notif1", "text": "You see a red badge with the number 3 on the bell icon in the header. What does this mean?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "There are 3 critical-severity notifications waiting"}, {"id": "b", "text": "There are 3 unread, non-dismissed notifications across all types and severities", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "There are 3 new notifications that arrived in the last hour"}, {"id": "d", "text": "There are 3 pinned notifications in your panel"}], "explanation": "The bell badge shows the count of all unread, non-dismissed notifications regardless of type or severity. It updates in real time via SignalR as new notifications arrive or existing ones are marked read."}, {"id": "notif2", "text": "You want to see only system-generated alerts (overdue warnings, sync events) and hide messages from other users. Which panel tab do you select?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "All — then manually scan for system notifications"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Messages — system alerts are classified as messages"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Alerts — this tab filters to system-sourced notifications only", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "There is no way to filter by source in the panel"}], "explanation": "The Alerts tab filters to system-sourced notifications only. The Messages tab shows user-sourced notifications (DMs, mentions, assignments from people). The All tab shows everything."}, {"id": "notif3", "text": "A notification has a yellow left border in the panel. What severity level does this indicate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Info — general informational update"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Warning — something needs attention soon", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Critical — immediate action required"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Pinned — the notification has been pinned to the top"}], "explanation": "Yellow border indicates Warning severity — something needs your attention soon but is not yet critical. Blue is Info (informational), Red is Critical (immediate action). Border color is not related to pin status."}, {"id": "notif4", "text": "You dismiss all notifications using the Dismiss All button. Can these notifications be recovered?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "No — dismissed notifications are permanently deleted from the system"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Yes — dismissed notifications are hidden from the panel but preserved in the database for audit purposes", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Only if you undo within 10 seconds using the snackbar action"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Only admins can recover dismissed notifications from the trash"}], "explanation": "Dismissed notifications are flagged as dismissed and hidden from the panel, but they are never deleted. The data is preserved in the database for audit and compliance purposes. However, there is no UI to un-dismiss from the panel."}, {"id": "notif5", "text": "You want an important notification to stay at the top of your panel regardless of when it was created. How do you accomplish this?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Mark it as critical severity to force it to the top"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Pin the notification — pinned items always sort above unpinned ones", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Star the notification using the star icon"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Move it to the Messages tab where it will be prioritized"}], "explanation": "Pinning a notification keeps it at the top of the list. Pinned notifications sort before all unpinned notifications regardless of their creation date. You can toggle pin on and off for any notification."}, {"id": "notif6", "text": "Which notification preference is enabled by default to ensure you receive emails for the most urgent events?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Email on all notifications regardless of severity"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Email on Critical notifications", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Email on Warning and Critical notifications"}, {"id": "d", "text": "No email preferences are enabled by default"}], "explanation": "Email on Critical is enabled by default because critical notifications indicate events requiring immediate attention (overdue jobs, system errors, asset failures). Email on Assignment and Email on Mention are also ON by default. General info and warning notifications do not trigger emails by default."}, {"id": "notif7", "text": "You are working on the shop floor and not looking at the screen. How does Forge alert you when a new notification arrives?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The screen flashes red for critical notifications"}, {"id": "b", "text": "An audio alert plays when a notification arrives via SignalR, if the Sound Enabled preference is on", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The application sends an SMS to your registered phone number"}, {"id": "d", "text": "There is no way to receive alerts without watching the screen"}], "explanation": "When Sound Enabled is toggled ON in notification preferences (the default), an audio alert plays each time a new notification is pushed via SignalR. This is especially useful on shop floor kiosk displays. You can disable it in the Preferences tab if you prefer a quiet environment."}, {"id": "notif8", "text": "Your browser lost its WebSocket connection to the server for 2 minutes. When the connection recovers, how does Forge ensure you haven't missed any notifications?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The SignalR client automatically replays all missed events from a server-side buffer"}, {"id": "b", "text": "When the notification panel opens, it reloads the full notification list from the API to catch any missed during disconnection", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Missed notifications are permanently lost and must be checked via email"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The system sends a batch email with all missed notifications after reconnection"}], "explanation": "Every time the notification panel opens, it makes a fresh API call to reload notifications. This catches any notifications that were created while the WebSocket was disconnected. The connection banner also shows a visual indicator (yellow for reconnecting, red for disconnected) so you know the real-time feed is interrupted."}, {"id": "notif9", "text": "A colleague @mentions you in a job's activity log comment. What type and source will the resulting notification have?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Type: mention, Source: user", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Type: message, Source: system"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Type: assignment, Source: user"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Type: system, Source: user"}], "explanation": "An @mention from another user creates a notification with type 'mention' and source 'user'. This means it appears under the Messages tab (user-sourced), and if your Email on Mention preference is enabled, you'll also receive an email."}, {"id": "notif10", "text": "You click Mark All Read in the notification panel. Which of the following is true?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Only notifications in the currently selected tab are marked as read"}, {"id": "b", "text": "All notifications are marked as read, the badge count drops to zero, and the change syncs to the server in the background", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "All notifications are dismissed and removed from the panel"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Only unread notifications from the last 24 hours are marked as read"}], "explanation": "Mark All Read is a bulk action that marks every notification as read (not just those in the current tab). The bell badge count drops to zero immediately via optimistic UI, and the change is persisted to the server via an API call in the background. Notifications remain visible in the panel — they are not dismissed."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Welcome to Forge
summary: Get oriented with Forge — what it does, how it's organized, and what you'll use every day.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Welcome to Forge

Forge is a manufacturing operations platform built for small engineering shops. It connects every part of your workflow — from quoting a job to shipping it — in one unified system. You'll use it every day for tracking work, logging time, submitting expenses, and staying compliant.

### What It Does

At its core, Forge manages jobs on a kanban board. Every job moves through stages from Quote Requested all the way to Payment Received, matching how work actually flows through a machine shop. Along the way it integrates with QuickBooks Online to keep your financials in sync automatically.

Beyond jobs, Forge handles parts catalogs (including BOMs), inventory, purchase orders, sales orders, shipments, and quality inspections. Everything is connected — a job links to a part, which links to its BOM, which links to the vendor POs.

### How It's Organized

The left sidebar is your primary navigation. Icons expand to labeled sections when you hover. The main areas you'll use:

- **Dashboard** — your daily summary: open jobs, tasks, timers, cycle progress
- **Board** — the kanban board for all active jobs
- **Time Tracking** — start/stop timers and review your logged hours
- **Account** — your profile, compliance forms, pay stubs, and security settings

### What to Do Next

Start by completing the remaining modules in this onboarding path. They'll walk you through setting up your profile, completing required compliance forms, logging time, and submitting expenses. The whole path takes about 25 minutes.

After onboarding, your manager will assign additional training paths based on your role.", "sections": []}

title: Setting Up Your Profile
summary: How to complete your contact info, emergency contact, and account security settings.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Setting Up Your Profile

Your employee profile is how the system knows who you are, where to reach you, and how to pay you. Completing it accurately is important — your work location drives which state withholding forms you'll need to fill out.

### Profile Section

Navigate to **Account → Profile**. Fill in your first and last name, then confirm your work location. If you work at a location other than the company's default, update this now — it affects your state tax withholding.

### Contact Information

Go to **Account → Contact**. Enter your personal phone number and home address. This is kept confidential and is only used for HR and payroll purposes.

### Emergency Contact

Under **Account → Emergency**, add the name, phone number, and relationship of someone to contact in an emergency. This is required as part of your onboarding.

### Security Settings

Visit **Account → Security** to review your login settings. If your employer uses badge/PIN kiosk login, you'll use the 4–6 digit PIN you were assigned. You can change this PIN here at any time.

### Why It Matters

Your profile drives your compliance form requirements. An incomplete work location means the system can't determine which state withholding form to show you. An incomplete address or emergency contact will show a yellow warning on your profile completeness tracker.", "sections": []}

title: Onboarding Knowledge Check
summary: A short quiz to confirm you're ready to work in Forge.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "ob1", "text": "Where do you go to start a work timer for a specific job?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The Reports page — it tracks all job activity"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The job card on the Kanban Board or the Time Tracking page", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The Admin panel under Time Settings"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The Backlog — select the job and click Start"}], "explanation": "Timers can be started from the job card on the Kanban Board or from the Time Tracking page. Both give you a running clock tied to that specific job."}, {"id": "ob2", "text": "You forgot to start your timer this morning. How do you record those hours?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Ask your manager to add them to your timesheet"}, {"id": "b", "text": "You can't — only real-time timers are allowed"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Add a manual time entry in the Time Tracking page", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Edit the job description to note the time worked"}], "explanation": "The Time Tracking page supports manual entries for cases when you forgot to start a timer. Enter the date, job, and duration directly."}, {"id": "ob3", "text": "Which section shows all your required compliance forms (W-4, I-9, etc.)?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Account → Tax Forms", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Admin → Compliance Templates"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Dashboard → Getting Started"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Reports → Employee Documents"}], "explanation": "All personal tax and compliance forms live under Account → Tax Forms. You'll see your submission status and any forms that still need to be completed."}, {"id": "ob4", "text": "You need to submit a $45 fuel expense for a job site visit. What should you do?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Email your manager the receipt and they'll enter it"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Go to Expenses, click New Expense, fill in the details, upload your receipt, and submit", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Add a note to the job card with the dollar amount"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Log it as billable time since you were traveling"}], "explanation": "All expense reimbursements go through the Expenses page. Upload your receipt, select the category, link it to a job if applicable, and submit for approval."}, {"id": "ob5", "text": "Where do you update your emergency contact information?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Account → Emergency", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Admin → Users → your profile"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Dashboard → Profile card"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Account → Security"}], "explanation": "Emergency contacts are managed under Account → Emergency. Keep this up to date — it's required for onboarding completion."}, {"id": "ob6", "text": "You want to see how complete your employee profile is. Where do you look?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Dashboard → Profile Completeness widget", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Admin → Users → your account"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Reports → Employee Status"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Account → Security → Profile Score"}], "explanation": "The Dashboard shows a Profile Completeness widget that lists exactly which items are still missing. It disappears once you've finished all required sections."}, {"id": "ob7", "text": "You want to change your login password. Where do you go?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Admin → Users — your admin can reset it"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Account → Security", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Dashboard → Settings → Password"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Account → Profile → Edit"}], "explanation": "Your password and authentication settings are under Account → Security. You can update your password there at any time without contacting an admin."}, {"id": "ob8", "text": "A notification badge appears on the bell icon in the header. What does that indicate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "You have a new message in the Chat feature"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Your session is about to expire"}, {"id": "c", "text": "You have unread notifications from the system or your teammates", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "A compliance form is overdue — the badge always means that"}], "explanation": "The bell badge shows the count of unread notifications. Click it to open the notification panel where you can see, dismiss, and pin individual notifications."}, {"id": "ob9", "text": "You need to attach a PDF drawing to a job. Where in the job is the best place to do this?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Job detail panel → Files section (drag and drop or click to browse)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Parts Catalog → attach it to the related part instead"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Reports → upload to a saved report"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Admin → Document Storage"}], "explanation": "The job detail panel has a Files section where you can attach PDFs, images, CAD files, and other documents directly to the job record."}, {"id": "ob10", "text": "What happens to a job when it is archived?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "It is permanently deleted from the system"}, {"id": "b", "text": "It moves to a separate archive database that admins can access"}, {"id": "c", "text": "It is removed from the board but preserved in the system for reporting and traceability", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "It is locked and no further changes can be made"}], "explanation": "Jobs are never deleted in Forge — they're archived. An archived job disappears from the board but remains fully searchable and visible in the Backlog with the Archived filter."}, {"id": "ob11", "text": "Where would you find jobs assigned to you that are due today?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Reports → My Jobs Due Today"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Dashboard → Today's Tasks widget", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Backlog → filter by assignee and due date"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Kanban Board → scroll to find your cards"}], "explanation": "The Today's Tasks widget on the Dashboard shows all active jobs assigned to you, sorted by due date, with overdue jobs highlighted."}, {"id": "ob12", "text": "You started a timer on the wrong job. What should you do?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Let it run — you can't stop a timer once started"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Stop the timer, then manually edit the time entry to correct the job", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Ask your manager to delete it"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Archive the job and start again"}], "explanation": "Stop the running timer, then go to Time Tracking to edit the entry — you can correct the linked job, adjust the duration, or delete it entirely."}, {"id": "ob13", "text": "Which page shows all open purchase orders for materials your team is waiting on?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Inventory → Pending Receipts"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Backlog → filter by status Materials Ordered"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Purchase Orders page", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Dashboard → Open Orders widget shows all POs"}], "explanation": "The Purchase Orders page lists all POs with their status (Draft, Sent, Partially Received, Received). You can see what's outstanding and when deliveries are expected."}, {"id": "ob14", "text": "You want to send a message to a coworker about a specific job without leaving the app. What do you use?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Email them from your regular email client"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Add a note to the job card and @mention them", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Update the job description and hope they notice"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Use the Reports page to flag the job for review"}], "explanation": "Typing a comment in the job's activity feed and @mentioning a teammate sends them an in-app notification. They can reply directly on the job card."}, {"id": "ob15", "text": "Your W-4 was recently updated and you need to re-submit it. Where do you go?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Admin → Compliance → re-upload"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Account → Tax Forms — find the W-4 and click to complete it again", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Reports → Employee → Tax Documents"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Contact payroll — you cannot self-submit"}], "explanation": "Account → Tax Forms lets you view and re-submit compliance forms like the W-4 whenever they need to be updated. Your submission history is also visible there."}, {"id": "ob16", "text": "The Kanban board shows a column with a red header. What does this indicate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The column contains at least one overdue job"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The column has been locked by an admin"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The job count in that column has exceeded the WIP limit", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "The column contains jobs with Critical priority"}], "explanation": "Column headers turn red when the number of jobs exceeds the configured WIP (Work In Progress) limit. It's a visual signal to finish work before adding more to that stage."}, {"id": "ob17", "text": "You want to see everything that happened to a specific job — who moved it, what changed, what notes were added. Where is this?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Reports → Job History"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Job detail panel → Activity tab", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Admin → Audit Log — filter by job"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Backlog → job row → expand"}], "explanation": "Every job has a full activity timeline in its detail panel. It shows stage moves, field edits, file uploads, comments, @mentions, and timer starts/stops in chronological order."}, {"id": "ob18", "text": "Where do you go to view your recent pay stubs?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Dashboard → Payroll widget"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Admin → Payroll → your records"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Account → Pay Stubs", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Reports → Payroll → filter by employee"}], "explanation": "Your personal pay stubs are available under Account → Pay Stubs. Admins upload pay stubs there and you receive a notification when a new one is available."}, {"id": "ob19", "text": "What is the correct way to log time on a job you finished yesterday but forgot to track?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "You cannot log time for a previous date — only today"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Ask your manager to enter it in the system for you"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Open Time Tracking, click New Entry, set yesterday's date, and enter the duration", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Add a comment to the job explaining the hours worked"}], "explanation": "Manual time entries support any past date. title: Parts Catalog Overview
summary: What parts are, how types and statuses work, Bills of Materials, operations routing, and how parts connect to jobs, inventory, and purchase orders.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Parts Catalog Overview

The Parts Catalog is the central register for every item your shop manufactures, purchases, or stocks. Each part record captures the full engineering definition — what the part is, what it is made from, how it is built, and where it lives in inventory. Parts connect to nearly every other module in Forge: jobs consume parts, inventory tracks their quantities, purchase orders procure them, sales orders sell them, and operations define how to produce them.

### What Is a Part?

A part is any discrete item that your business tracks. This includes finished goods you manufacture, raw materials you buy, assemblies you build from sub-components, consumables used during production, fasteners, electronics, packaging materials, and tooling. Every part gets a system-generated part number and a human-readable description.

### Part Types

Each part is assigned a type that describes its role in your manufacturing process:

- **Part** — A standard manufactured or purchased component. This is the default type for most items.
- **Assembly** — A part that is built from other parts. Assemblies have a Bill of Materials (BOM) listing their sub-components. The Assembly type uses a tree icon (account_tree) in the UI to distinguish it visually.
- **Raw Material** — Base materials purchased in bulk (sheet metal, bar stock, plastic resin, lumber). Typically bought by weight or length and consumed during production.
- **Consumable** — Items used up during manufacturing but not part of the finished product (cutting fluid, sandpaper, welding gas, gloves).
- **Tooling** — Molds, dies, jigs, fixtures, and cutting tools. Tooling parts can be linked to a tooling asset in the Assets module for shot count and maintenance tracking.
- **Fastener** — Screws, bolts, nuts, rivets, clips. Separated from generic parts for BOM clarity and purchasing convenience.
- **Electronic** — Circuit boards, sensors, wiring harnesses, connectors. Useful when your products include electronic sub-assemblies.
- **Packaging** — Boxes, foam inserts, labels, shrink wrap. Tracked separately so packaging costs don't get mixed into material costs.

### Part Statuses

Every part moves through a lifecycle tracked by its status:

- **Draft** — The part record exists but is not ready for use. Engineers can fill in details without the part appearing in production workflows or purchase orders.
- **Prototype** — The part is in development or testing. It can be used in jobs and inventory but is flagged as not yet finalized.
- **Active** — The part is fully defined and approved for production, purchasing, and sales. This is the default status for new parts.
- **Obsolete** — The part is no longer manufactured or purchased. The record is preserved for historical traceability (past jobs, old BOMs, audit trail), but it won't appear in active searches or new orders.

You can change a part's status at any time from the detail panel using the status buttons at the bottom of the Info tab.

### Revisions

Parts support revision tracking. The default revision is 'A'. When a part's design changes, you increment the revision (A → B → C). The revision history is accessible from the detail panel, showing who changed what and when. Revisions let you trace which version of a part was used in a specific job.

### Bill of Materials (BOM)

The BOM tab on a part's detail panel lists every component needed to build that part. Each BOM line specifies:

- **Child Part** — The component part (selected via entity picker search)
- **Quantity** — How many of the child part are needed per parent part
- **Source Type** — How the component is obtained:
  - **Make** — Manufactured in-house (triggers a sub-job)
  - **Buy** — Purchased from a vendor (triggers a purchase order)
  - **Stock** — Pulled from existing inventory (no procurement needed)
- **Reference Designator** — An engineering reference (e.g., R1, C4, J2 for electronics) identifying where the component goes in the assembly
- **Lead Time Days** — How many days it takes to obtain this component. Critical for production planning.
- **Notes** — Free-form notes about the BOM line

BOM lines can be viewed in two modes: a flat **table view** with sortable columns and per-column filtering, or a hierarchical **tree view** that shows nested sub-assemblies visually.

The **Used In** tab shows the reverse relationship — which parent assemblies use this part as a component. Clicking a parent part navigates to its detail panel.

### Operations (Routing)

The Routing tab (also called Process tab) defines the step-by-step manufacturing process for a part. Each operation represents a production step:

- **Step Number** — The sequence order (1, 2, 3...)
- **Title** — A short name for the operation (e.g., 'CNC Rough Cut', 'Deburr', 'Anodize')
- **Instructions** — Detailed work instructions for the operator
- **Work Center** — Which work center or machine area performs this step
- **Estimated Minutes** — How long the operation should take
- **QC Checkpoint** — Whether this step requires a quality inspection before proceeding
- **QC Criteria** — What to inspect if it is a QC checkpoint
- **Referenced Operation** — A cross-reference to another operation in the same routing (for dependencies or sub-operations)

Operations can also have **materials** assigned to them — BOM lines that are consumed during that specific operation step. This connects the BOM to the routing, showing exactly which materials are needed at each production step.

Operations support their own file attachments (setup photos, drawings, reference docs) and an activity log with comments for collaboration.

The routing can be viewed in **list mode** (sequential cards) or **flow mode** (visual flow diagram).

### How Parts Connect to Other Modules

**Jobs:** When a job is created on the Kanban board, it can be linked to a part. The part's BOM drives material requirements, and the part's routing defines the production steps for the job.

**Inventory:** Parts are tracked in inventory storage locations and bins. The part detail panel shows an inventory summary with total quantity on hand and a low-stock warning when quantity drops below the minimum stock threshold.

**Purchase Orders:** When a BOM line has source type 'Buy', the system knows the component needs to be purchased. Parts with a preferred vendor link streamline PO creation.

**Sales Orders & Quotes:** Parts appear as line items on sales orders and quotes with pricing from the part's price history.

**Assets:** Tooling-type parts can be linked to a tooling asset for shot count tracking and maintenance scheduling.

**Barcode/QR:** Every part can have a barcode label printed for quick identification. Scanning a part's barcode on the parts page searches for and selects that part instantly.

### Inventory Thresholds & Replenishment

The part form includes inventory management fields:

- **Min Stock Threshold** — The minimum quantity to keep on hand. When inventory drops below this level, a low-stock warning appears.
- **Reorder Point** — The quantity level at which a reorder should be triggered.
- **Reorder Quantity** — How many to order when reordering.
- **Lead Time Days** — Standard lead time for procurement.
- **Safety Stock Days** — Extra buffer days of stock to maintain.

These fields feed into inventory planning and the low-stock warning indicator on the part detail panel.

### Pricing

Each part can have a list price with effective dates. The Info tab shows the current price and a history of past prices. You can set a new price at any time — the system automatically marks the previous price as historical. Price history tracks the effective date range and any notes.

### Accounting Integration

When an accounting provider (e.g., QuickBooks) is connected, parts can be linked to accounting items. This synchronizes the part record with the accounting system's item catalog. You can link and unlink accounting items from the Info tab.

### View Modes

The parts list page supports two view modes:
- **Table View** — A data table with sortable columns (Part #, External Part #, Description, Rev, Type, Status, Material, BOM count). Click column headers to sort. Use the gear icon for column visibility and ordering.
- **Card Grid View** — A visual card layout showing part thumbnails and key details. Useful for browsing parts visually.

Toggle between views using the view mode buttons next to the filters. Your preference is remembered across sessions.", "sections": []}
