title: Quality Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the Quality module: inspections, templates, lots, SPC, NCRs, CAPAs, ECOs, gages, and scanner integration.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "qc1", "text": "You need to inspect incoming material for a specific part that has a standard checklist. What is the most efficient workflow?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Quality → Inspections tab, click New Inspection, select the QC template for that part, enter the lot number, and work through the pre-populated checklist items", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Create a new QC template from scratch each time you perform an inspection"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Go to the Parts module and record inspection results in the part notes field"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Skip the inspection and mark the lot as accepted in Inventory"}], "explanation": "QC templates define reusable checklists. When creating an inspection, selecting a template pre-populates all the checklist items. Enter the lot number for traceability, and record pass/fail results for each check."}, {"id": "qc2", "text": "An inspection has a results summary showing '4/6'. What does this mean?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "4 out of 6 inspectors approved the part"}, {"id": "b", "text": "4 out of 6 individual checklist checks passed", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The part scored 4 out of 6 on a quality rating scale"}, {"id": "d", "text": "4 of 6 lots in the batch were inspected"}], "explanation": "The results summary shows how many individual checklist items passed out of the total. '4/6' means 4 checks passed and 2 failed. This gives a quick at-a-glance view of inspection quality."}, {"id": "qc3", "text": "A customer reports a defect in parts from lot L-2024-0042. You need to find every job, PO, and bin location that used material from this lot. Where do you look?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Quality → Lots tab → search for L-2024-0042 → click the traceability button (tree icon) to see all linked jobs, production runs, POs, bin locations, and inspections", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Run a report in the Reports module filtered by lot number"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Check the Inventory module for bin contents matching that lot"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Search for the lot number in the Kanban board search"}], "explanation": "The Lot Tracking tab has a traceability button per row (tree icon) that opens a dialog showing every linked record: jobs, production runs, purchase orders, bin locations, and QC inspections. This is the fastest way to trace a lot through your operation."}, {"id": "qc4", "text": "You are monitoring a critical dimension on a CNC-machined part and notice the SPC chart shows a point beyond the upper control limit (UCL). What should you expect from the system?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Nothing — the chart is informational only and does not trigger any alerts"}, {"id": "b", "text": "An out-of-control (OOC) event is automatically generated in the OOC Events tab, with severity based on the rule violated. If the characteristic has 'Notify on OOC' enabled, a notification is also sent.", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The system automatically stops the CNC machine via IoT integration"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The part status is automatically changed to Obsolete"}], "explanation": "When SPC detects an out-of-control condition (like a point beyond 3-sigma), it creates an OOC event with the appropriate severity. If the characteristic has 'Notify on OOC' enabled, relevant personnel receive a notification. The OOC event can then be acknowledged and a CAPA created if needed."}, {"id": "qc5", "text": "A batch of supplier-provided material fails incoming inspection. You document this as an NCR. What NCR type and detection stage should you select?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Type: Internal, Detection Stage: In Process"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Type: Customer, Detection Stage: Receiving"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Type: Supplier, Detection Stage: Receiving", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Type: Supplier, Detection Stage: Final Inspection"}], "explanation": "Since the defect is in supplier-provided material, the NCR type is 'Supplier'. Since it was caught during incoming material inspection, the detection stage is 'Receiving'. This combination accurately categorizes the non-conformance for reporting and vendor quality tracking."}, {"id": "qc6", "text": "An NCR has been investigated and the quality team decides the non-conforming parts can be reworked to meet specification. What is the correct disposition code?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Use As Is"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Rework", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Sort & Screen"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Concession"}], "explanation": "'Rework' means the non-conforming material will be repaired or reworked to bring it into conformance with the specification. 'Use As Is' would accept it without changes, 'Sort & Screen' would separate good from bad, and 'Concession' would require customer approval."}, {"id": "qc7", "text": "A recurring quality issue has been identified through multiple NCRs. You need to perform a root cause analysis and track corrective actions to closure. Which feature should you use?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Create an ECO to document the engineering change"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Create a CAPA with type Corrective, link it to the NCRs, perform root cause analysis (e.g., 5 Whys or Fishbone), document corrective actions, and track through verification and effectiveness check", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Add notes to each NCR describing the corrective action taken"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Create a job on the Kanban board to track the fix"}], "explanation": "CAPAs are designed for systematic problem resolution. Create a Corrective CAPA, link it to the source NCRs, use a root cause method (5 Whys, Fishbone, Fault Tree, or 8D), document the corrective and preventive actions, then track through verification and effectiveness checking."}, {"id": "qc8", "text": "A design change to a part requires updating the BOM, revising the drawing, and modifying the CNC program. Which Quality feature manages this controlled change?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Create an NCR to document the required changes"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Create an ECO with change type 'Revision', list the affected items (part, BOM, operations) with old and new values, and advance through Draft → Review → Approved → Implementation → Implemented", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Update the part record directly in the Parts module"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Create a CAPA to track the design change"}], "explanation": "Engineering Change Orders (ECOs) manage controlled changes to parts, processes, and documentation. Create an ECO with the appropriate change type, list all affected items with their old and new values, get approval through the review process, and track implementation status."}, {"id": "qc9", "text": "A digital caliper needs its annual calibration. How do you manage this in the Quality module?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Quality → Gages tab, find the caliper, click it to open the detail, then click 'Record Calibration' and enter the date, result, lab name, standards used, and as-found/as-left conditions", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Update the caliper's status to 'In Service' and change the last calibration date manually"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Create a maintenance work order in the Assets module"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Record the calibration as a time entry in Time Tracking"}], "explanation": "The Gages tab provides a complete calibration management workflow. Open the gage detail, record a new calibration with the result (Pass/Fail/Adjusted/Out of Tolerance), lab name, certificate, standards used, and as-found/as-left conditions. The system automatically updates the next calibration due date."}, {"id": "qc10", "text": "You scan a barcode while on the Quality page's Lots tab. What happens?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Nothing — the scanner is not active on the Quality page"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The scanned value fills the lot search field, letting you quickly find the scanned lot", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "A new inspection is automatically created for the scanned lot"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The system opens the traceability dialog for the scanned lot"}], "explanation": "The Quality page sets the scanner context to 'quality'. On the Lots tab, a barcode scan fills the lot search field with the scanned value. On the Inspections tab, it fills the inspection search field instead. This enables quick lookups on the shop floor."}, {"id": "qc11", "text": "What is the difference between control limits (UCL/LCL) and spec limits (USL/LSL) on an SPC chart?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "They are the same thing — just different naming conventions"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Control limits are calculated from actual process data and show natural variation boundaries. Spec limits are defined by engineering requirements and show the acceptable range for the product.", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Control limits are set by management, spec limits are set by customers"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Spec limits are always wider than control limits"}], "explanation": "Control limits (UCL/LCL) are statistically calculated from measurement data — they show the expected range of natural process variation (typically +/- 3 sigma from the mean). Spec limits (USL/LSL) are engineering requirements defined on the SPC characteristic — they define what the customer or design requires. A process can be in control (within UCL/LCL) but still out of spec, or vice versa."}, {"id": "qc12", "text": "You need to create a QC template for a specific part that includes 5 required checks and 2 optional reference checks. How do you configure the optional checks?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Create two separate templates — one for required checks and one for optional"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Set the IsRequired toggle to OFF for the 2 reference checks. They will still appear during inspection but won't block the inspection from being finalized.", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Add the optional checks as notes in the template description field"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Optional checks are not supported — all items in a template are always required"}], "explanation": "Each checklist item in a QC template has an IsRequired toggle. When set to OFF, the check still appears during inspection for reference but does not prevent the inspection from being completed. This is useful for advisory measurements or secondary characteristics that are good to record but not critical."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Quotes Overview
summary: What quotes are, how they differ from estimates, line item structure, lifecycle statuses, and conversion to sales orders.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Quotes Overview

The Quotes module manages binding fixed-price commitments to your customers. A quote is a formal document that lists specific parts, quantities, and unit prices — once accepted by the customer, it becomes the contractual basis for a sales order.

### Quotes vs Estimates

Forge has two distinct pricing documents, both stored in the same underlying table but serving very different purposes:

- **Estimates** are non-binding ballpark figures. They have a single lump-sum amount, no line items, and no formal acceptance workflow. Estimates live on the customer detail page under the Estimates tab.
- **Quotes** are binding fixed-price commitments. They are line-itemized (each line specifies a part, quantity, and unit price), have a formal lifecycle (Draft → Sent → Accepted → Converted to Order), and can be converted into a sales order.

An estimate can be converted into a quote when the customer wants to move forward. The resulting quote retains a link back to the source estimate for traceability.

### Line Items

Every quote must have at least one line item. Each line specifies:
- **Part** — selected from your parts catalog via autocomplete search
- **Description** — auto-filled from the part record
- **Quantity** — how many units
- **Unit Price** — defaults to the part's list price if one is configured; can be overridden
- **Line Total** — calculated as Quantity × Unit Price

The quote's financial summary is computed from its lines:
- **Subtotal** — sum of all line totals
- **Tax** — subtotal multiplied by the tax rate (auto-filled from the customer's state; editable)
- **Grand Total** — subtotal plus tax

### Quote Lifecycle

Quotes follow a defined status progression:

1. **Draft** (gray chip) — Initial state. The quote can be edited, lines added or removed, and deleted if no longer needed.
2. **Sent** (blue chip) — The quote has been sent to the customer. Set via the Send action button. Records the sent date.
3. **Accepted** (green chip) — The customer has accepted the quote. Set via the Accept action button. Records the accepted date.
4. **Declined** (red chip) — The customer declined the quote. Set via the Reject action button with a confirmation dialog.
5. **Expired** (yellow chip) — The quote passed its expiration date without being accepted.
6. **Converted to Order** (purple/primary chip) — The accepted quote has been converted into a sales order. The quote links to the resulting sales order.

### Status Transition Rules

- **Send** is available only when status is Draft
- **Accept** and **Reject** are available only when status is Sent
- **Convert to Order** is available only when status is Accepted
- **Delete** is available only when status is Draft (soft delete)
- Quotes cannot move backward in the lifecycle

### Conversion to Sales Order

When you convert an accepted quote, the system:
1. Creates a new sales order with lines matching the quote's line items
2. Links the quote to the new sales order (visible in the quote detail as "Linked Order")
3. Updates the quote status to Converted to Order
4. The sales order inherits the customer, shipping address, and line item details

### Tax Rate Auto-Fill

When you select a customer on the quote form, the system looks up the applicable sales tax rate based on the customer's state. The tax rate field is auto-filled with this value (shown with a badge like "CA 7.25%"). You can manually override the rate if needed.

### When to Use Quotes

- When a customer requests formal pricing with specific parts and quantities
- When you need a binding price commitment before creating a sales order
- When converting an estimate into a detailed line-itemized proposal
- When you need to track the acceptance/rejection history of pricing proposals", "sections": []}

title: Quotes Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the Quotes module: lifecycle, line items, statuses, conversion, tax auto-fill, and common workflows.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "qt1", "text": "A customer asks for a rough ballpark on a project before committing to specifics. Which document type should you create?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "A Quote with a single line item for the estimated total"}, {"id": "b", "text": "An Estimate — a non-binding ballpark figure with a single lump-sum amount", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "A Sales Order with a 'Pending' status"}, {"id": "d", "text": "A Quote in Draft status with no line items"}], "explanation": "Estimates are non-binding ballpark figures with a single amount. Quotes are binding, line-itemized commitments. Use an Estimate first, then convert it to a Quote when the customer wants detailed pricing."}, {"id": "qt2", "text": "What is the minimum requirement to save a new quote?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "A customer must be selected and at least one line item must be added", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Only a customer selection is required — line items can be added later"}, {"id": "c", "text": "A customer, expiration date, and at least two line items"}, {"id": "d", "text": "A quote number must be manually entered along with the customer"}], "explanation": "The save button is disabled until a customer is selected and at least one line item exists. The validation popover shows 'At least one line item is required' if no lines have been added. Quote numbers are auto-generated."}, {"id": "qt3", "text": "You select 'Acme Corp' as the customer and notice the tax rate auto-fills to 7.25% with a badge showing 'CA 7.25%'. What happened?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The system looked up the sales tax rate for California (the customer's state) and auto-filled it", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "7.25% is the default tax rate for all customers"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The tax rate was copied from Acme Corp's last quote"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The rate was pulled from the federal tax table"}], "explanation": "When you select a customer, the system looks up the applicable sales tax rate based on the customer's state. The badge (e.g., 'CA 7.25%') confirms the source. You can manually override the rate if needed."}, {"id": "qt4", "text": "When adding a line item, you select a part and see a 'LIST' badge appear next to the unit price. What does this indicate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The price was auto-filled from the part's list/default price and can be overridden", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "The price is locked and cannot be changed"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The part is on a special price list for this customer"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The price includes a volume discount"}], "explanation": "When you select a part, the unit price auto-fills from the part's default/list price. The 'LIST' badge indicates this is the catalog price. Manually editing the price clears the badge — you can always override the default."}, {"id": "qt5", "text": "A quote is currently in 'Sent' status. Which actions are available?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Accept and Reject only", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Send, Accept, Reject, and Delete"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Accept, Reject, Convert to Order, and Delete"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Only Convert to Order"}], "explanation": "When a quote is Sent, only Accept and Reject are available. Send is only for Draft quotes. Convert to Order requires Accepted status. Delete is only available for Draft quotes."}, {"id": "qt6", "text": "What happens when you click 'Convert to Order' on an accepted quote?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The system creates a new sales order with lines matching the quote, links the quote to the order, and updates the quote status to Converted to Order", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "The quote is renamed to a sales order — same record, different label"}, {"id": "c", "text": "A blank sales order is created and you must manually copy the line items"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The quote is archived and a notification is sent to create a sales order manually"}], "explanation": "Converting creates a new sales order with lines matching the quote's line items, inheriting the customer and details. The quote links to the new sales order (visible as 'Linked Order' in the detail) and its status changes to Converted to Order."}, {"id": "qt7", "text": "You want to delete a quote that has already been sent to the customer. Can you?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Yes — any quote can be deleted at any time"}, {"id": "b", "text": "No — only Draft quotes can be deleted. Sent, Accepted, Declined, and Expired quotes cannot be deleted.", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Yes, but only if the customer hasn't responded within 30 days"}, {"id": "d", "text": "No — quotes can never be deleted, only archived"}], "explanation": "The Delete action is only available when the quote status is Draft. Once a quote has been sent, it becomes part of the audit trail and cannot be deleted. This ensures you maintain a complete record of all pricing communications."}, {"id": "qt8", "text": "Which chip color represents a quote that has been declined by the customer?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Yellow (warning)"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Gray (muted)"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Red (error)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Blue (info)"}], "explanation": "Declined quotes show a red chip (chip--error). The full color mapping: Draft = gray, Sent = blue, Accepted = green, Declined = red, Expired = yellow, Converted to Order = purple/primary."}, {"id": "qt9", "text": "An estimate was converted to a quote. Where can you see the link back to the original estimate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "In the quote detail dialog — the sourceEstimateId field links back to the originating estimate", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "There is no link — estimates and quotes are completely separate"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Only in the database — the UI doesn't show estimate-to-quote links"}, {"id": "d", "text": "In the customer's activity log only"}], "explanation": "When an estimate is converted to a quote, the resulting quote stores a sourceEstimateId that links back to the originating estimate. This provides full traceability of the pricing history from initial ballpark to formal commitment."}, {"id": "qt10", "text": "How is the grand total on a quote calculated?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "It is manually entered by the user"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Subtotal (sum of all line totals) plus Tax (subtotal × tax rate percentage)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Sum of all line quantities multiplied by a single unit price"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The total from the linked estimate plus a markup percentage"}], "explanation": "The grand total is computed: Subtotal = sum of (Quantity × Unit Price) for each line. Tax = Subtotal × Tax Rate %. Grand Total = Subtotal + Tax. All three values are displayed in the summary section."}, {"id": "qt11", "text": "You're on the quotes list page and want to see only quotes that have been accepted but not yet converted. How do you filter?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Use the status filter dropdown and select 'Accepted'", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Type 'accepted' in the search box"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Click the column header to sort by status, then scroll to the Accepted section"}, {"id": "d", "text": "There is no way to filter by status — you must scan the full list"}], "explanation": "The status filter dropdown on the quotes list page lets you filter by any single status. Select 'Accepted' to see only accepted quotes that haven't been converted yet. The table also supports column-level status filtering via the column header filter."}, {"id": "qt12", "text": "What columns are visible in the quotes data table by default?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Quote #, Customer, Status, Lines, Total, Expires, and Created", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Quote #, Customer, and Total only"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Quote #, Customer, Status, Assigned To, Priority, and Due Date"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Quote #, Description, Amount, Tax, and Grand Total"}], "explanation": "The quotes table shows seven columns: Quote # (monospace), Customer name, Status (color-coded chip), Lines count (center-aligned), Total (currency, right-aligned), Expires (date), and Created (date). All columns are sortable. You can manage column visibility via the gear icon."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Reports Overview
summary: What the report builder does, entity sources, pre-built templates, chart visualizations, and export options.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Reports Overview

The Reports module is the central analytics hub for Forge. It provides two complementary systems: a library of 27 pre-built report templates covering common operational questions, and a dynamic Report Builder that lets you query any of 28 entity sources with 350+ fields to construct custom reports from scratch.

### Three Report Sections

The Reports page is organized into three sections, accessible via the top navigation:

1. **Pre-Built Reports** — The default landing page. A left sidebar lists 27 ready-made reports organized by domain (production, financial, personal, quality, etc.). Click any report to load its chart and data table. Reports that depend on a date range (like Time by User or Revenue) show date pickers in the header.

2. **Report Builder** — A dynamic query tool. Select an entity source (e.g., Jobs, Parts, Invoices), pick columns, add filters, choose grouping and sorting, then run the query. Results display in a data table with optional chart visualization. Save your configurations as named reports to reuse later.

3. **Sankey Flow Reports** — Visual flow diagrams showing how data moves through your business: Quote-to-Cash, Job Stage Flow, Material-to-Product, Worker Orders, Expense Flow, Vendor Supply Chain, Quality Rejection, Inventory Location, Customer Revenue, and Training Completion.

### Pre-Built Report Templates (27)

The pre-built reports cover every major domain:

**Production & Operations:**
- **Jobs by Stage** — Bar chart showing job counts per kanban stage
- **Overdue Jobs** — Table of jobs past their due date
- **Job Completion Trend** — Line chart of jobs completed over time
- **On-Time Delivery** — Percentage of jobs delivered on or before due date
- **Average Lead Time** — Average days from job creation to completion by track type
- **Time in Stage** — How long jobs spend in each stage
- **Job Margin** — Revenue vs. cost analysis per job
- **Cycle Review** — Planning cycle performance summary
- **R&D Report** — R&D/tooling track activity and outcomes

**People & Time:**
- **Time by User** — Hours logged per team member (date range required)
- **Team Workload** — Current job assignments per user
- **Employee Productivity** — Output metrics per employee
- **My Work History** — Personal job completion history
- **My Time Log** — Personal time entries
- **My Cycle Summary** — Personal planning cycle performance
- **My Expense History** — Personal expense submissions

**Sales & Financial:**
- **Expense Summary** — Spending by category
- **Lead Pipeline** — Lead counts by status
- **Customer Activity** — Customer engagement metrics
- **AR Aging** — Outstanding receivables by aging bucket
- **Revenue** — Revenue over time
- **Profit & Loss** — Simple income vs. expenses
- **Quote-to-Close** — Quote conversion rates and timelines
- **Lead-to-Sales** — Lead conversion funnel

**Logistics & Quality:**
- **Shipping Summary** — Shipment volume and carrier usage
- **Inventory Levels** — Current stock quantities
- **Maintenance** — Asset maintenance activity and costs
- **Quality/Scrap** — Scrap rates and quality inspection results

### The Dynamic Report Builder

The Report Builder gives you full control over what data you query and how it is presented.

**Step 1 — Choose a Data Source:** Select from 28 entity sources. Each source exposes a different set of fields. Sources include: Jobs, Parts, Customers, Expenses, Time Entries, Invoices, Leads, Assets, Purchase Orders, Sales Orders, Quotes, Shipments, Inventory (Bin Contents), Payments, Vendors, Production Runs, Lot Records, QC Inspections, Maintenance Schedules, Maintenance Logs, Downtime Logs, Customer Returns, Bin Movements, Planning Cycles, Invoice Lines, Sales Order Lines, Purchase Order Lines, and Quote Lines.

**Step 2 — Select Columns:** Check the fields you want included in your results. Each entity source has 10–35 fields available including related entity fields (e.g., Customer Name on a Job, Vendor Name on a PO).

**Step 3 — Add Filters:** Build filter conditions using field + operator + value. Operators include Equals, NotEquals, Contains, StartsWith, GreaterThan, LessThan, GreaterThanOrEqual, LessThanOrEqual, Between, IsNull, IsNotNull, and In.

**Step 4 — Group & Sort:** Optionally group results by any groupable field (typically enums, dates, and category fields). Sort by any sortable field, ascending or descending.

**Step 5 — Visualize:** Choose a chart type: Bar, Line, Pie, Doughnut, or Table (data only). For chart visualizations, select a label field (the axis/segment labels) and a value field (must be numeric — the measurement).

**Run and Save:** Click Run Report to execute the query. Results appear in a data table below the configuration panel. Save your configuration as a named report with an optional description and a shared/private toggle. Saved reports appear in the toolbar dropdown for quick access.

### Chart Visualizations

Both pre-built reports and the Report Builder use ng2-charts (Chart.js) for visualization:

- **Bar charts** — Used for categorical comparisons (jobs by stage, time by user, expenses by category)
- **Line charts** — Used for trends over time (completion trend, revenue, lead time)
- **Pie charts** — Used for proportional breakdowns (lead pipeline, expense distribution)
- **Doughnut charts** — Similar to pie, used for status distributions
- **Sankey diagrams** — Flow visualizations showing data movement between stages

Charts support drill-down: click a bar or slice to filter the data table to that segment. Click again or use the breadcrumb to return to the full view.

### Export Options

- **CSV Export** — Click the CSV button after running a report to download results as a comma-separated file. Available in the Report Builder.
- **PDF Export** — Server-side PDF generation via QuestPDF for formatted report output.
- **Print** — Browser print styling hides navigation and interactive controls, showing only the report content.

### Roles and Access

The Reports module is available to Admin, Manager, Office Manager, and PM roles. Engineers and Production Workers do not have direct access to the full reports page, though personal reports (My Work History, My Time Log, My Expense History) are available through their respective feature pages.

### Saved Reports

Saved reports store the complete configuration: entity source, selected columns, filters, grouping, sorting, chart type, and chart field mappings. Reports can be marked as shared (visible to all users with report access) or private (visible only to the creator). Saved reports are loaded from the toolbar dropdown and can be updated or deleted.", "sections": []}

title: Reports Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the Reports module: pre-built reports, the report builder, entity sources, filters, charts, and export.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "rpt1", "text": "You want to see how many jobs are currently in each kanban stage. Which pre-built report should you use?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Jobs by Stage", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Job Completion Trend"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Time in Stage"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Team Workload"}], "explanation": "Jobs by Stage shows a bar chart of job counts per kanban stage. Job Completion Trend shows completions over time, Time in Stage shows duration spent in each stage, and Team Workload shows assignments per user."}, {"id": "rpt2", "text": "In the Report Builder, what is the minimum configuration needed to run a report?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Select an entity source and choose at least one column", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Select an entity source, add at least one filter, and choose a chart type"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Select an entity source only — columns are auto-selected"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Load a saved report — you cannot run an ad-hoc query"}], "explanation": "The Run Report button is enabled once you select an entity source and check at least one column. Filters, grouping, sorting, and chart type are all optional."}, {"id": "rpt3", "text": "You need to find all invoices sent to a specific customer in Q1. In the Report Builder, how do you set this up?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Select Invoices as the entity source, add a filter on Customer Name (Equals), and add a filter on Invoice Date (Between) with Q1 start and end dates", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Select Customers as the entity source and filter by invoice count"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Use the AR Aging pre-built report and filter by customer"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Select Invoice Lines and group by customer name"}], "explanation": "Use the Invoices entity source, then add two filters: one for Customer.Name (Equals) with the customer name, and one for InvoiceDate (Between) with the Q1 date range. The Between operator requires two values."}, {"id": "rpt4", "text": "What does the 'Shared' toggle do when saving a report in the Report Builder?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "When ON, the report is visible to all users with report access. When OFF, only the creator can see it.", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "When ON, the report results are cached and shared across sessions"}, {"id": "c", "text": "When ON, the report is exported to a shared network drive"}, {"id": "d", "text": "When ON, the report sends email notifications to all managers"}], "explanation": "The Shared toggle controls visibility. Shared reports appear in the Saved Reports dropdown for all users who have access to the Reports module (Admin, Manager, Office Manager, PM). Private reports are visible only to the creator."}, {"id": "rpt5", "text": "You want to create a pie chart showing expenses by category. In the Report Builder, which fields do you configure?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Set chart type to Pie, label field to Category, and value field to Amount", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Set chart type to Pie and select Category as the only column"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Set chart type to Pie, group by Category, and sort by Amount"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Set chart type to Bar and label field to Category — pie charts are not supported"}], "explanation": "For any chart visualization, you need three settings: Chart Type (Pie), Label Field (the category/segment axis — Category), and Value Field (must be numeric — Amount). The label field determines what each slice represents, and the value field determines its size."}, {"id": "rpt6", "text": "Which filter operator would you use to find all jobs where the Due Date has not been set?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "IsNull", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Equals with an empty value"}, {"id": "c", "text": "NotEquals with today's date"}, {"id": "d", "text": "LessThan with a very old date"}], "explanation": "The IsNull operator checks whether a field has no value at all. It does not require a filter value. This is the correct way to find records with missing dates, as opposed to comparing against empty strings or arbitrary dates."}, {"id": "rpt7", "text": "How many entity sources are available in the Report Builder?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "10"}, {"id": "b", "text": "20"}, {"id": "c", "text": "28", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "35"}], "explanation": "The Report Builder provides 28 entity sources: Jobs, Parts, Customers, Expenses, Time Entries, Invoices, Leads, Assets, Purchase Orders, Sales Orders, Quotes, Shipments, Inventory, Payments, Vendors, Production Runs, Lot Records, QC Inspections, Maintenance Schedules, Maintenance Logs, Downtime Logs, Customer Returns, Bin Movements, Planning Cycles, Invoice Lines, Sales Order Lines, Purchase Order Lines, and Quote Lines."}, {"id": "rpt8", "text": "You click on a bar in a chart within the Report Builder. What happens?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The data table below filters to show only the rows matching that bar segment", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "A new report opens for that specific segment"}, {"id": "c", "text": "The bar is highlighted but nothing else changes"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The chart zooms into that bar segment, showing sub-categories"}], "explanation": "Clicking a bar or pie/doughnut slice activates drill-down filtering. The data table updates to show only the rows that match the clicked segment. Click the same segment again or use the breadcrumb to return to the full unfiltered view."}, {"id": "rpt9", "text": "Which roles have access to the Reports module?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "All roles including Engineers and Production Workers"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Admin, Manager, Office Manager, and PM only", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Admin and Manager only"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Admin only"}], "explanation": "The Reports module is restricted to Admin, Manager, Office Manager, and PM roles. Engineers and Production Workers do not have direct access to the full reports page, though they can view personal reports (My Work History, My Time Log) through their respective feature pages."}, {"id": "rpt10", "text": "You want to see a flow diagram showing how quotes convert into cash through the order lifecycle. Where do you go?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Reports → Sankey Flow Reports → Quote-to-Cash", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Reports → Pre-Built → Quote-to-Close"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Reports → Builder → select Quotes entity source"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Dashboard → Financial widget"}], "explanation": "The Quote-to-Cash Sankey diagram is one of 10 Sankey flow visualizations available under Reports → Sankey. It shows the flow from quotes through sales orders, shipments, invoices, to payments. The pre-built Quote-to-Close report shows conversion rates, not the visual flow."}, {"id": "rpt11", "text": "After running a custom report in the Report Builder, you want to download the results. What export option is available directly in the builder?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "CSV — click the CSV button that appears after running the report", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Excel — click the Excel button in the toolbar"}, {"id": "c", "text": "PDF — click the PDF button next to the chart"}, {"id": "d", "text": "No export is available — you must copy-paste from the table"}], "explanation": "The Report Builder shows a CSV export button in the actions bar after a report has been run. It exports all result rows with the selected columns as a comma-separated file. PDF export is available via the server-side API endpoint, but the primary in-builder export is CSV."}, {"id": "rpt12", "text": "You have a saved report that you want to modify. How do you update it?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Load the saved report from the dropdown, modify the configuration, then click Save — it updates the existing report", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "Delete the old report, make changes, and save as a new report"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Saved reports cannot be modified — you must create a new one"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Right-click the saved report name and select Edit"}], "explanation": "When you load a saved report from the toolbar dropdown, modify any settings (columns, filters, chart, etc.), and click Save, it updates the existing saved report with the new configuration. You can also delete a saved report using the red trash icon next to the dropdown."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Sales Orders Overview
summary: What sales orders are, their lifecycle from draft to completion, line items, and how they connect to quotes, jobs, shipments, and invoices.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Sales Orders Overview

A Sales Order (SO) is a confirmed commitment to deliver specific parts to a customer at agreed-upon prices. It is the central document in the order-to-cash workflow, linking upstream quotes to downstream production, shipping, and invoicing.

### What Is a Sales Order?

A sales order captures:
- **Who** is ordering (customer, with optional customer PO reference)
- **What** they want (line items — each referencing a part, quantity, and unit price)
- **When** they need it (requested delivery date)
- **Payment terms** (credit terms like Net 30, Net 60, etc.)
- **Tax** (tax rate applied to the subtotal)

Each SO is assigned a unique order number automatically. The order number appears throughout the system — on shipments, invoices, and the kanban board — so you can always trace back to the original commitment.

### Sales Order Lifecycle

Sales orders move through a defined status progression:

1. **Draft** — The order is being prepared. You can still edit all fields, add/remove line items, and delete the order entirely. Draft orders have not been committed to the customer.

2. **Confirmed** — The order has been reviewed and confirmed. This locks in the commitment. The confirmed date is recorded automatically. From here, production can begin.

3. **In Production** — Jobs have been created from the SO lines and work is underway on the shop floor. The SO moves to this status when linked jobs are active.

4. **Partially Shipped** — Some line items have been shipped but others remain. Each line tracks its own shipped quantity vs. ordered quantity, so you can see exactly what's outstanding.

5. **Shipped** — All line items have been fully shipped. The order is complete from a fulfillment perspective.

6. **Completed** — The order is fully closed — shipped and invoiced. No further action needed.

7. **Cancelled** — The order was cancelled before completion. Cancellation is allowed from Draft or Confirmed status only. Once production has started, you cannot cancel.

### Line Items

Each sales order contains one or more line items. A line item includes:
- **Part** — the part being ordered (selected from the Parts Catalog)
- **Description** — auto-filled from the part, can be overridden
- **Quantity** — how many units the customer wants
- **Unit Price** — price per unit
- **Line Total** — quantity x unit price (calculated automatically)
- **Shipped Quantity** — how many have been shipped so far
- **Remaining Quantity** — how many are still to ship

The line item table in the detail view color-codes shipped quantities: green when fully shipped, yellow when partially shipped.

### How Sales Orders Connect to Other Features

**Quotes:** A sales order can originate from an accepted quote. When a quote is converted to a sales order, the quote reference is preserved and visible in the SO detail. The Quote link in the detail panel is clickable.

**Jobs (Kanban):** Sales order lines drive production. Each line can generate one or more jobs on the kanban board. Moving a job through production stages updates the SO's fulfillment status.

**Shipments:** When goods are ready, you create shipments against SO lines. Each shipment line references an SO line and records how many units are being shipped. Partial shipments are supported — ship 50 of 100 now, the rest later.

**Invoices:** Invoices are generated from shipments or directly from the SO. The invoice references the SO and its lines for billing accuracy.

**Customers:** Every SO belongs to a customer. The customer link in the SO detail panel navigates directly to the customer record.

### Financial Summary

Each sales order displays:
- **Subtotal** — sum of all line totals
- **Tax** — subtotal multiplied by the tax rate percentage
- **Total** — subtotal + tax

These values update automatically as line items are added or modified.

### When to Use Sales Orders

- When a customer confirms a quote and you need to formalize the commitment
- When a customer sends a purchase order (PO) that you need to fulfill
- When you need to track what has been shipped vs. what's still outstanding
- When you need to generate invoices based on fulfilled orders
- When you want full traceability from order through production to delivery", "sections": []}

title: Sales Orders Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the Sales Orders module: statuses, line items, connections to quotes/shipments/invoices, and common workflows.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "so1", "text": "A customer calls to confirm their quote and place an order. What is the correct first step in Forge?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Create a new job on the kanban board for the customer's parts"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Convert the accepted quote to a sales order, which preserves the quote reference and line items", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Create an invoice directly from the quote"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Go to Shipments and create a shipment for the quoted parts"}], "explanation": "When a quote is accepted, you convert it to a sales order. This creates the SO with the quote's line items and preserves the quote reference link. From the SO, you then drive production, shipping, and invoicing."}, {"id": "so2", "text": "You're creating a new sales order. Which of the following is NOT required to save the order?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Selecting a customer"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Adding at least one line item"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Setting the tax rate"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Entering the customer's PO number", "isCorrect": true}], "explanation": "Customer PO is optional — not all customers send purchase orders. However, a customer selection, at least one line item, and a tax rate (which defaults to 0) are all required to save."}, {"id": "so3", "text": "A sales order is in Confirmed status. The customer wants to cancel. What can you do?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Delete the order permanently from the system"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Cancel the order — cancellation is allowed from Draft or Confirmed status", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Nothing — confirmed orders cannot be cancelled or modified"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Change the status back to Draft first, then cancel"}], "explanation": "Orders can be cancelled from either Draft or Confirmed status. Once an order moves to In Production or beyond, cancellation is no longer available. Note that Delete is only available for Draft orders."}, {"id": "so4", "text": "You've shipped 50 out of 100 units on a sales order line. What status does the order show?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Shipped — any shipment changes the status"}, {"id": "b", "text": "In Production — the order stays in production until all units ship"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Partially Shipped — some lines have shipped quantities but not all are fully fulfilled", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Draft — partial shipments don't affect status"}], "explanation": "When some but not all line items are fully shipped, the order shows Partially Shipped (yellow chip). Each line independently tracks its shipped quantity vs. ordered quantity. The order moves to Shipped only when all lines are fully fulfilled."}, {"id": "so5", "text": "In the sales order detail view, you notice a line item's shipped quantity is shown in green. What does this mean?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The line item is on backorder"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The line item has been fully shipped — shipped quantity equals or exceeds ordered quantity", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The line item's shipment is in transit"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The line item has been invoiced"}], "explanation": "Green text on the shipped quantity means the line is fully shipped (isFullyShipped = true). Yellow text means partially shipped (some shipped but not all). This color coding gives you a quick visual status of fulfillment per line."}, {"id": "so6", "text": "When adding a line item to a new sales order, where do parts come from?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "You type in a free-text part description — no part catalog lookup"}, {"id": "b", "text": "An autocomplete searches the Parts Catalog, showing only Active parts by part number and description", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "You select from a dropdown of all parts regardless of status"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Parts are automatically pulled from the linked quote"}], "explanation": "The line item part field is an autocomplete that searches only Active parts from the Parts Catalog. It shows results in 'Part # — Description' format. You must select a part from the search results."}, {"id": "so7", "text": "Where can you see how a sales order connects to its source quote?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "In the Sales Orders table — there's a Quote column showing the link"}, {"id": "b", "text": "In the detail view's info grid — the Quote field shows the quote number as a clickable entity link", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Quotes and sales orders are not linked in the system"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Only in the Reports module under Order Traceability"}], "explanation": "The sales order detail view shows the linked quote number in the info grid. The quote number is a clickable entity link that opens the quote detail dialog, giving you full traceability from quote to order."}, {"id": "so8", "text": "You want to find all orders for a specific customer that haven't shipped yet. How do you filter the list?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Use the Search field to type the customer name and manually scan for non-shipped statuses"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Set the Customer filter to the specific customer, then set the Status filter to Draft, Confirmed, or In Production", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Go to the customer's detail page and check their Orders tab"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Both B and C would work", "isCorrect": true}], "explanation": "The sales orders list page has both Customer and Status filters. Select the customer from the dropdown and then filter by status. You could also navigate to the customer's detail page and check their Orders tab for a customer-centric view."}, {"id": "so9", "text": "What happens to the financial summary when you change the tax rate on a sales order?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Nothing changes until you save — totals are calculated server-side"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The Tax amount and Total update in real time as you type the new rate", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "You must click a Recalculate button to update the totals"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Tax is calculated per line item, not at the order level"}], "explanation": "The summary section in the create/edit dialog computes subtotal, tax amount (subtotal x rate%), and grand total in real time using computed signals. Changes to the tax rate or line items are reflected immediately."}, {"id": "so10", "text": "A Draft sales order can be deleted, but a Confirmed order cannot. Why?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Confirmed orders have been communicated to the customer — deletion would break the audit trail. Cancellation preserves the record.", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "b", "text": "There is no difference — both can be deleted"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Confirmed orders are locked by the accounting system"}, {"id": "d", "text": "It's a bug — confirmed orders should also be deletable"}], "explanation": "Draft orders haven't been committed, so deletion is safe. Confirmed orders represent a business commitment — deleting them would break the audit trail. Instead, you cancel a confirmed order, which preserves the record with a Cancelled status for history and traceability."}, {"id": "so11", "text": "How does the create dialog for a sales order differ from a simple form dialog?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "It uses a standard single-column form layout like other create dialogs"}, {"id": "b", "text": "It uses a split layout — line items table and add-line form on the left, order details and summary on the right sidebar", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "It opens as a full-page view instead of a dialog"}, {"id": "d", "text": "It has a multi-step wizard with separate pages for details and lines"}], "explanation": "The SO create dialog uses a 1100px split layout. The left (main) panel contains the line items table and the add-line form with part autocomplete, quantity, and unit price. The right sidebar has order details (customer, PO, credit terms, delivery date, tax rate), notes, and a live summary showing subtotal, tax, and total."}, {"id": "so12", "text": "You click a sales order row in the table. What happens to the URL?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Nothing — the detail opens as a popup with no URL change"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The URL updates to include ?detail=sales-order:{id}, making the view shareable and bookmarkable", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The page navigates to /sales-orders/{id} as a separate route"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The URL changes to /sales-orders?selected={id}"}], "explanation": "Clicking a row opens the detail dialog via DetailDialogService, which updates the URL to include ?detail=sales-order:{id}. This means you can copy the URL and share it — anyone opening it will land on the same sales order detail. The URL is cleared when the dialog closes."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Search Overview
summary: What global search does, entity types searched, full-text and RAG hybrid search, and keyboard shortcuts.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Global Search Overview

Global search is always available from the header bar at the top of every page. It provides instant, cross-entity search across six core entity types, combining traditional full-text search with AI-powered RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) results when the AI module is enabled.

### How It Works

The search input sits in the application header between the breadcrumb navigation and the action buttons. As you type, results appear in a dropdown overlay grouped by entity type. The system searches across six entity types simultaneously:

- **Jobs** — matches on job number and title
- **Customers** — matches on customer name and company name
- **Parts** — matches on part number and description
- **Leads** — matches on company name and contact name
- **Assets** — matches on asset name and serial number
- **Expenses** — matches on description and category

Search requires at least 2 characters before results appear. Results are debounced so the system waits for you to stop typing before querying — this keeps the interface responsive even on large datasets.

### Full-Text Search

The left column of the search overlay shows traditional full-text results. Each result displays an icon representing the entity type, the primary title (job number, part number, customer name, etc.), a subtitle with additional context, and a type label. Clicking any result navigates you directly to that entity's page or opens its detail dialog.

### AI-Powered RAG Results

When the Ollama AI module is running, the search overlay expands to two columns. The right column shows AI results powered by RAG — Retrieval-Augmented Generation. This uses document embeddings stored in PostgreSQL via pgvector to find semantically related content, not just exact text matches.

The AI column shows:
- A **generated answer** — a natural-language summary responding to your query
- **Relevant document chunks** — pieces of indexed content that match your query, each with a relevance score percentage

RAG results are especially useful for questions like 'what jobs involve aluminum machining' or 'which customer ordered the gear housing' — queries where the exact words might not appear in the record but the meaning is captured in indexed documents.

### Keyboard Shortcuts

- **Ctrl+K** — Focus the search input from anywhere in the application. This is the fastest way to start searching without reaching for the mouse.
- **Escape** — Close the search results overlay and clear the search input
- **Click a result** — Navigate to that entity and close the overlay

### When to Use Global Search

- When you need to quickly find a job by number or title
- When looking up a customer or lead by name
- When you know a part number but don't want to navigate to the Parts page first
- When you have a vague query and want AI to help find relevant records
- When jumping between different entity types frequently throughout the day", "sections": []}

title: Shipments Overview
summary: What shipments are, the shipment lifecycle, line items, and how shipments connect to sales orders and invoices.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Shipments Overview

The Shipments module tracks every outbound shipment from your shop. Each shipment is tied to a Sales Order and contains one or more line items that describe what is being shipped, in what quantity. Shipments move through a defined lifecycle from creation to delivery, and they connect directly to invoicing so you always know what has been shipped and billed.

### What Is a Shipment?

A shipment represents a physical package or set of packages leaving your facility to fulfill (or partially fulfill) a Sales Order. Each shipment has:
- A unique **Shipment Number** (auto-generated)
- A link to exactly one **Sales Order**
- One or more **Line Items** specifying parts and quantities
- Optional **Carrier** and **Tracking Number** for logistics
- Optional **Weight** and **Shipping Cost** for record-keeping
- A **Status** indicating where the shipment is in its lifecycle

### Shipment Lifecycle

Shipments follow a linear status progression:

1. **Pending** — Shipment has been created but nothing has been packed yet. This is the initial status for all new shipments.
2. **Packed** — Items have been physically packed and are ready to ship. The shipment is waiting for carrier pickup or drop-off.
3. **Shipped** — The shipment has left your facility. The shipped date is recorded automatically when you mark it shipped.
4. **In Transit** — The carrier has the shipment and it is en route to the customer. Tracking information updates are available if a tracking number is provided.
5. **Delivered** — The shipment has been received by the customer. The delivered date is recorded automatically.
6. **Cancelled** — The shipment was cancelled before delivery. Cancelled shipments are preserved for audit purposes.

You can move a shipment forward through statuses using the action buttons in the detail view. Shipments can be marked shipped from either Pending or Packed status, and marked delivered from either Shipped or In Transit status.

### Line Items

Each shipment line references a Part from your Parts Catalog and a quantity. Lines can optionally link to a specific Sales Order Line to track partial fulfillment. When you create a shipment, you select a Sales Order first, then add parts and quantities. A single Sales Order can have multiple shipments (partial deliveries), and each shipment tracks exactly which items and quantities were included.

### How Shipments Connect to Other Features

**Sales Orders:** Every shipment belongs to one Sales Order. The Sales Order tracks overall fulfillment across all its shipments. You can see the linked Sales Order directly from the shipment detail view.

**Invoices:** After a shipment is shipped, it can be linked to an Invoice. The shipment detail view shows the linked invoice when one exists, providing a clear audit trail from order to shipment to billing.

**Customers:** The customer name and shipping address flow from the Sales Order. The shipment detail view shows the customer name and allows navigation to the Sales Order for full order context.

**Carrier Integration:** The system supports carrier rate comparison and label generation. From the shipment detail, you can request shipping rates from configured carriers, compare options, and create a shipping label — all without leaving Forge.

**Tracking:** When a tracking number is entered (manually or via label creation), you can view the tracking timeline directly in the shipment detail. The timeline shows each tracking event with location, status, and timestamp.

**Packing Slip PDF:** Every shipment can generate a printable packing slip that includes the shipment number, customer information, shipping address, and a table of all line items with quantities. This is useful for including in the physical package.

### When to Use Shipments

- When fulfilling a Sales Order — create a shipment to record what is being sent
- When shipping partial orders — create multiple shipments against the same Sales Order
- When you need carrier rate comparison before choosing a shipping method
- When you need tracking visibility for outbound packages
- When generating packing slips to include with shipments
- When you need an audit trail connecting orders, shipments, and invoices", "sections": []}

summary: A guided tour of the shipments list page: search, filters, data table, creating shipments, and viewing shipment details.
contentJson: {"steps": [{"element": "[data-testid='new-shipment-btn']", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "New Shipment Button", "description": "Click here to create a new shipment. You'll select a Sales Order, add line items with parts and quantities, and optionally enter carrier and tracking information. The dialog uses a split layout: line items on the left, shipment details and notes on the right."}}, {"element": ".filters-bar app-input", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Search Shipments", "description": "Search by shipment number, Sales Order number, customer name, carrier, or tracking number. Press Enter to apply the search. Results update in the table below."}}, {"element": ".filters-bar app-select", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Status Filter", "description": "Filter shipments by their current status: Pending, Packed, Shipped, In Transit, Delivered, or Cancelled. Each status has a distinct color-coded chip in the table for quick identification."}}, {"element": "app-data-table", "popover": {"side": "top", "title": "Shipments Table", "description": "Click any row to open the shipment detail dialog. Columns include Shipment Number, SO Number, Customer, Status (color chip), Carrier, Tracking Number, Shipped Date, and Created date. Click column headers to sort. Use the gear icon to manage column visibility and order."}}, {"element": "[data-testid='new-shipment-btn']", "popover": {"side": "left", "title": "Creating a Shipment — Walkthrough", "description": "When you click New Shipment, a dialog opens with a split layout. On the left, you manage line items: select a part from the autocomplete dropdown, enter a quantity, and click Add. Each line appears in a table where you can remove it. On the right sidebar, select the Sales Order (required), and optionally enter Carrier, Tracking Number, Weight, Shipping Cost, and Notes. Save is disabled until you have at least one line item and a Sales Order selected."}}, {"element": "app-data-table", "popover": {"side": "top", "title": "Shipment Detail View", "description": "Clicking a row opens the detail dialog. It shows the shipment number, status chip, linked Sales Order (clickable), customer name, carrier info, tracking number, shipped/delivered dates, weight, shipping cost, notes, and all line items. Action buttons appear based on status: Mark Shipped (from Pending/Packed), Get Rates (carrier rate comparison), Track (loads tracking timeline), and Mark Delivered (from Shipped/In Transit). An activity timeline shows the full history of status changes and updates."}}], "appRoute": "/shipments", "startButtonLabel": "Tour Shipments"}

What is the first step to record the outbound shipment in Forge?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Sales Orders and click a Ship button on the order"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Navigate to Shipments, click New Shipment, select the Sales Order, add line items with parts and quantities, then save", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Go to Inventory and create a bin movement to remove the items from stock"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Create an Invoice first and the shipment is generated automatically"}], "explanation": "Shipments are created from the Shipments page. Click New Shipment, select the Sales Order from the autocomplete, add the parts and quantities being shipped as line items, and save. The shipment starts in Pending status."}, {"id": "sh2", "text": "What is the correct order of shipment statuses from creation to completion?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Draft → Packed → Shipped → Delivered"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Pending → Packed → Shipped → In Transit → Delivered", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Open → Processing → Shipped → Closed"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Created → Ready → Out for Delivery → Complete"}], "explanation": "The shipment lifecycle is: Pending (created) → Packed (items ready) → Shipped (left facility) → In Transit (carrier has it) → Delivered (customer received). Not every shipment passes through all statuses — you can skip from Pending directly to Shipped."}, {"id": "sh3", "text": "You have a Sales Order with 100 units but the customer wants 50 shipped now and 50 later. How do you handle this?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "You cannot split shipments — the entire Sales Order must ship at once"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Create two separate shipments against the same Sales Order, each with 50 units", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Edit the Sales Order to reduce it to 50, then create a second Sales Order for the remaining 50"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Ship all 100 and mark 50 as backordered in the notes"}], "explanation": "A single Sales Order can have multiple shipments (partial deliveries). Create the first shipment with 50 units, then later create a second shipment with the remaining 50 units — both linked to the same Sales Order."}, {"id": "sh4", "text": "A shipment is currently in Pending status. Which actions are available in the detail view?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Only Mark Delivered"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Mark Shipped and Get Rates", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Mark Shipped, Get Rates, Mark Delivered, and Track"}, {"id": "d", "text": "No actions — you must edit the shipment first"}], "explanation": "From Pending (or Packed) status, two actions are available: Mark Shipped (transitions to Shipped and records the date) and Get Rates (opens carrier rate comparison dialog). Mark Delivered is only available from Shipped or In Transit status. Track is available whenever a tracking number exists."}, {"id": "sh5", "text": "What is required to save a new shipment?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Only a Sales Order selection"}, {"id": "b", "text": "A Sales Order selection and at least one line item with a part and quantity", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "A Sales Order, carrier, and tracking number"}, {"id": "d", "text": "A Sales Order, line items, carrier, tracking number, and weight"}], "explanation": "Two things are required: a Sales Order must be selected (the only required sidebar field), and at least one line item must be added (each line needs a part and a quantity of at least 1). Carrier, tracking number, weight, shipping cost, and notes are all optional."}, {"id": "sh6", "text": "You want to compare shipping rates from different carriers before shipping. Where do you do this?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Admin → Shipping → Rate Calculator"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Open the shipment detail (click the row), then click Get Rates to open the rate comparison dialog", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Navigate to the linked Sales Order and use its shipping tab"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Rates are not available in Forge — you must check carrier websites separately"}], "explanation": "In the shipment detail view, click the Get Rates button (available when status is Pending or Packed). This opens a dialog showing rates from configured carriers with service level, price, and estimated delivery time. You can create a shipping label directly from this dialog."}, {"id": "sh7", "text": "A shipment has a tracking number entered. How do you view the tracking timeline?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to the carrier's website and paste the tracking number"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Open the shipment detail and click the Track button to load the tracking timeline directly in Forge", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Tracking timelines are only available for shipments with carrier API integration enabled"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Navigate to Admin → Tracking to look up any tracking number"}], "explanation": "When a tracking number is present, a Track button appears in the shipment detail view. Click it to load the tracking timeline, which shows each tracking event with location, status, and timestamp — all within Forge. Click the close button to collapse the timeline."}, {"id": "sh8", "text": "What happens when you click Mark Shipped on a shipment?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The shipment is immediately deleted and an invoice is created"}, {"id": "b", "text": "A confirmation dialog appears, and if confirmed, the status changes to Shipped and the shipped date is recorded automatically", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The status changes without confirmation and you must manually enter the shipped date"}, {"id": "d", "text": "An email is automatically sent to the customer with the tracking number"}], "explanation": "Clicking Mark Shipped opens a confirmation dialog. If you confirm, the status transitions to Shipped and the shipped date is automatically set to the current date. A success snackbar confirms the action. The customer is not automatically notified."}, {"id": "sh9", "text": "Where can you see which invoice is linked to a shipment?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "In the Sales Order detail under the Shipments tab"}, {"id": "b", "text": "In the shipment detail view — a Linked Invoice field appears with a clickable link to the invoice", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Invoices and shipments are not linked in Forge"}, {"id": "d", "text": "In the Reports module under Shipment-Invoice Mapping"}], "explanation": "The shipment detail view shows a Linked Invoice field when an invoice has been created for the shipment. The invoice number is a clickable entity link that opens the invoice detail dialog directly."}, {"id": "sh10", "text": "You need to generate a packing slip to include in a physical package. How do you do this?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Print the shipment detail page using the browser's print function"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Generate a Packing Slip PDF from the shipment, which includes the shipment number, customer info, shipping address, and line items with quantities", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Packing slips are only available through the Sales Order detail"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Create a custom report in the Reports module filtered by shipment number"}], "explanation": "Each shipment can generate a Packing Slip PDF. The packing slip includes the shipment number, company information, customer details, shipping address, and a table of all line items with part numbers, descriptions, and quantities. This is designed to be printed and included in the physical package."}, {"id": "sh11", "text": "The Shipments table shows '---' in the Tracking # column for a shipment. What does this mean?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The tracking number has been redacted for security"}, {"id": "b", "text": "No tracking number has been entered for this shipment", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The shipment is using a carrier that does not support tracking"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The tracking number will appear once the shipment status changes to Shipped"}], "explanation": "When no tracking number has been entered, the table displays '---' as a placeholder. The same applies to the Carrier and Shipped Date columns. Tracking numbers can be entered when creating the shipment, or added later via carrier label creation or manual edit."}, {"id": "sh12", "text": "A shipment is in Shipped status. Which action button is available in the detail view?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Mark Shipped and Get Rates"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Mark Delivered (and Track, if a tracking number exists)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Cancel Shipment and Create Invoice"}, {"id": "d", "text": "No actions — Shipped is a terminal status"}], "explanation": "From Shipped (or In Transit) status, the Mark Delivered button is available. If a tracking number exists and the tracking timeline hasn't been loaded yet, the Track button also appears. Mark Shipped and Get Rates are only available from Pending or Packed status."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Shop Floor Display Overview
summary: What the shop floor display is, how kiosk mode works, scan-based authentication, worker cards, and job actions.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Shop Floor Display Overview

The Shop Floor Display is a full-screen kiosk interface designed for production environments. It runs at `/display/shop-floor` and provides a touch-friendly, always-on dashboard where workers can clock in/out, view their assigned jobs, start and stop timers, mark jobs complete, receive goods, and ship orders — all without navigating the main application.

### Kiosk Mode

The shop floor display operates in kiosk mode — it is not part of the standard application layout. There is no sidebar, no toolbar, and no header navigation. The entire screen is dedicated to the shop floor interface. It is meant to run on a dedicated monitor or tablet mounted on the production floor.

The display auto-refreshes every 15 seconds to keep worker status and job data current. A live clock is shown in the header. Theme (light/dark) and font size are persisted to localStorage so the kiosk retains its settings across browser restarts.

### Two Display Variants

There are two shop floor routes:
- **`/display/shop-floor`** — the main shop floor display with a worker card grid, unassigned jobs section, upcoming events, and full action overlays (clock, timer, receiving, shipping)
- **`/display/shop-floor/clock`** — a dedicated clock-in/clock-out kiosk with terminal setup, team filtering, and a simpler scan-first flow using the `BarcodeScanInputComponent` (focused input field)

The main display uses the global `ScannerService` (passive keyboard-wedge detection on the document), while the clock kiosk uses a focused barcode scan input component.

### Authentication Flow

The shop floor display uses ephemeral authentication — no persistent login session. Workers authenticate for a single action, then the session is cleared automatically. There are two ways to authenticate:

1. **Scan + PIN:** The worker scans their RFID badge or barcode. The system identifies the scan via the API (`/display/shop-floor/identify-scan`). If it matches an employee, a PIN overlay appears. The worker enters their numeric PIN (minimum 4 digits) and submits. After successful auth, the actions overlay appears.

2. **Card Tap + Password:** The worker taps their name card on the main grid. A password overlay appears (not PIN — full password). After successful auth, the actions overlay appears.

Both flows use a phase-based state machine: `main → pin → actions → (job-select | receiving | shipping) → main`. Auto-dismiss timeouts prevent stale overlays:
- PIN phase: 20 seconds before auto-cancel
- Job selection phase: 15 seconds before auto-cancel
- Actions phase: 30 seconds of inactivity before auto-logout

### Worker Card Grid

The main dashboard shows all workers in a responsive grid. Each worker card is a horizontal layout with:
- A left **status stripe** whose color indicates the worker's clock status (green for working, yellow for break/lunch, gray for clocked out)
- An **avatar** with the worker's initials and color
- The worker's **name** and **current status** (short label from ClockEventTypeService)
- **Elapsed time** since the worker entered their current status (live-updating every second)
- A list of **assigned jobs** (up to 4 visible, with a '+N more' overflow indicator), each showing the job number, title, stage color dot, and a timer icon if the job has an active timer

Worker cards are interactive — tapping one enters the PIN/password phase. Cards also serve as drop targets for drag-and-drop job assignment from the unassigned jobs section.

### Job Actions

After authenticating, the worker sees their assigned jobs with action buttons:
- **Start Timer** (`play_circle`) — begins tracking time against the selected job
- **Stop Timer** (`stop_circle`) — stops the running timer
- **Mark Complete** (`check_circle`) — moves the job to the next stage and logs completion

Below the jobs, **clock action buttons** are shown dynamically based on the worker's current status (from `ClockEventTypeService`). These include Clock In, Clock Out, Break, Lunch, and any custom clock event types configured in the admin.

### Receiving and Shipping

Role-gated dock actions appear after authentication:
- **Receive Goods** — available to Admin, Manager, OfficeManager, Engineer, and ProductionWorker roles. Shows open purchase orders with receivable lines, quantity adjustment (+/- buttons), optional bin location selection, and a Receive All confirmation button.
- **Ship Goods** — available to Admin, Manager, and OfficeManager roles. Shows pending/packed shipments, shipment detail with carrier and tracking info, a Print Packing Slip button, and a Mark Shipped confirmation button.

### IsShopFloor Stage Filter

Not all kanban stages appear on the shop floor. The `IsShopFloor` boolean flag on `TrackType` and `JobStage` entities controls which stages are visible. Only stages marked `IsShopFloor = true` show their jobs on the shop floor display. This filters out non-physical stages like quoting, invoicing, and payment.

### Unassigned Jobs and Drag-and-Drop

Unassigned jobs (jobs with no `assigneeId`) appear in a separate section below the worker grid. Each unassigned job card shows the job number, title, stage name, stage color dot, and priority. Cards are draggable — drag an unassigned job onto a worker card to assign it. After clock-in, if the worker has no assignments, a job picker overlay appears automatically (15-second timeout).

### Upcoming Events

The shop floor display shows upcoming events (meetings, training sessions, safety events) loaded from the Events API. Each event card shows the type icon, title, start time, location, and a 'Required' badge if applicable.

### Kiosk Settings

The header includes controls for:
- **Font size** — increase/decrease buttons with 5 steps (12px, 14px, 16px, 18px, 20px), applied via CSS zoom. Persisted to localStorage as `sf-font-index`.
- **Theme toggle** — switches between light and dark themes. Persisted to localStorage as `sf-theme`.
- **Live clock** — displays current time in `HH:MM:SS` format, updating every second.
- **Kiosk search bar** — a debounced search input (300ms, min 2 chars) that queries `/display/shop-floor/search` for jobs, parts, and other entities.", "sections": []}

title: Shop Floor Display — Guided Tour

All kiosk settings are saved to localStorage and persist across browser restarts."}}, {"element": ".sf-header__stats", "popover": {"side": "bottom", "title": "Status Summary Bar", "description": "Real-time counts of workers currently Working (green), On Break (yellow), Unassigned Jobs, jobs Done Today, and maintenance Alerts (red, shown only when > 0). These refresh automatically every 15 seconds."}}, {"element": ".sf-grid", "popover": {"side": "top", "title": "Worker Cards Grid", "description": "All workers are shown in a responsive grid. Each card has a colored left stripe (green = working, yellow = break, gray = clocked out), an avatar, the worker's name and status, elapsed time, and up to 4 assigned jobs. Tap a card to authenticate and access clock actions. You can also drag unassigned jobs onto worker cards to assign them."}}, {"element": ".sf-card", "popover": {"side": "right", "title": "Worker Card", "description": "A single worker card.
