Returns ScanIdentification with scanType, entityId, entityNumber, entityTitle."}, {"label": "Scan types", "value": "employee (→ PIN phase), job (→ feedback message), sales-order (→ feedback message), unknown (→ 'Scan not recognized')."}, {"label": "Fallback", "value": "If identification API fails, the scan value is treated as an employee badge and the PIN overlay opens."}, {"label": "Scanner context", "value": "ScannerService context set to 'shop-floor'. Only processes scans during the main phase — ignores scans during overlays."}], "heading": "Scan Identification"}]}

title: Shop Floor Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the shop floor display: kiosk mode, authentication, worker cards, job actions, receiving, shipping, and stage filtering.

This prevents stale overlays from blocking the kiosk for other workers."}, {"id": "sf3", "text": "On the worker card grid, what does a yellow left stripe on a worker's card indicate?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The worker is currently working on a job"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The worker is on break or lunch", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The worker is clocked out for the day"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The worker has an overdue job assignment"}], "explanation": "The left stripe color indicates the worker's clock status: green means working, yellow means on break or lunch, and gray means clocked out. The colors are determined by the ClockEventTypeService status mapping."}, {"id": "sf4", "text": "A worker clocks in but has no assigned jobs. What happens next on the shop floor display?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Nothing — the worker sees the standard actions overlay and can log out"}, {"id": "b", "text": "A job picker overlay appears automatically, showing unassigned jobs the worker can select", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The worker is redirected to the kanban board to find a job"}, {"id": "d", "text": "An alert is sent to the manager to assign a job"}], "explanation": "After clocking in, if the worker has no assigned jobs, a job selection overlay appears automatically (after a brief 0.8-second feedback delay). The worker can pick an unassigned job or skip. The overlay has a 15-second auto-dismiss timeout."}, {"id": "sf5", "text": "How do you assign an unassigned job to a worker directly from the shop floor main dashboard (without authenticating)?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Click the unassigned job, then click the worker's name from a dropdown"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Drag the unassigned job card from the unassigned section and drop it onto the worker's card", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Right-click the job and select 'Assign To'"}, {"id": "d", "text": "You cannot assign jobs from the main dashboard — you must authenticate first"}], "explanation": "Unassigned job cards are draggable. Drag a job from the unassigned jobs section and drop it onto any worker card in the grid. The assignment is made immediately via the API, and the display refreshes. No authentication is required for drag-and-drop assignment."}, {"id": "sf6", "text": "A Production Worker authenticates on the shop floor kiosk. Which dock actions will they see?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Both Receive Goods and Ship Goods"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Receive Goods only", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Ship Goods only"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Neither — dock actions are only for Admin users"}], "explanation": "Receive Goods is available to Admin, Manager, OfficeManager, Engineer, and ProductionWorker roles. Ship Goods is restricted to Admin, Manager, and OfficeManager roles only. Both require the worker to be actively clocked in."}, {"id": "sf7", "text": "You adjust the font size on the shop floor kiosk to 18px and switch to dark theme. The browser is restarted. What happens to these settings?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "They reset to defaults (12px, light theme)"}, {"id": "b", "text": "They are preserved — font size and theme are saved to localStorage", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Only the theme is preserved, font size resets"}, {"id": "d", "text": "They are saved to the user's account preferences on the server"}], "explanation": "Both font size (stored as 'sf-font-index' in localStorage) and theme (stored as 'sf-theme' in localStorage) persist across browser restarts. This ensures the kiosk retains its display settings without reconfiguration."}, {"id": "sf8", "text": "What does the IsShopFloor flag on a job stage control?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Whether jobs in that stage can be edited from the shop floor"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Whether jobs in that stage appear on the shop floor display", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Whether workers can clock in when assigned to jobs in that stage"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Whether the stage column is visible on the kanban board"}], "explanation": "The IsShopFloor boolean flag on TrackType and JobStage entities controls visibility on the shop floor display. Only stages with IsShopFloor = true show their jobs. This filters out non-physical stages like quoting, invoicing, and payment that are irrelevant to production floor workers."}, {"id": "sf9", "text": "During the receiving flow on the shop floor, a worker selects a purchase order. What can they adjust before confirming receipt?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The vendor name and PO number"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The quantity to receive for each line item and optionally select a bin location", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The unit price and total cost of each line"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The expected delivery date and carrier information"}], "explanation": "The receiving overlay shows each PO line with its remaining quantity. Workers adjust the receive quantity per line using +/- buttons (capped at the remaining quantity). They can also optionally select a bin location from a dropdown. Clicking 'Receive All' submits all lines with quantity > 0."}, {"id": "sf10", "text": "A worker starts a timer on a job from the shop floor actions overlay. Where does this timer show up?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Only in the Time Tracking page — the shop floor display does not show active timers"}, {"id": "b", "text": "On the worker's card in the main grid, as a timer icon next to the job, and the job row is highlighted in the actions overlay", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Only in the header stats bar as a global timer count"}, {"id": "d", "text": "In a separate timer panel that slides out from the right"}], "explanation": "Active timers are visible in multiple places on the shop floor: the worker's card shows a timer icon (timer) next to the job with an active timer, and the job row in the actions overlay gets an active highlight (sf-actions-card__job--active). The timer is started via POST /time-tracking/timer/start and can be stopped from the same overlay."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Time Tracking Overview
summary: What time tracking does, how timers and manual entries work, clock events, job linking, and how time data connects to payroll and costing.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Time Tracking Overview

The Time Tracking module records how employees spend their working hours. It supports two methods of capturing time — live timers and manual entries — and integrates with clock events, jobs, payroll, and cost accounting.

### Two Ways to Track Time

**Live Timers:** Click Start Timer to begin tracking time in real time. The timer runs until you click Stop Timer. The elapsed duration is calculated automatically from the start and stop timestamps. Timers sync across browser tabs and devices via SignalR — if you start a timer on your phone, your desktop shows it running immediately.

**Manual Entries:** Click Manual Entry to log time after the fact. You enter the date, hours, minutes, an optional category, and optional notes. Manual entries are marked with an edit_note icon in the table to distinguish them from timer-based entries.

### Categories

Every time entry (timer or manual) can be tagged with a category:
- **Production** — direct production work on a job
- **Setup** — machine setup, tooling changes, fixture preparation
- **Inspection** — quality checks, measurements, first-article inspection
- **Maintenance** — equipment maintenance, cleaning, calibration
- **Training** — learning, onboarding, skill development
- **Meeting** — team meetings, planning sessions, standups
- **Admin** — paperwork, emails, administrative tasks
- **Cleanup** — end-of-shift cleanup, organizing
- **Other** — anything that doesn't fit the above

Categories are optional but recommended — they feed into reports and help managers understand how time is distributed across activities.

### Clock Events

Clock events are separate from time entries. They record when an employee clocks in or out of the facility using an RFID badge, barcode scan, or manual entry on the shop floor kiosk. Clock events track:
- **Event type** — clock-in, clock-out, break-start, break-end (driven by reference data)
- **Timestamp** — when the event occurred
- **Scan method** — how the employee identified themselves (RFID, barcode, manual)
- **Source** — which device or kiosk recorded the event
- **Reason** — optional note (e.g., 'early departure — appointment')

Clock events establish the employee's presence on-site. Time entries track what they worked on during that presence.

### Job Linking

Time entries can be linked to a specific job. When a timer is started from a job's detail panel on the kanban board, the job association is set automatically. This connects time directly to job costing — the labor cost for each entry is calculated from the employee's rate and applied to the job's cost rollup.

### Date Range Filtering

The time tracking page has From and To date pickers that filter the entries table. A summary line shows the total hours and entry count for the current filter. When no dates are set, recent entries are shown.

### Locking and Editability

Time entries can be locked by a manager or by pay period closure. Locked entries show a lock icon and cannot be edited or deleted. Unlocked entries from today or future dates can be deleted; past entries require an admin time correction (which creates an audit trail).

### Admin Time Corrections

Managers and admins can correct time entries via the Admin > Time Corrections page. Corrections require a reason and create a TimeCorrectionLog entry that preserves the original values for audit purposes. Fields that can be corrected include date, duration, start/end times, category, notes, and job assignment.

### Pay Periods and Overtime

Time entries roll up into pay periods (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, or monthly — configurable in admin settings). When a pay period is locked, all entries within that period become read-only. Overtime rules can be configured (e.g., >40 hours/week = 1.5x) and the system calculates overtime breakdowns per employee per week.

### SignalR Real-Time Sync

Timer start and stop events broadcast via SignalR's TimerHub. If two browser tabs are open, starting a timer in one tab updates the other tab's display immediately. The page header dynamically switches between Start Timer and Stop Timer buttons based on whether a timer is currently active.

### Connection to Payroll

Time entries feed into payroll calculations. Each entry's labor cost is computed from the employee's hourly rate multiplied by the duration. Burden cost (overhead allocation) is tracked separately. When QuickBooks integration is active, time entries can sync as Time Activities to the accounting system via the AccountingTimeActivityId field.", "sections": []}

title: Time Tracking Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of time tracking: timers, manual entries, clock events, job linking, locking, corrections, and real-time sync.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "tt1", "text": "You need to track time for a 2-hour machine setup that happened yesterday. Click it to open the stop dialog."}, {"id": "tt3", "text": "What is the difference between a time entry and a clock event?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "They are the same thing — clock events are just time entries from the shop floor"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Time entries record what work was done and for how long; clock events record when an employee arrived at or left the facility", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Clock events track machine uptime; time entries track human labor"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Time entries are for salaried employees; clock events are for hourly workers"}], "explanation": "Time entries track work duration and can be linked to jobs and categories. Clock events track facility presence — when someone clocked in, clocked out, or took breaks. They serve different purposes but complement each other."}, {"id": "tt4", "text": "A time entry in the table shows a lock icon instead of a delete button. What does this mean?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The entry is linked to a job and cannot be modified without unlinking it first"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The entry is either locked by a manager/pay period or is a past-date entry that requires an admin correction to change", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The entry has been synced to QuickBooks and is waiting for confirmation"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The entry belongs to another user and you don't have permission to delete it"}], "explanation": "The lock icon appears when an entry is locked (by a manager or pay period closure) or when it's a past-date entry. Only today's or future unlocked entries can be deleted directly. Past entries require an admin time correction."}, {"id": "tt5", "text": "You want to see only the time entries from the first week of March. How do you filter the table?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Type '03/01' into the search box in the page header"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Set the From date to 03/01 and the To date to 03/07 using the date pickers in the filter bar", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Click the Date column header and select the date range from the dropdown"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Go to Reports > Time Report and set the date range there"}], "explanation": "The filter bar has From and To date pickers. Set From to the start date and To to the end date. The table and summary line update automatically to show only entries within that range."}, {"id": "tt6", "text": "An active timer entry shows 'Running' in green in the Duration column. What happens if you close the browser and reopen the time tracking page?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "The timer is lost — you need to manually add the time"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The timer is still running because it tracks server-side start time; the elapsed time recalculates from the stored TimerStart timestamp", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The timer pauses and you can resume it from where you left off"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The timer stops automatically and a manual entry is created for the elapsed time"}], "explanation": "Timers are server-side. The TimerStart timestamp is stored when you click Start, and duration is calculated from that timestamp when you stop. Closing the browser doesn't affect the running timer — it continues tracking based on the server timestamp."}, {"id": "tt7", "text": "You started a timer on your laptop. Will it show as running on your phone's browser too?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "No — timers are per-device and per-session"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Yes — SignalR broadcasts timer events via the TimerHub, so all connected clients update in real time", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Only if you manually refresh the page on your phone"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Only if both devices are on the same network"}], "explanation": "The TimerHub broadcasts onTimerStartedEvent and onTimerStoppedEvent to all connected clients. When you start a timer on any device, the page header on all your open tabs and devices switches from Start Timer to Stop Timer automatically."}, {"id": "tt8", "text": "A manager needs to correct a time entry from last week that has the wrong duration. What is the proper process?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Delete the entry and create a new one with the correct duration"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Use Admin > Time Corrections, select the entry, change the duration, and provide a reason for the correction", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Edit the entry directly in the data table by double-clicking the duration cell"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Ask the employee to submit a new manual entry with the correct hours"}], "explanation": "Admin time corrections preserve an audit trail. The manager enters the corrected values and a required reason. The original values are stored in a TimeCorrectionLog entry so there is a complete record of what changed and why."}, {"id": "tt9", "text": "What categories are available when starting a timer or creating a manual entry?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Only Production and Setup — other categories must be created in Admin settings"}, {"id": "b", "text": "None, Production, Setup, Inspection, Maintenance, Training, Meeting, Admin, Cleanup, and Other", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Categories are not available for timers, only for manual entries"}, {"id": "d", "text": "The category list is custom per company — there are no default categories"}], "explanation": "Both timer and manual entry dialogs offer the same category dropdown: None (default), Production, Setup, Inspection, Maintenance, Training, Meeting, Admin, Cleanup, and Other. Categories are optional but help with reporting."}, {"id": "tt10", "text": "How is a time entry linked to a job for cost tracking?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "You must manually type the job number into the notes field"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Time entries can have a JobId foreign key — when a timer is started from a job's detail panel on the kanban board, the job link is set automatically", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Jobs are linked after the fact by running a batch report in Admin"}, {"id": "d", "text": "All time entries are automatically linked to whatever job is currently selected on the kanban board"}], "explanation": "The TimeEntry entity has an optional JobId field. When you start a timer from a specific job's detail panel, the JobId is set automatically. The linked job number appears in the Job column of the time tracking table. Labor cost is then allocated to that job."}, {"id": "tt11", "text": "The summary line below the date filters shows '8.5 hours across 12 entries'. You change the From date to yesterday. What happens?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Nothing — the summary only updates when you click a Refresh button"}, {"id": "b", "text": "The table reloads with the new date filter applied and the summary line updates to reflect the filtered results", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The summary line stays the same but the table filters to show only yesterday's entries"}, {"id": "d", "text": "A confirmation dialog asks whether to apply the new filter"}], "explanation": "The date filter controls trigger an automatic reload of the entries table whenever their value changes. The summary line recalculates from the filtered entries, showing the new total hours and count."}, {"id": "tt12", "text": "What does the 'Type' column badge tell you about a time entry?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Whether the entry is billable (blue) or non-billable (gray)"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Whether the entry was created via a live timer ('Timer' badge, blue) or logged after the fact ('Manual' badge, gray)", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "The entry's priority level — high (blue) or normal (gray)"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Whether the entry has been approved by a manager (blue) or is pending (gray)"}], "explanation": "The Type badge distinguishes how the time was recorded. 'Timer' (blue) means it was tracked via the Start/Stop Timer feature with precise timestamps. 'Manual' (gray) means it was entered by the user after the work was done with a specified duration."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}

title: Vendors Overview
summary: What vendors are, how to manage them, payment terms, and how vendors connect to purchase orders and inventory.
contentJson:
{"body": "## Vendors Overview

The Vendors module is your centralized supplier directory. It tracks every company you buy from — raw material suppliers, tooling vendors, service providers, and subcontractors — along with their contact information, addresses, payment terms, and purchase history.

### What Is a Vendor?

A vendor is any external company or individual you purchase goods or services from. Each vendor record stores:
- **Company name** — the legal or trade name of the supplier
- **Contact information** — primary contact name, email, and phone number
- **Address** — street address, city, state, ZIP code, and country
- **Payment terms** — negotiated terms like Net 30, Net 60, Due on Receipt, etc.
- **Active/Inactive status** — whether you currently do business with this vendor

### How Vendors Connect to Other Features

**Purchase Orders:** Every purchase order is linked to a vendor. When you create a PO, you select the vendor, and the PO inherits the vendor's address and payment terms. The vendor detail view shows a complete PO history so you can see all orders placed with a supplier.

**Inventory and Receiving:** When materials arrive, receiving records tie back to the PO and its vendor. This gives you full traceability: which vendor supplied which materials, when they arrived, and whether they met quality standards.

**Vendor Scorecard:** Each vendor has a performance scorecard that tracks on-time delivery percentage, quality acceptance rate, quantity accuracy, and total spend. Vendors receive an overall score and letter grade (A through F) based on weighted criteria: Delivery (40%), Quality (30%), Price (20%), and Quantity (10%).

**Accounting Integration:** When QuickBooks or another accounting provider is connected, vendor records sync automatically. In integrated mode, vendor management becomes read-only in Forge — edits are made in the accounting system. In standalone mode (no provider connected), you have full CRUD control over vendors.

### When to Use Vendors

- When onboarding a new supplier before placing your first PO
- When updating contact information or payment terms for an existing supplier
- When reviewing a vendor's delivery and quality performance via the scorecard
- When deactivating a vendor you no longer do business with (records are preserved)
- When looking up PO history to see what you've ordered from a specific supplier", "sections": []}

This gives you a quick sense of how frequently you order from a supplier without opening the detail view."}}], "appRoute": "/vendors", "startButtonLabel": "Tour Vendors"}

title: Vendors Knowledge Check
summary: Test your knowledge of the Vendors module: creating vendors, payment terms, scorecard, PO history, and accounting integration.
contentJson: {"questions": [{"id": "vn1", "text": "You need to onboard a new raw material supplier before placing your first purchase order. What is the correct first step?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Purchase Orders and create a PO — the vendor will be auto-created"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Navigate to Vendors, click New Vendor, and fill in the company name, contact details, address, and payment terms", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Add the supplier to Inventory as a new storage location"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Go to Admin → Reference Data and add the vendor as a lookup value"}], "explanation": "New suppliers must be created in the Vendors module first. The vendor then becomes available in PO vendor dropdowns."}, {"id": "vn2", "text": "Which field is the only required field when creating a new vendor?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Contact Name"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Email"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Company Name", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "d", "text": "Payment Terms"}], "explanation": "Company Name is the only required field on the vendor form. All other fields — contact name, email, phone, address, payment terms, and notes — are optional."}, {"id": "vn3", "text": "A vendor's scorecard shows a grade of 'C' with an overall score of 68/100. A 'C' grade with 68/100 indicates below-average performance across these areas."}, {"id": "vn4", "text": "You want to see all purchase orders placed with a specific vendor. Where do you find this?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Go to Purchase Orders and filter by vendor name"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Click the vendor row to open the detail dialog, then select the Purchase Orders tab", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "Run a report in the Reports module filtered by vendor"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Check the vendor's Notes field for PO references"}], "explanation": "The vendor detail dialog has three tabs: Info, Purchase Orders, and Scorecard. The Purchase Orders tab shows a data table with all POs linked to that vendor, including PO number, status, expected delivery date, and creation date."}, {"id": "vn5", "text": "Your company just connected QuickBooks Online as the accounting provider. What happens to vendor management in Forge?", "options": [{"id": "a", "text": "Nothing changes — vendors are managed independently in both systems"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Vendor management becomes read-only in Forge — vendor records sync from QuickBooks, and edits must be made in the accounting system", "isCorrect": true}, {"id": "c", "text": "All vendor records are deleted and replaced with QuickBooks data"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Vendors can only be created in Forge and push to QuickBooks"}], "explanation": "When an accounting provider is connected, vendor management becomes read-only in Forge. Vendor records sync from QuickBooks automatically. The vendor list, PO history, and scorecard remain fully functional — only direct editing is disabled."}, {"id": "vn6", "text": "You no longer do business with a supplier but want to preserve their purchase history. The vendor simply won't appear in active vendor lists or PO vendor dropdowns. These terms are inherited by new POs placed with the vendor."}, {"id": "vn8", "text": "You search for a vendor by typing in the search box but nothing happens. This reflects that reliable delivery is the most critical factor in vendor performance."}], "passingScore": 80, "shuffleOptions": true, "questionsPerQuiz": 8, "showExplanationsAfterSubmit": true}
